generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control (RBAC) such that a user's access to case records, suspect profiles, and property records is determined exclusively by their assigned role(s) and group membership(s), and no record outside a user's authorised scope shall appear in any search result, list view, or navigation path.
generated_requirements.rationale: Directly implements BR-AC-01 and the principle of least privilege to prevent unauthorised exposure of criminal justice records.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support three configurable unauthorised-access response modes for case records: (a) display title and metadata only; (b) display the existence of the case but not its title or metadata; (c) display nothing and give no indication that the case exists. The active mode shall be configurable at deployment time by a super-user. Mode (c) shall be the mandatory, non-overridable default for all cases classified at national security level.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements BR-AC-02 including the mandatory national-security override, preventing even existence disclosure of classified cases to unauthorised users.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall permit a user to belong to multiple user groups simultaneously, with access rights accumulated cumulatively across all group memberships. The cumulative access rights of any user shall not exceed that user's individual security clearance ceiling as defined by the super-user.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements BR-AC-03 while bounding cumulative rights to the user's clearance ceiling to enforce least privilege.

generated_requirements.text: Only users with the Station Administrator role shall be permitted to create, modify, deactivate, or delete user profiles and assign or remove users from groups. Station Administrators shall not have the ability to modify security attributes, access rights, security levels, privileges, or password policies.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements BR-AC-04 and BR-AC-05, separating administrative user management from security attribute control.

generated_requirements.text: Only users with the Super-User role shall be permitted to create, modify, or delete security attributes for any user or group, including access rights, security levels, privileges, and password policies. No other role — including Station Administrator — shall have access to security attribute management functions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements BR-AC-05, ensuring the highest-privilege security configuration functions are restricted to a single, audited role.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow individual users or case owners to stipulate which other named users or groups may access a specific case, subject to the stipulating user's own access level. A user shall not be able to grant access to a case at a level exceeding their own authorised access level.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements BR-AC-06, enabling case-level access delegation while preventing privilege escalation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with the Common User Access and Authentication Service to provide Single Sign-On (SSO) for all users. The SSO integration shall conform to SAML 2.0 or OAuth 2.0 / OpenID Connect standards. Users shall not be required to maintain separate credentials for the CCTNS system.
generated_requirements.rationale: Addresses the SSO requirement and federated identity standard constraint to eliminate multiple-credential management and ensure interoperability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce step-up multi-factor authentication (MFA) — requiring a second authentication factor beyond SSO — before permitting any of the following elevated operations: accessing a national-security-classified case record, exporting audit trail data, modifying security attributes, and modifying password policies.
generated_requirements.rationale: Operationalises the BRD's multi-tier authentication requirement by enumerating the specific high-risk operations that trigger step-up authentication.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically lock the active session and obscure all displayed case data after a configurable period of user inactivity (default: 10 minutes). Re-authentication via the SSO service or MFA (where applicable) shall be required to resume the session. A locked session shall not display any case record data on screen.
generated_requirements.rationale: Addresses the shared-terminal risk in station environments by preventing unauthorised access to an unattended authenticated session.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically and continuously record an audit trail entry for every create, read, update, and soft-delete operation performed on any case record, suspect profile, property record, or administrative parameter. The audit trail shall operate without manual activation and shall not be deactivatable by any user, including super-users.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements BR-AT-01 and BR-AT-02, ensuring 100% automated audit coverage of all critical entity actions.

generated_requirements.text: Each audit trail entry shall record, at minimum: the action type (create, read, update, delete); the entity type and unique identifier affected; the username and user role of the initiating user; the workstation identifier; the network address (IP) of the client; and a trusted system-generated timestamp sourced from a designated trusted time authority.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements BR-AT-02 with the additional fields required for legal admissibility and the reporting dimensions specified in the BRD.

generated_requirements.text: The audit trail shall be implemented using a write-once, append-only storage model with cryptographic hash-chaining of sequential log entries, such that any modification, deletion, or reordering of audit records — including by a database administrator — is detectable. No user or system process shall be able to alter or delete an existing audit trail record.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements BR-AT-03 and the tamper-evident audit log architecture pattern required for legal admissibility of electronic evidence.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall record every unauthorised access attempt — defined as any instance where a user requests access to a case record for which they are denied access — as a distinct audit trail entry, including the requesting user, the target case identifier (where known), the timestamp, and the workstation and network address.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements BR-AT-05, ensuring 100% logging of access violations as required by the KPI table.

generated_requirements.text: Audit trail records shall be retained for at least the full operational life of the case to which they relate. The system shall enforce this retention policy automatically and shall prevent any process from purging audit records for active or legally retained cases.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements BR-AT-04 and RC-05, satisfying legal admissibility and records management obligations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an audit trail export function that allows authorised users (Internal Auditor, External Auditor, Super-User) to export the complete audit trail for a specified case or time period. The export shall be produced as a digitally signed, human-readable document (PDF) and a structured machine-readable format (XML or JSON) with an embedded cryptographic hash to enable offline integrity verification. The export operation shall not modify the stored audit trail in any way.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements BR-AT-06 with specific format and integrity requirements to support external auditors with little system familiarity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide audit trail reports filterable and sortable by: case identifier, user, chronological date/time range, workstation identifier, and network address. These reports shall be accessible to Internal Auditors and Super-Users from within the application.
generated_requirements.rationale: Addresses the BRD's audit reporting dimensions and supports compliance officer oversight functions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorised users to create, retrieve, update, and soft-delete case records, suspect profiles, and property records. All such operations shall be available from any police station through the centralised browser-based interface.
generated_requirements.rationale: Establishes the core case management CRUD lifecycle across all stations as the primary functional scope.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement all record deletions as soft deletes: a deleted record shall be flagged with a deletion marker and the deleting user's identity and timestamp, but shall remain physically present in the database and fully retrievable by authorised personnel for the full statutory retention period. Hard deletion of any case, suspect, or property record shall not be permitted by any user or system process.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements BR-DI-01 and the data retention obligation, ensuring records remain available for legal and audit purposes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a simple (single-criterion) search function and an advanced (multi-criteria) search function across case records, suspect profiles, and property records. Search results shall be filtered in real time by the requesting user's access rights, such that no record outside the user's authorised scope is returned or displayed in any result list.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements BR-SR-01 and the search scope requirement, ensuring access control is enforced at the search result layer.

generated_requirements.text: Search result lists shall display only the minimum metadata fields required for record identification (e.g., case reference number, case type, date created, station). For cases subject to access restriction mode (b) or (c) per BR-AC-02, no title, suspect name, or sensitive metadata shall appear in the result list, even as a partially redacted value.
generated_requirements.rationale: Addresses the data minimisation in search results pattern, preventing indirect disclosure of restricted case data through result list metadata.

generated_requirements.text: Search results shall be displayed in paginated batches of 10 or 20 records per page, with the batch size configurable by the user or administrator. Full record details shall only be retrieved and displayed when the user explicitly selects a specific record from the result list.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements BR-SR-02 and supports the performance optimisation strategy by deferring full record retrieval.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an offline operation mode for critical functions — including case record creation, update, and retrieval of locally cached records — that remains fully functional when network connectivity is unavailable. Access control rules and audit trail recording shall continue to be enforced in offline mode, with audit entries queued locally and synchronised to the central audit store upon reconnection.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements OC-02 and BR-DI-02 while ensuring that offline operation does not create gaps in audit trail completeness or access control enforcement.

generated_requirements.text: Upon restoration of network connectivity after an offline period, the system shall detect and present to the user any conflicts between locally created or modified records and records modified centrally during the outage. The system shall apply a defined conflict resolution policy (last-write-wins or manual merge, as configured), record the conflict event and its resolution in the audit trail, and ensure no data is silently overwritten.
generated_requirements.rationale: Addresses the offline synchronisation conflict resolution pattern to prevent data loss and maintain audit trail continuity across the sync boundary.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an integrated support portal, accessible both from within the application and via a standalone URL without requiring application login, through which any user may log a defect report or enhancement request, assign it a category and description, and track its status through the full resolution lifecycle.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements BR-SD-01 and the requirement for external portal access, enabling users to report issues even when the main application is unavailable.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide help-desk users with management reports on submitted defect and enhancement requests, filterable and sortable by: category, current status, and age (days since submission). These reports shall be accessible from within the support portal.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements BR-SD-02, enabling help-desk operators to manage workload and identify ageing or high-volume issue categories.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide context-sensitive help on every user interface screen, accessible without navigating away from the current screen. Context-sensitive help coverage shall be 100% of all application screens.
generated_requirements.rationale: Addresses the KPI target of 100% context-sensitive help coverage and reduces training burden for officers.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support opt-in alert notifications delivered via email and/or SMS. A user who has opted in to alerts for a tracked case or entity shall receive a notification for every action taken on that case or entity. Notification delivery shall not be dependent on the user being logged into the application.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements BR-NT-01, enabling officers to monitor case activity without continuous system access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a multilingual user interface. All UI labels, messages, help text, and navigation elements shall be externally configurable for translation without code changes. The languages to be supported shall be specified during requirements elaboration; the primary national language(s) of the jurisdiction shall be supported as a minimum.
generated_requirements.rationale: Addresses the multilingual interface scope requirement and AS-06, ensuring the system is accessible to officers across linguistic regions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a controlled public-access interface exposing only data and functionality explicitly designated for public consumption by authorised police leadership. Public users shall have the most restricted access level and shall have no ability to access, infer, or navigate to any non-public record or function.
generated_requirements.rationale: Addresses the public access subset scope requirement and stakeholder 3.1.9, ensuring public exposure is strictly bounded.

generated_requirements.text: The system architecture shall be designed to support future extension of case management and search functions to PDA and mobile data terminal clients without requiring changes to the core business logic or data access layers. The API layer shall expose services in a device-agnostic manner consistent with the SOA design.
generated_requirements.rationale: Addresses the mobile/PDA extensibility scope requirement and business objective 7, ensuring the architecture does not foreclose future mobile delivery.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall return simple (single-criterion) search results within 5 to 8 seconds and advanced (multi-criteria) search results within 10 to 15 seconds, measured from the moment the user submits the search query to the moment the first paginated result page is rendered in the browser, under normal operating load.
generated_requirements.rationale: Directly implements the search performance KPIs from the BRD success criteria table.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall retrieve and display a case record that has been accessed within the previous 2 months (active cache) within 5 to 8 seconds, and a case record not accessed within the previous 2 months (archived) within 20 seconds, measured from user selection to full record display in the browser.
generated_requirements.rationale: Directly implements the case retrieval performance KPIs for active and archived records from the BRD success criteria table.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall employ the following performance optimisation techniques: hierarchical caching of frequently accessed data, AJAX-based UI interactions to avoid full page reloads, asynchronous HTTP requests, static content served from the web server tier, database indexing on all key search columns, and paginated result delivery. AJAX polling intervals shall be configurable to reduce traffic on constrained network links.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements TC-08 and the bandwidth-adaptive content delivery pattern to meet performance targets on low-bandwidth connections.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall operate satisfactorily on low-bandwidth network connections typical of rural or remote police stations. Individual page payloads (excluding user-uploaded attachments) shall not exceed a defined maximum size (to be agreed during technical design, recommended ≤ 150 KB compressed). Non-critical UI elements (e.g., decorative images, non-essential animations) shall degrade gracefully on constrained links without affecting core functionality.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements OC-01 and the bandwidth-adaptive content delivery pattern with concrete payload constraints.

generated_requirements.text: Planned system downtime per rolling 3-month period, unplanned downtime per rolling 3-month period, and the number of unplanned downtime incidents per rolling 3-month period shall each not exceed thresholds to be defined and agreed in the Service Level Agreement (SLA) at contract negotiation. [TBD — requires stakeholder resolution before acceptance testing can be defined.]
generated_requirements.rationale: Captures the availability KPIs from the BRD while flagging them as TBD pending SLA negotiation, as required by OC-04.

generated_requirements.text: Following a hardware or software failure, the system shall be fully restored to operational status within a maximum restoration time to be defined and agreed in the SLA at contract negotiation. [TBD — requires stakeholder resolution before acceptance testing can be defined.] The system shall be designed to prevent operational data loss in the event of equipment or network communication failure.
generated_requirements.rationale: Captures the disaster recovery KPI and BR-DI-02 while flagging the restoration time target as TBD pending SLA negotiation.

generated_requirements.text: All data transmitted between client browsers and the system, and between system components, shall be encrypted using HTTPS (HTTP over TLS). The minimum acceptable TLS version shall be TLS 1.2; TLS 1.0, TLS 1.1, SSL 3.0, and earlier protocol versions shall be explicitly disabled. All inter-component communications shall use 2-way digital signatures conforming to national government PKI standards.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements TC-03 and the cryptographic protocol constraint, explicitly prohibiting deprecated protocol versions as required by the domain expert advisory.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support SSL-encrypted connections and secure VPN connections for remote access by officers at stations connecting over public or untrusted networks.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements TC-04, ensuring remote access channels are protected against interception.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement selective encryption of sensitive data fields at rest (e.g., national security case content, biometric data, personal identifiers) using a recognised encryption standard (AES-256 minimum). Encryption key management shall follow ISO/IEC 27001 principles.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements TC-09 and the ISO/IEC 27001 constraint for data-at-rest protection of sensitive criminal justice records.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate all user-supplied input on both the client side (browser) and the server side before processing. Server-side validation shall be the authoritative check. All database interactions shall use parameterised queries or prepared statements — dynamic SQL construction from user input is prohibited. All output rendered to the browser shall be encoded to prevent cross-site scripting (XSS). The system shall address as a minimum all OWASP Top 10 vulnerability categories.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements BR-DI-03 and the OWASP Top 10 baseline constraint, providing comprehensive input/output security for a system holding criminal justice data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be implemented using a Service Oriented Architecture (SOA) with a modular design, deployed in a 3-tier data centre architecture (presentation, application, data tiers), with strict separation of presentation logic, business logic, and data access logic across n-tier layers. No business logic shall reside in the presentation layer; no data access logic shall reside in the business logic layer.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements TC-02 and TC-06, ensuring the architecture supports independent maintenance, testing, and extension of each layer.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be browser-based and shall function correctly on all major modern browsers (including Chrome, Firefox, Edge, and Safari) without browser-specific customisation or plugins. No software installation beyond a standard browser shall be required on client devices at police stations.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements TC-01 and TC-07, ensuring the system is deployable across the heterogeneous device estate of police stations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall scale to support police stations of all sizes — from small rural stations with a handful of concurrent users to large urban headquarters with hundreds of concurrent users — without requiring architectural changes, redeployment, or manual tuning. Scalability shall be achievable through configuration or infrastructure scaling alone.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements OC-03 and business objective 7, ensuring the platform accommodates the full range of station sizes.

generated_requirements.text: The user interface shall comply with ISO 9241 (Ergonomics of Human-System Interaction), specifically: ISO 9241-20 (ICT accessibility), ISO 9241-110 (self-descriptive navigation), ISO 9241-171 (software accessibility), ISO 9241-303 (screen text legibility), and ISO 9241-12, 14, 15, 16, 17 (information presentation and interaction). Content accessibility shall comply with WCAG 1.0. Multimedia presentations shall follow ISO 14915-1 to ISO 14915-3. UI design shall follow the guidelines at www.usability.gov.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements RC-01 through RC-04, ensuring the interface meets all mandated accessibility and usability standards.

generated_requirements.text: The user interface shall be consistent in layout, terminology, and interaction patterns across all police stations and all screens. Users shall be able to customise their interface layout and set persistent defaults (e.g., preferred search filters, default station) that are retained across sessions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Addresses the standardised UI and customisable layout requirements from the BRD future state, reducing training burden and data entry effort.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall adopt standardised data formats and common metadata elements across all modules and stations to facilitate interoperability with other government and law enforcement systems. All service interfaces shall be built on open standards (e.g., REST or SOAP with WSDL) with no proprietary protocols.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements TC-10 and the open standards constraint, ensuring the system can be integrated with and extended by other government systems.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support multiple types of communication services for remote access (e.g., broadband, GPRS, satellite, dial-up) to accommodate the varied connectivity environments of police stations. The system shall detect available bandwidth and adapt content delivery accordingly.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements TC-05 and OC-01, ensuring the system remains accessible across the full range of connectivity environments present at police stations.

generated_requirements.text: The audit trail module shall satisfy the legal admissibility standard for electronic evidence by: (a) using a trusted timestamp authority for all log entry timestamps; (b) implementing cryptographic hash-chaining so that any gap, deletion, or modification in the log sequence is detectable; (c) storing audit data in a write-once, append-only store that is logically and physically separate from operational data; and (d) producing exports that include the hash chain and a digital signature verifiable offline.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements RC-06 and the tamper-evident audit log architecture pattern, satisfying the legal admissibility chain-of-custody requirement for digital evidence.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall capture 100% of CRUD actions on critical entities (cases, suspects, properties, administrative parameters) and 100% of unauthorised access attempts in the audit trail automatically, with no manual intervention required. This coverage shall be verifiable through automated audit completeness checks.
generated_requirements.rationale: Directly implements the 100% audit trail coverage and 100% access attempt logging KPIs from the BRD success criteria table.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support multiple types of communication services and remote access methods. All remote access connections shall be secured via SSL/TLS or VPN. The system shall support at least two independent communication paths for remote access to ensure continuity of connectivity in the event of a single path failure.
generated_requirements.rationale: Addresses the connectivity resilience requirement for remote stations, reducing the risk of complete connectivity loss due to a single network path failure.

sme_subject: law enforcement information systems architect with expertise in criminal justice data standards, RBAC policy design, and legal admissibility of digital evidence
sme_advisory: CCTNS is a high-stakes, multi-jurisdictional law enforcement case management platform where failures in access control, audit integrity, or data availability can have direct legal consequences — including evidence inadmissibility, civil liability, and officer safety risks. The requirements engineer must pay particular attention to the legal admissibility chain of custody for digital records, the nuanced layering of RBAC with case-level and national-security-level overrides, and the offline/low-bandwidth resilience model which must not compromise audit trail completeness or access control enforcement. Every quality attribute — security, auditability, performance, usability, and scalability — intersects with legal or operational risk in this domain, and requirements must be precise enough to be independently testable and traceable to specific business rules and compliance obligations.

ground_truth_requirements.text: Avoiding opening unnecessary windows: Additional windows such as new browser windows or pop-up windows should only be opened if this supports the user’s task. Opening new windows can distract, confuse or impede users for a variety of reasons. They can superimpose the primary window, hiding relevant information. They could make it cognitively more difficult to understand the navigation structure with negative effects on both usability and accessibility. They also require additional user actions for closing unwanted windows. ground_truth_requirements.text: Designing for input device independence: User interfaces should be designed to allow activation of controls by a variety of input devices. In general, device independence can be achieved if the functionality is operable via a keyboard. ground_truth_requirements.text: Making user interfaces robust: User interfaces should be designed to be as robust as possible in the face of changing technology. ground_truth_requirements.text: Acceptable opening / download times: Application pages should be designed and implemented so that there are acceptable opening times and download times for the expected range of technical contexts of use (e.g. bandwidth between the application and the user). ground_truth_requirements.text: Providing clear error messages: The content of error messages shown on the pages or special error pages should clearly state the reason why the error occurred and, if possible, actions the user can take to resolve the error. ground_truth_requirements.text: Writing style: The reading and understanding of the textual content on the screen should be supported by suitable means, including the use of short sentences, the division of the text into shorter chunks or the presentation of content items in the form of bullet points. ground_truth_requirements.text: Using familiar terminology for navigation links: Navigation links — particularly links representing the main navigation structure — should be labelled with terms that are familiar to the user, based on his/her general knowledge, prior experience in the application domain or experience of using other systems. ground_truth_requirements.text: Using descriptive link labels: The target or purpose of a link should be directly indicated by its label, avoiding generic labels such as “go” or “click here” except where the purpose of the link is clear from its context on the page or the labels have commonly understood semantics in the particular application domain. Using appropriate terminology specific to the user’s tasks and information needs is important for making the content easy to understand. ground_truth_requirements.text: Use of “white space”: “White space” on a page i.e. space filled only with the background color should be used in such a way that it does not impair the visual skimming of the page. ground_truth_requirements.text: Selecting appropriate page lengths The length of a page should be selected so as to support the primary purpose and use of the page. Longer pages can be more appropriate when users want to read the content without interruptions or when the page needs to match a paper counterpart. ground_truth_requirements.text: Consistent page layout: Pages should be designed using consistent layout schemes, supporting the user in finding similar information at the same position on different pages. Overall layout schemes apply to all pages and are preferable when all pages have a similar structure. Frequently, however, different pages have different purposes and types of content. In such cases, pages can usually be grouped in different categories, using one layout scheme for each category consistently. ground_truth_requirements.text: Placing title information consistently: Page titles should be placed in a consistent location on the different pages. Placing title information consistently: Page titles should be placed in a consistent location on the different pages.

ground_truth_requirements.text: Observing principles of human perception When designing application pages, the general principles of human perception should be taken into account. The International Standards mentioned below shall be consulted for guidance. Practical guidelines for presenting information to the user are to be found in ISO 9241-12. Guidance on selecting and using different forms of interaction techniques is to be found in ISO 9241-14 to ISO 9241-17. ISO 9241-14 gives guidance about menus, ISO 9241-15 about command dialogues, ISO 9241-16 about direct manipulation and ISO 9241-17 about forms. In addition, when designing multimedia information presentations, the design principles and recommendations described in ISO 14915-1 to ISO 14915-3 should be taken into account. Appropriate content presentation also plays a key role in accessibility. ground_truth_requirements.text: Individualization and user adaptation : Adapting the content and the navigation of a user interface to individual users or user groups can be a useful mechanism for providing information that is of interest to the users and for making access to relevant information more efficient. Different approaches can be used for achieving these goals, like providing users with means for customizing the user interface to their personal needs i.e. individualization designing content and navigation differently for varying user groups or roles i.e. such as employees of different levels, citizens etc, monitoring the user’s behaviour and adapting to the user’s goals that are inferred from the behaviour observed, recommending information that is potentially more relevant or interesting to the specific user, based on the behaviour of all users or a user group. generated_requirements.text: The system shall become fully operational within 60 seconds of the Yoggie USB device being inserted into a compatible Intel-based host system, requiring no manual configuration steps.
generated_requirements.rationale: BO-1 and the KPI table mandate sub-60-second deployment as the primary differentiator from existing solutions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 1,000 simultaneous logged-in customer sessions without measurable degradation in response time or transaction integrity.
generated_requirements.rationale: BO-2 and the KPI table set 1,000 concurrent users as the scalability target to address the pain point of low-cost shared hosting limitations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall return product search results within 1 second under full concurrent load of 1,000 users.
generated_requirements.rationale: The KPI table and business pain points identify sub-5-second search latency as a critical improvement, with a 1-second target specified.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete add-to-cart operations within 2 milliseconds as measured at the server side.
generated_requirements.rationale: The KPI table explicitly specifies a 2-millisecond add-to-cart latency target to ensure a responsive shopping experience.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall retrieve and render a minimum of 200 product records per second during catalogue browsing operations.
generated_requirements.rationale: The KPI table specifies a product retrieval throughput of at least 200 products per second.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete credit card validation against the third-party payment gateway and return a result to the customer within 2 seconds.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.2 and the KPI table mandate 2-second credit card validation to prevent customer abandonment.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall acquire and present shipping charges to the customer within 2 seconds of initiating a shipping rate query.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.3 and the KPI table specify a 2-second shipping charge acquisition time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall dispatch order confirmation and vendor notification emails within 1 second of an order event being triggered.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.3 and the KPI table specify a 1-second email dispatch time for both customer and vendor notifications.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restore database records at a throughput of at least 1,000 records per second during a recovery operation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.6 and the KPI table mandate 1,000 records/second restore throughput to minimize recovery time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve a minimum availability of 99.99% (no more than approximately 52 minutes of unplanned downtime per calendar year), and the architecture shall document how this SLA is addressed given the single-point-of-failure USB hardware constraint.
generated_requirements.rationale: The KPI table mandates 99.99% uptime; the domain expert advisory flags the inherent conflict with the single-device USB architecture that must be formally resolved.

generated_requirements.text: All sensitive data transmitted between the customer browser and the web store server shall be encrypted using TLS 1.2 at minimum, with TLS 1.3 preferred; prohibited cipher suites shall include RC4, DES, 3DES, and all export-grade ciphers.
generated_requirements.rationale: PCI-DSS v4.0 Requirement 4.2.1 mandates trusted TLS for cardholder data transmission, and the domain expert advisory specifies minimum TLS 1.2 with explicit cipher suite restrictions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall formally document and record as an accepted risk the incompatibility between the PCI-DSS Requirement 4.2.1 TLS 1.2 minimum and the browser compatibility targets of Internet Explorer 6 and Netscape Communicator 4/5, which do not support TLS 1.2; customers using those browsers shall be warned that secure checkout is unavailable.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert advisory identifies a direct conflict between the TLS requirement and the legacy browser targets that must be formally resolved rather than silently ignored.

generated_requirements.text: All customer personally identifiable information (PII) and cardholder data stored in the MySQL database shall be encrypted at rest using AES-256 or equivalent strong cryptography, with explicit key management procedures documented and implemented.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.5, PCI-DSS Requirement 3, and GDPR data protection obligations mandate encryption at rest with documented key management.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with a PCI-DSS-compliant payment gateway that supports tokenization or point-to-point encryption (P2PE) such that raw Primary Account Numbers (PANs) never traverse or reside on the USB device itself.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert advisory recommends P2PE/tokenization integration to dramatically reduce PCI-DSS scope on the portable USB device.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall never store CVV, CVC, or CAV card verification codes after transaction authorization under any circumstances, including in logs, temporary files, or database fields.
generated_requirements.rationale: PCI-DSS v4.0 Requirement 3.3.1 is an absolute prohibition on post-authorization storage of sensitive authentication data with no architectural workaround.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall never store PANs in plaintext; any stored PAN shall be rendered unreadable via strong cryptography (AES-256 minimum) and the system shall document which PAN storage fields exist, their encryption method, and the associated key management lifecycle.
generated_requirements.rationale: PCI-DSS Requirement 3 mandates PAN minimization and unreadability; the domain expert advisory specifies AES-256 and explicit key management documentation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall define and document the Cardholder Data Environment (CDE) boundary on the USB device, explicitly identifying which processes, storage partitions, and network interfaces are in-scope for PCI-DSS, and shall implement controls to prevent out-of-scope components from accessing CDE data.
generated_requirements.rationale: PCI-DSS v4.0 Requirement 9.4 and the domain expert advisory require explicit CDE scoping because the entire device is physically portable and constitutes portable storage of cardholder data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all vendor and system administrator access to the web store management interface and any component within the Cardholder Data Environment.
generated_requirements.rationale: PCI-DSS v4.0 Requirement 8 mandates MFA for all personnel with access to the CDE, going beyond the BRD's password-only brute-force lockout.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect consecutive failed login attempts and automatically lock the affected account after a configurable threshold (default: 6 attempts), alerting the system administrator and requiring administrator or verified-email unlock.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.1 mandates brute-force detection and protective action; PCI-DSS Requirement 8 requires account lockout after failed attempts.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce an inactivity session timeout for all authenticated users (customers, vendors, and administrators), requiring re-authentication after a configurable idle period (default: 15 minutes for administrative sessions).
generated_requirements.rationale: PCI-DSS Requirement 8 and the domain expert advisory specify session timeout and re-authentication policies for CDE access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically detect and block IP addresses conducting Denial of Service (DoS) attacks without requiring manual administrator intervention, and shall log each blocking event with timestamp, source IP, and detection criteria.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.5 mandates automated DoS detection and blocking as a core security control.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall operate behind the Firestarter open-source firewall at all times; no alternative firewall product may be substituted, and firewall compatibility with Slackware Linux 2.6 on the XScale PXA270 architecture must be verified and documented before system acceptance.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.5 and Section 7.4 mandate Firestarter as the sole permitted firewall; the domain expert advisory flags compatibility verification as a dependency risk.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically record a tamper-evident audit log entry for every change, update, patch, fix, login event, and administrative action; log entries shall be cryptographically chained or written to a write-once partition to prevent unauthorized modification or deletion.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.6 mandates automatic audit logging; PCI-DSS v4.0 Requirement 10.3 requires logs to be protected from destruction and unauthorized modification.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall ship audit logs off the USB device to a remote log storage destination at configurable intervals, with a defined on-device log retention period and rotation policy that prevents storage exhaustion on the 4 GB flash device.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert advisory identifies flash storage exhaustion from unbounded logging as a direct threat to the 99.99% availability SLA and PCI-DSS Requirement 10.3 compliance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall constrain high-frequency write operations — including session state persistence, database journaling, and log writes — to use flash-wear-levelling-aware storage strategies (e.g., tmpfs for ephemeral data, log buffering) to avoid premature NAND flash wear-out on the 4 GB storage device.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert advisory identifies finite NAND flash write-cycle limits as a direct threat to long-term availability on the Yoggie hardware.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall perform automated periodic backups of all application data, configuration, and database content over a live internet connection; the backup schedule, retention policy, and off-device destination shall be defined and documented during requirements elicitation and configurable by the system administrator.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.6 mandates automated periodic backups; the schedule and retention policy are explicitly flagged as TBD during elicitation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall gracefully invalidate all active customer sessions, complete or roll back in-flight transactions, and preserve database integrity when the USB device is physically removed from the host system mid-operation.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert advisory requires defined behavior for mid-session USB removal to protect data integrity and prevent partial transaction corruption.

generated_requirements.text: The USB device shall be classified as portable storage of cardholder data; the system documentation shall specify physical access controls, a device inventory register, an authorized personnel list, and tamper-evidence or device authentication mechanisms required by PCI-DSS v4.0 Requirement 9.4.
generated_requirements.rationale: PCI-DSS v4.0 Requirement 9.4 mandates physical security controls for portable media containing cardholder data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall define and enforce a customer PII data retention limit; upon expiry or upon a verified customer erasure request, all associated PII shall be securely deleted from the device and all backup copies, with a documented breach notification workflow in the event of device loss or theft.
generated_requirements.rationale: GDPR right-to-erasure and data minimization obligations apply to PII on a physically portable device that can be lost or stolen.

generated_requirements.text: The backup-to-USB-copy mechanism shall encrypt all copied data using AES-256 and shall require authenticated authorization before a copy operation is initiated, to prevent uncontrolled creation of unencrypted PII copies.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert advisory flags that the USB-copy backup mechanism must not create uncontrolled copies of PII, consistent with GDPR data minimization.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall define a patch management process specifying maximum remediation windows: critical vulnerabilities (CVSS ≥ 9.0) patched within 30 days, high vulnerabilities (CVSS 7.0–8.9) within 60 days, and medium/low vulnerabilities within 90 days; the process shall address how OS-level CVEs in the frozen Yoggie Slackware Linux 2.6 baseline are escalated to Yoggie Corporation for remediation.
generated_requirements.rationale: PCI-DSS v4.0 Requirement 6.3.3 mandates a defined patch cadence; the frozen OS baseline creates a compliance risk requiring a formal escalation path to Yoggie.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support remote application of patches, fixes, and updates over an internet connection without requiring physical access to the USB device beyond the initial insertion; all applied patches shall be automatically recorded in the audit log.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.7 and BO-6 mandate remote maintainability; Business Rule 5.6 requires automatic logging of all changes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support interchangeable plugins that extend or modify functionality without replacing the core application; plugin installation and removal shall be performable remotely and shall be recorded in the audit log.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.8 mandates a plugin/extensibility architecture to support incremental upgrades.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a customer-facing web storefront supporting product browsing, keyword-based product search, shopping cart management, checkout with credit card payment, shipping charge display, and order confirmation, accessible via web browser.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6 (In Scope) defines the complete customer-facing feature set as the core deliverable of the platform.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall require customers to register an account with an email address that is validated for existence before the account is activated; unvalidated email addresses shall not be permitted to complete a purchase.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.1 mandates email validation as a prerequisite for purchase completion.

generated_requirements.text: The product search function shall implement a search algorithm appropriate to keyword and catalogue matching; the mandated use of Dijkstra's shortest-path algorithm shall be formally validated with stakeholders during requirements elicitation, as it is a graph-traversal algorithm not conventionally applicable to product catalogue search, and an alternative algorithm shall be specified if validation fails.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.4 mandates Dijkstra's algorithm, but the domain expert advisory and elicitation strategy flag this as technically questionable and requiring stakeholder validation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall store all inventory and product data in a MySQL relational database; no NoSQL or non-SQL-compliant data store shall be used for inventory data.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.4 and Section 7.2 mandate MySQL as the exclusive database platform for inventory data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically compress product images that exceed a defined file size threshold before storage or display; the specific threshold value shall be determined and documented during requirements elicitation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.4 mandates automatic image compression above a threshold; the threshold is explicitly flagged as TBD during elicitation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate all credit card transactions against a fraud detection service before processing; any transaction that fails fraud validation shall be rejected and the customer notified with a clear error message; no fraudulent transaction shall be processed.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.2 mandates fraud validation as a hard gate on all credit card transactions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall re-validate the payment token or card reference (but never the raw PAN or CVV) associated with a returning customer's stored payment method upon each login, to confirm the card has not been flagged for fraud; this re-validation shall be reconciled with the PCI-DSS prohibition on storing CVV/CVC post-authorization.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.1 requires re-validation of stored cards on login; the domain expert advisory requires reconciliation with PCI-DSS Requirement 3.3.1's CVV storage prohibition.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a vendor management interface enabling the vendor to manage product inventory, upload and manage product images, view and manage orders, and receive email notifications of new orders, without requiring technical IT expertise.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6 and BO-4 define vendor self-sufficiency and the vendor management feature set as in-scope deliverables.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a system administrator interface supporting system configuration, remote patch and update management, audit log review, and user account management.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6 defines administrator features as in-scope; Section 3.3 describes the administrator's operational needs.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a dedicated test mode in which test credit card transactions and test shipping order processing can be executed without initiating real financial transactions or live carrier queries; test mode shall be clearly distinguished from production mode in the UI.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rules 5.2 and 5.3 mandate test mode for credit card and shipping transactions to support safe testing.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall use asynchronous page loading techniques compatible with Internet Explorer 6/7 and Netscape Communicator 4/5 to improve perceived responsiveness; front-end design shall be constrained to the CSS and JavaScript capabilities supported by these browser versions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6 and Section 7.3 mandate compatibility with IE 6/7 and Netscape 4/5, which impose severe front-end technology constraints.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide contextual help bubbles on all major UI controls, inline error messages with actionable guidance for all error conditions, and an online help documentation system accessible from within the web interface, for both customer and vendor users.
generated_requirements.rationale: BO-4 and Section 4 (Future State) mandate vendor and customer self-sufficiency through intuitive UI with help bubbles and clear error messages.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall deliver an Operations and Maintenance Manual, a User Guide, and Administrator Installation Instructions as formal project deliverables, all completed and reviewed before system acceptance.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6 (In Scope) and Section 8.2 (D-7) identify documentation as a mandatory deliverable and dependency for usability and administrator self-sufficiency goals.

generated_requirements.text: The complete web store — including application binaries, MySQL database, configuration files, and all data — shall reside entirely on the Yoggie USB device and shall be migratable by copying the device contents to another USB drive using an authenticated, encrypted copy operation.
generated_requirements.rationale: BO-5 and Business Rule 5.7 define USB portability and migration as a core platform capability.

generated_requirements.text: The system software shall be designed and optimized to operate within the fixed hardware constraints of the Yoggie XScale PXA270 chipset: 520 MHz CPU, 128 MB SDRAM, and 4 GB Flash RAM; no assumption of additional hardware resources shall be made, and performance targets shall be validated against these constraints during testing.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 7.1 defines the hardware ceiling; the elicitation strategy flags the tension between aggressive KPIs and the severely constrained hardware as the highest technical risk.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall use the Apache web server exclusively; no alternative web server shall be used, and the required Apache version and module set shall be confirmed with Yoggie Corporation before the OS baseline freeze.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 7.2 mandates Apache as the sole permitted web server; dependency D-5 requires version confirmation before baseline freeze.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall define and document the behavior of all customer-facing and vendor-facing functions when internet connectivity is unavailable (e.g., credit card validation, shipping rate queries, backup, email dispatch), including whether operations are queued, blocked, or degraded, as this offline-mode gap is explicitly unaddressed in the current BRD.
generated_requirements.rationale: Assumption A-4 explicitly acknowledges that offline behavior is unaddressed; the elicitation strategy requires this gap to be formally resolved before requirements are baselined.

sme_subject: PCI-DSS payment security auditor with embedded Linux systems expertise
sme_advisory: This system sits at a uniquely challenging intersection of PCI-DSS payment compliance, embedded Linux resource constraints, and portable/removable media security. The requirements engineer must pay particular attention to the tension between the 99.99% availability SLA and the inherently single-point-of-failure USB hardware architecture, the strict PCI-DSS scoping implications of storing or transmitting cardholder data on a physically portable device, and the severe resource ceiling imposed by the XScale PXA270 chipset (520 MHz, 128 MB SDRAM) when targeting 1,000 concurrent users. Several business rules in the BRD contain technically questionable assumptions — notably the use of Dijkstra's algorithm for product search and the targeting of Netscape Communicator 4/5 alongside HTTPS/TLS — that the engineer should probe and validate with stakeholders before committing them to formal requirements.
