ground_truth_requirements.text: When the THEMAS system detects a request for an alarm, this process shall detect which of the two alarms are being requested. If the system detects an invalid temperature, this process shall output a continuous series of alternating 500 Hz and 700 Hz beeps on the supervisor’s computer. Each beep shall have a three - quarter second duration. This series of beeps shall continue until the supervisor manually resets the alarm through the supervisor’s interface window. If the system detects a temperature limit has been exceeded, this process shall output a continuous series of alternating 1000 Hz and 1500 Hz beeps on the supervisor’s computer. Each beep shall have a one-half second duration. This series of beeps shall continue until the supervisor manually resets the alarm through the supervisor’s interface window. Each time an alarm is requested, an alarm event shall be recorded. This event shall be used to provide operational and statistical reports about the system. When the THEMAS system detects a request for an alarm, this process shall detect which of the two alarms are being requested. If the system detects an invalid temperature, this process shall output a continuous series of alternating 500 Hz and 700 Hz beeps on the supervisor’s computer. Each beep shall have a three - quarter second duration. This series of beeps shall continue until the supervisor manually resets the alarm through the supervisor’s interface window. If the system detects a temperature limit has been exceeded, this process shall output a continuous series of alternating 1000 Hz and 1500 Hz beeps on the supervisor’s computer. Each beep shall have a one-half second duration. This series of beeps shall continue until the supervisor manually resets the alarm through the supervisor’s interface window. Each time an alarm is requested, an alarm event shall be recorded. This event shall be used to provide operational and statistical reports about the system.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: For each event that is generated, the THEMAS system shall identify each event and generate the appropriate event data. For each event that is generated, the THEMAS system shall identify each event and generate the appropriate event data.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: When an event occurs, the THEMAS system shall identify the event type and format an appropriate event message. The THEMAS system shall record each event by a description and the c urrent system time for that event. This information shall be recorded in a Microsoft® Access® database that shall reside on the supervisor’s computer. When an event occurs, the THEMAS system shall identify the event type and format an appropriate event message. The THEMAS system shall record each event by a description and the c urrent system time for that event. This information shall be recorded in a Microsoft® Access® database that shall reside on the supervisor’s computer.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The THEMAS system shall provide the supervisor a mechanism to change the temperature setting of any of the thermostats in the system. The THEMAS system shall provide the supervisor a mechanism to change the temperature setting of any of the thermostats in the system.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The supervisor’s interface shall display the available thermostats and their individual current temperature settings. The supervisor shall be able to select one of the thermostats and select a value for the current temperature from a list of valid temperatures. The supervisor’s interface shall display the available thermostats and their individual current temperature settings. The supervisor shall be able to select one of the thermostats and select a value for the current temperature from a list of valid temperatures.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The THEMAS shall provide the ability for the supervisor to select between two different types of reports: 1) the operational history of the THEMAS system for the past twelve months and 2) a statistical summary for any selected mont h. The THEMAS shall provide the ability for the supervisor to select between two different types of reports: 1) the operational history of the THEMAS system for the past twelve months and 2) a statistical summary for any selected mont h.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The supervisor’s interface to the THEMAS system shall provide a mechanism to select between an operational history report or a statistical summary report. Either report shall consist of an ASCII file whose location and name shall be selectable by the operator. If the operational history report is selected, the THEMAS system shall select all the events from the event database, sort the events by date and time, and create the ASCII report file. If the statistical report is selected, the THEMAS system shall present the operator with a list of available months from which to make a selection. After selecting one of the months, the system shall generate the ASCII report file. The statistical reports shall consist of the following statistics: The supervisor’s interface to the THEMAS system shall provide a mechanism to select between an operational history report or a statistical summary report. Either report shall consist of an ASCII file whose location and name shall be selectable by the operator. If the operational history report is selected, the THEMAS system shall select all the events from the event database, sort the events by date and time, and create the ASCII report file. If the statistical report is selected, the THEMAS system shall present the operator with a list of available months from which to make a selection. After selecting one of the months, the system shall generate the ASCII report file. The statistical reports shall consist of the following statistics: The percentage of time each heating and cooling unit was on for each day of that month. For each thermometer, the ratio of the number of requests for a heating and cooling unit that was granted to the number of requests for a heating or cooling unit that was denied.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a web-accessible XML Registry that serves as the single authoritative repository for DoD XML tags, schemas, Document Type Definitions (DTDs), and associated metadata.
generated_requirements.rationale: Establishes the core registry capability required to eliminate semantic fragmentation across the DoD enterprise.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce a four-stage Information Resource lifecycle workflow — Submit, Review, Approve/Reject, Publish — for every XML tag or information resource submitted to the registry. No resource shall be published without explicit approval by an authorized Registry Manager.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements the governed producer workflow mandated by BR-02 and Objective 4, ensuring no unapproved tags reach consumers.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce package completeness as a blocking gate at intake: a submission shall not enter the review workflow unless it contains all three required components — an XML Tag Specification form, an XML Specification artifact (DTD, DCD, or equivalent), and a sample XML document. Incomplete packages shall be rejected at intake with a descriptive rejection notice returned to the submitting producer.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements BR-01 and the domain expert guidance that package completeness must be enforced architecturally at intake, not deferred to the review stage.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall perform three sequenced, blocking validation steps on every submitted XML artifact prior to admitting it to the review workflow: (1) XML well-formedness checking, (2) DTD/schema validity checking against registry schema rules, and (3) DoD data standards naming convention conformance checking. A submission that fails any step shall be rejected with step-specific annotations; it shall not proceed to subsequent steps or enter the review queue.
generated_requirements.rationale: Ensures that only structurally sound, standards-conformant artifacts enter the governance workflow, consistent with domain expert guidance that validation must be a gate, not a report.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support both online (interactive web-based) and offline (batch file upload) submission modes for metadata producers, with no loss of data integrity or validation enforcement in either mode.
generated_requirements.rationale: Satisfies Objective 4 success criteria requiring producers to submit both online and offline without data integrity compromise.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall store and expose, for each registered XML tag, at minimum the following metadata attributes: data type, parent/child (uses/used-by) relationships, ancestor/descendant relationships, version history, domain value reference sets, and links to amplifying resources (e.g., ERwin models, DTDs).
generated_requirements.rationale: Directly implements Objective 2 success criteria to enable semantic interoperability without out-of-band communication.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide at least three distinct tag discovery modalities for consumers: (1) hierarchical/tree-view browsing, (2) tabular/list view, and (3) search by author or subscriber. Additional search dimensions shall include tag name, data type, and relationship (parent/child, ancestor/descendant).
generated_requirements.rationale: Satisfies Objective 1 success criteria requiring at minimum three search modalities to support enterprise-wide tag discovery.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow consumers to download complete Information Resource Submittal Packages — including the XML Tag Specification, XML Specification artifact, and sample XML document — for any approved, published information resource.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enables consumers to obtain all artifacts needed to correctly interpret and reuse a registered tag without out-of-band requests.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide subscription services allowing consumers to subscribe to change notifications at the Community of Interest (COI) level and at the individual information resource level. Consumers shall receive automated notifications within one business cycle of an approved change to any subscribed resource.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements Objective 4 success criteria and the change notification SLA pattern identified by the domain expert.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall queue change notifications and retry delivery when the underlying email or FTP delivery infrastructure is unavailable. The system shall alert Registry Managers when notification delivery has been pending beyond a configurable threshold. The notification payload shall include: resource identifier, change type, change description, effective date, and a link to the updated resource.
generated_requirements.rationale: Addresses the domain expert's requirement to specify notification delivery behavior under infrastructure unavailability and define the notification payload content.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate an immutable, timestamped audit record attributable to an authenticated user for every registry lifecycle transition, including: Submit, Review-Started, Approve, Reject, Publish, Version-Created, and Retire. Audit records shall not be modifiable or deletable by any user, including Registry Managers.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements the domain expert's audit trail completeness pattern and supports formal qualification inspection evidence as required by COE certification.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support versioning of registered information resources. When a producer modifies an approved, published resource, the modification shall create a new version, re-enter the approval workflow, and trigger change notifications to all subscribers. Prior versions shall remain accessible in the registry with their version history intact.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements BR-05 and ensures consumers can track the evolution of tags they depend upon.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall require producers to post planned changes to a registered information resource in the registry in advance of implementation, providing consumers advance notice before the change takes effect.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements BR-06 to protect consumers from unannounced breaking changes to tags they are using.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce DoD data standards naming conventions on all submitted XML tag names and metadata identifiers as a blocking validation step within the submission workflow. Naming convention enforcement logic shall be implemented in the system and shall not rely solely on submitter adherence to documentation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements BR-03 and the domain expert constraint that naming convention enforcement must be a system behavior, not just a style guide.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a style guide wizard tool that guides producers in applying XML element/attribute design rules, specifically: terms that are well-recognized and meaningful outside their container element shall be designated as XML elements rather than attributes, and XML attributes shall be used sparingly.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements BR-04 and A-08, providing tooling to enforce element/attribute design guidance during the submission process.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support federation capability whereby registry requests that fall outside the scope of the local registry are forwarded to peer registries. The system shall enforce, as a technical gate, that a pre-established federation agreement exists for the target peer registry before forwarding any request. No ad hoc forwarding to registries without a recorded agreement shall be permitted.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements BR-18 and the domain expert's requirement that federation agreement enforcement be a testable system behavior, not merely an organizational policy.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide Registry Manager administrative functions including: creating and managing user accounts, assigning and revoking superuser designations, establishing and enforcing naming conventions, reviewing and approving or rejecting submissions with annotations, managing federation agreements, and monitoring data model changes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Addresses the comprehensive administrative control needs of Registry Managers as defined in stakeholder section 3.6.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement Mandatory Access Controls (MAC) that mediate access to all objects based on the clearance level of the subject and the sensitivity label of the object. MAC shall be enforced above any Discretionary Access Controls (DAC) at all times and shall not be overridable by any user-level discretionary setting.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements the non-negotiable MAC-over-DAC security architecture mandated by BR-08 and C-14, consistent with DoD information assurance policy.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement Discretionary Access Controls (DAC) allowing subjects with appropriate permissions to delegate access to other subjects, subject to the overriding constraints of MAC. No user shall be permitted to self-elevate privileges; all privilege assignments and superuser designations shall be managed exclusively by authorized Registry Managers.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements BR-07 and BR-09, ensuring DAC delegation is available but bounded by MAC and administrative governance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an XML Services (XS) module that operates across all four defined operational modes: Environmental (real-time operations), End User, Maintenance, and Training. The module shall expose the full set of data management services appropriate to each mode as defined in the operational mode capability matrix.
generated_requirements.rationale: Establishes the foundational multi-mode operational requirement for the XS module as specified in Section 6.1 and Objective 3.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to define and reset session time-out values prior to the initialization of any user session. Session time-out values shall be dynamically adjustable in response to communications performance data provided by network management or DBMS monitoring capabilities, without requiring system restart.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements BR-12 and Objective 3 success criteria for dynamic session management across all operational environments.

generated_requirements.text: When network management data feeds or DBMS performance monitoring outputs are unavailable, the system shall fall back to manual configuration of session time-out values. The system shall alert the operator when automatic session time-out adjustment is unavailable due to missing data feeds.
generated_requirements.rationale: Addresses dependency D-03 and ensures graceful degradation of the dynamic session tuning capability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized on-site personnel to redefine database connect descriptors — including fully qualified object name, protocol, host, port, and instance name — without requiring system reinitialization or restart.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements BR-13 and Objective 3 success criteria, enabling operational flexibility when network topology changes.

generated_requirements.text: In fixed (garrison) operational environments, the system shall provide on-site personnel with the capability to tune data management service parameters — including workload configurations and database sizing — without requiring vendor intervention.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements BR-10 and the domain expert's requirement for explicit per-mode capability decomposition for fixed environments.

generated_requirements.text: In deployed (dynamic) operational environments, the system shall provide a reduced but defined set of administrative tuning capabilities to on-site personnel, reflecting the more predetermined nature of deployed mission workloads. The specific parameters available for tuning in deployed mode shall be documented in the operational mode capability matrix.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements BR-11 and the domain expert's requirement that fixed and deployed administrative capability scopes be captured as separate, explicit requirement sets.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall operate in a Training mode that uses training application interfaces to the DBMS rather than production mission application interfaces. Training mode shall replicate production processing fidelity. No read or write path from training mode to the production database schema shall exist; training operations shall not access, modify, or expose live operational data.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements BR-14 and the domain expert's requirement that training-mode isolation be enforced architecturally, not merely as policy.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall process all relational data access operations using ANSI standard SQL as specified in FIPS PUB 127-2. No proprietary SQL extensions shall be used for any data access operation that is required to be interoperable across COE-compliant platforms.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements C-02 and BR-19, satisfying the federal information processing standard with direct procurement and certification implications.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall record all semantic structures in JTA-compliant formalism, using the version of the Joint Technical Architecture in effect at project initiation as the binding reference. Any JTA revision during the project lifecycle shall be assessed for impact on recorded semantics and a change impact report produced.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements C-04 and the domain expert constraint that JTA-compliant semantic recording is a non-negotiable regulatory baseline.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support remote data access conforming to ISO RDA (ISO 9579) standards for application-layer interoperability across OSI networks, applicable to federated registry queries and cross-site data access operations.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements C-10, ensuring federated and cross-site data access patterns conform to the required ISO standard.

generated_requirements.text: The Registry and XS module software shall execute without platform-specific modification on all COE-compliant hardware and operating system platforms designated in the program baseline. No platform-specific APIs, file system paths, native libraries, or COTS RDBMS vendor extensions shall be used in any component required to be portable.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements C-06 and the COE portability constraint, ensuring the software can be certified and reused across the joint force without bespoke porting effort.

generated_requirements.text: All software segments shall be packaged, delivered, and segmented in accordance with DII COE guidelines and the compliance levels specified in the I&RTS. Each segment shall pass formal qualification through at least one of the following methods — test, demonstration, analysis, or inspection — as appropriate to the requirement type.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements C-01 and C-05, ensuring COE certification readiness and formal qualification traceability for every delivered segment.

generated_requirements.text: Every SRS-level requirement shall be assigned a unique identifier, a qualification method (test, demonstration, analysis, or inspection), and a reference to the corresponding qualification test procedure. This traceability matrix shall be maintained as a controlled deliverable throughout the project lifecycle.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements the domain expert's qualification traceability matrix pattern, which is a standard DoD acquisition deliverable required for COE certification.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall ensure that no database transaction is partially committed. Every transaction shall be either fully committed or fully rolled back, restoring all affected data items to their pre-transaction state. This atomicity guarantee shall apply to all distributed and replicated registry database operations across all sites.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements BR-15 and the domain expert's transaction atomicity pattern, which is critical for distributed registry databases where partial commits can corrupt the proponent scheme.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a transaction journal for all database transactions, recording sufficient information to support audit, recovery, and replication operations. The journal shall be used to support rollback to a consistent state following any failure.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements BR-17, providing the audit and recovery foundation required for a distributed DoD registry.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement and enforce a proponent scheme that explicitly defines: which sites hold replicated database copies, which site holds update authority at the source level, and which site holds update authority at the record level. Conflict resolution rules for concurrent updates shall be defined and enforced by the system.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements BR-16 and the domain expert's proponent scheme pattern, preventing data integrity failures in multi-site deployments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support mirrored database configurations and RAID storage implementations including RAID levels 0, 1, 3, 4, 5, and 6, as supported by the resident COTS RDBMS utilities and operating system, to provide reliability and enable rapid failure recovery.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements C-13, ensuring the storage infrastructure supports the reliability and rapid recovery requirements of operational DoD environments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain operational continuity and data integrity under degraded communications conditions. Session parameters shall remain adjustable in response to network performance data during degraded operations. The system shall not require full communications restoration to resume normal data management service operations.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements C-11 and Objective 3, treating operational continuity under degraded communications as a hard requirement across the full spectrum of military operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall define and document the behavior of in-progress sessions and their time-out state upon system restart or failover. In degraded communications environments, the system shall preserve session state sufficient to allow session resumption or graceful termination without data loss.
generated_requirements.rationale: Addresses the domain expert's session parameter persistence and recovery pattern, which is operationally critical in degraded communications scenarios.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide XML document structure services including: schema design assistance, XML tag validation, well-formedness checking, conformance validation against DoD standards, DTD generation, and version management for XML document structures.
generated_requirements.rationale: Defines the XML document structure service capabilities of the XS module as specified in Section 6.1.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide XML content services including: tag creation and reuse support, semantic structure recording in JTA-compliant formalism, XSL transformation-based view generation, XML document disassembly, and cross-referencing and indexing of XML content.
generated_requirements.rationale: Defines the XML content service capabilities of the XS module as specified in Section 6.1 and Objective 2.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide XML consumption services including: DOM and SAX parsing of XML documents, conformance checking of consumed documents against registered schemas, semantic view extraction via XSL transformations, and hierarchical-to-relational disassembly of XML structures.
generated_requirements.rationale: Defines the XML consumption service capabilities of the XS module as specified in Section 6.1, enabling consuming applications to correctly interpret registered tags.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement standard APIs for Registry access as specified in Appendix B and standard APIs for XS function access as specified in Appendix C. These APIs are fixed interface contracts; any deviation shall require formal change control. The APIs shall be transparent to operators — operators shall not be required to manage or configure internal interfaces to DBIF, DAC, or COTS RDBMS components.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements C-07 and C-08, ensuring consistent integration across mission systems and protecting the fixed API contracts.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support heterogeneous data types and sources across COE-compliant platforms, including: spatial/geographic data with graphical attributes, relational data, and XML-structured data. Spatial DBMS capabilities shall be integrated via the XS module's heterogeneous data management services.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements C-09 and D-07, ensuring the XS module can serve mission applications that require geographic and mixed-type data management.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain location transparency for all data access operations: the physical location of data shall be transparent to applications and users, with no requirement for users to know the physical origin of data accessed through views or federated queries.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements C-15, ensuring that distributed and federated data access does not impose physical topology knowledge on application developers or end users.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide data management administrative tools enabling authorized Database Managers to: manage storage and memory structures, control user permissions and privileges, perform database maintenance operations (shutdown, mount/unmount, online/offline transitions), and tune workload parameters appropriate to both fixed and deployed environments.
generated_requirements.rationale: Addresses the primary needs of Data and Database Managers as defined in stakeholder section 3.4.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide consumers with the capability to extract semantically valid alternate views of registered XML documents via XSL transformations without requiring access to the originating application.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements Objective 2 success criteria, enabling semantic interoperability for consuming applications across organizational boundaries.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate all submitted XML schemas for well-formedness and conformance to DoD standards prior to publication. No schema that fails well-formedness or conformance validation shall be published to the registry.
generated_requirements.rationale: Implements Objective 2 success criteria and the domain expert's requirement that validation be a blocking gate, ensuring only valid schemas are available to consumers.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the retirement lifecycle state for registered information resources. Retired resources shall remain accessible in the registry with their full version history and retirement rationale, but shall be clearly marked as retired and excluded from default discovery results.
generated_requirements.rationale: Completes the full information resource lifecycle (including retirement) required by Objective 1 and Section 6.1.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce that zero approved tags exist in the registry without a complete, validated Information Resource Submittal Package on record. Registry integrity checks shall be executable by Registry Managers to verify this invariant at any time.
generated_requirements.rationale: Directly implements Objective 1 success criteria and provides an auditable mechanism for Registry Managers to verify registry completeness.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be maintainable such that session time-out values, database connect descriptors, workload parameters, and database sizing configurations are all modifiable by authorized on-site personnel without requiring system reinitialization, vendor intervention, or redeployment of software components.
generated_requirements.rationale: Consolidates the operational adaptability maintainability requirements from BR-10 through BR-13 and Objective 3, ensuring the system can be tuned in real time across all operational environments.

sme_subject: DoD data standards architect and DII COE compliance engineer
sme_advisory: This system sits at the intersection of DII COE compliance engineering, DoD data standards governance, and operational XML infrastructure — a combination that demands requirements precision well beyond typical enterprise registry systems. The requirements engineer must treat MAC/DAC enforcement, FIPS PUB 127-2 SQL conformance, JTA-compliant semantic recording, and COE segmentation not as design choices but as non-negotiable regulatory baselines that must each be individually traceable to qualification evidence. Special attention is warranted for the operational spectrum requirement: the system must degrade gracefully under communications stress while preserving data integrity and access control invariants, and the distinction between fixed-garrison and deployed-unit administrative capability scopes must be explicitly captured as separate requirement sets rather than a single parameterized capability.

ground_truth_requirements.text: Environmental Mode: This is the real-time operations that must react to varying degrees of readiness to full scale wartime operations such as crisis planning with the use of heterogeneous data types and sources, transfer capabilities, data management services. COE Compliance. XML Services shall be segmented. COE sponsors shall adhere to compliance level requirements described in the I&RTS. In the fixed (static) mode of operation (base or data processing megacenter), the data management services shall have the capability of being tuned by on-site personnel to adjust for varying workloads and sizes of associated databases. These workloads and databases are expected to change more frequently and to a greater extent than for processing associated with deployed units In a changing (dynamic) environment, such as with deployed units, the workload and database sizes may be more predetermined (given a more precise mission) and require access to fewer data management administrative capabilities than needed in a fixed environment. The XS shall have the ability to redefine or reset names of connect descriptors to database server instances. Connect descriptors are fully qualified object names and include address (protocol/host/port) and instance name. In a degraded communications environment, there is a need, for example, to be able to reset session time-out values if the data management services are being accessed by users affected by the communications degradation. At a minimum, the session time-out values shall be user definable and be able to be reset prior to initialization of a user session. The goal is to provide the option of dynamically changing session time values based on current communications performance identified by capabilities of the network management or DBMS. End User Mode: Portion of XS services shall be used by various classes of users: data consumers, data and database managers, network information infrastructure resource managers. Some of these uses of the data management services will entail unique requirements that shall be fulfilled within the capability of XS services. Maintenance Mode: This mode includes modification and/or addition of application data segments, user permission, privileges, and restructuring storage and memory areas. In addition, maintenance also shall pertain to shutdown, open not-mounted and online/off-line implementations, modifications, upgrade, or other related actions. The data management services shall support managing various types of data, database architectures and platforms that includes hardware and software at the specified sites. Training Mode. In support of training activities, the data management services shall provide for the same processing as would be encountered in a production environment. However, access to the database may be via a training application access to the DBMS rather than from the production mission application.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Producer services A producer is an agent that contributes metadata for inclusion into an XML Registry for the purposes of ensuring maximum semantic understanding of a term as it appears in an XML document. To contribute metadata to a registry, the producer must be able to receive XML registry forms, submit metadata and the related information resource artifacts, and notify. Produce and display submittal forms as part of the Information Resource Submittal Package from the web containing the following Information Resource artifacts: XML Tag Specification, XML Spec (i.e. DTD, DCD etc.), Sample of XML document of the tag to be submitted. The Package is to be compressed and emailed or sent ftp to an addressee. Download Information Resource Submittal Package from the web containing forms, instructions, and tools for submission to XML Registry. Submit prescribed metadata related to information resource type, information resource association, status code, data types specified and other related information specified in Appendix A within a combination of forms. These forms are part of the Information Resource Submittal Package containing the following Information Resource artifacts: XML Tag Specification, XML Spec (i.e. DTD, DCD etc.), Sample of XML document of the tag to be submitted. The Package is to be compressed and emailed or sent ftp to an addressee. Submit metadata by an on-line interactive process Submit metadata by a off-line and interactive batch process Parse submitted XML Registry specification forms Populate XML Registry database Modify of specified terms & definitions of metadata and status of Information Resources. Associate Information Resources specified in Appendix A. Annotate rejected? Assemble registered information resources to form new components. Assemble new DTDs from current Produce DTD as an instantiation (others are database schema, message definition) for modeling environment. Notify change in Information Resources or authorized producer of tag. Provide a capability to post planned changes to a registry Approve and reject submissions Forward request to different registry Consumer services. A consumer examines a registry to select a tag structure for reuse in one or more applications that will exchange data according to a pre-defined agreement. Discovery View the XML Tags with the following relationships: Ancestor/ Descendant relationship: Provide the capability to view a Tag’s origin Uses/Used by relationship: Provide the capability to view a complex Container Tag’s parent/child relationship Data type information: Provide the capability to view a tag’s data type and related information. Versioning relationship: Provide the capability to view an Information resource’s versions Reference Sets: Provide the capability to view text related to the domai values or the related reference set Amplifying information: Provide the capability to view other information resources such as ERwin models, DTDs, documents, etc., which describe or otherwise provide amplifying information. View the XML Tags via a tree/hierarchy structure or tabular format. View the XML Tags by giving the user multiple search options to find a specific Tag. View the XML tags by giving the user the search option to find all tags of a given subscriber/author. Each Information Resource will have its own web page to allow the user to view all pertinent information, according to its information resource type. View the Information Resource Submittal Package containing the following Information Resource artifacts: XML Tag Specification, XML Spec (i.e. DTD, DCD etc.), Sample of XML document of the tag to be submitted. Display an XML Tag Specification form to the author of a given information resource. This XML Tag Spec will be used for inputting the requesting information for a specific Information Resource. Provide capabilities to download the XML Tags or other selected Information Resources. Catalog entities and attributes within servers to enable browsing, searching, retrieving of data related to XML sources. Process ANSI standard SQL as specified in FIPS PUB 127-2 Establish rules to ensure maximum semantic understanding of a term as it appears in an XML document Links to DTDs, DCDs, Namespace Provide a capability to subscribe for notification of changes to Communities of Interest (COE) or Information Resources. Manager services create and manage usernames, superusers Establish acceptable naming convention not to be in conflict with the DOD data standards convention and establish a relationship to other naming conventions. Create a naming structure within the COE architecture to express the context and relationship of the naming convention to other naming conventions specified in the I&RTS. Define a set of metadata tags, information attributed to metadata tags (meta-metadata) and other related terms for the maintenance and control of XML tags. Review/approve submission Monitor changes to data models recorded in Registry pass request off to … (per explicit federation agreements) Design the schema for an XML document Structure services Record structure Check against standard (e.g., style guide wizard for tags) Validate Use and reference existing tags and associated semantic structures Content services Use & reference existing tags & associated semantic structures (extracted from Registry) create new tags & associated semantic structures interchange w/other design tools & environments Record semantic structure in JTA-compliant formalism Generate standard views Version metadata objects Generate xml & schema documents Generate a document that includes (by ref or by value) a schema Develop scheme to permit dynamic cross-referencing and indexing of XML objects. ground_truth_requirements.text: COE Software will be qualified through formal validation tests of the SRS level requirements. The Qualification Methods applied to the software shall include test, demonstration, analysis, and inspection (T, D, A, I). COE Software will be qualified through formal validation tests of the SRS level requirements. The Qualification Methods applied to the software shall include test, demonstration, analysis, and inspection (T, D, A, I). TEST. A qualification method that is carried out by operation of the item/component/I/F (or some part of the computer S/W configuration item, etc.) and that relies on the collection and subsequent examination of data. DEMONSTRATIONS A qualification method that is carried out by operation of the item/component/I/F (or some part of the computer S/W configuration item, etc.), and that relies on observable functional operation not requiring the use of elaborate instrumentation or special test equipment. ANALYSIS A qualification method that is carried out by the processing of accumulated data. An example of accumulated data is the compilation of data obtained from other qualification methods. Examples of the processing of accumulated data are interpretations or extrapolations made from the data. INSPECTION A qualification method that is carried out by visual examination, physical manipulation, or measurement to verify that the requirements have been satisfied
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Automatically: Indicates processing initiated during execution of other processes, but dependent on information and/or parameters to be generated or supplied to these other processes. The information / parameters may be data dependent, or application dependent, or dependent on a manual process/human intervention. It will include controls qualifying the processing involved. Business Rule: A narrative description of policies, procedures, or principles within an organization. Business rules can be divided in to four categories: definitions, facts, constraints, and derivations. Definitions are business rules that define entities and attributes. Facts are either links (relationships) between entities or associations between an entity and attributes Constraints are conditions about the data that must always be true. They are the integrity rules that protect the data in the eventual database. Derivations are business rules that materialize a new piece of information (often attribute values) from other pieces of information. For example, a mathematical derivation might specify that you can obtain a person's age by subtracting his or her birth date from the current date. Commit/Rollback: An individual transaction is processed (commit) or discarded (rollback) by the proponent maintainer of the data items involved. Discretionary Access Controls (DAC): A means of restricting access to objects based on the identity of subjects or groups to which they belong. The controls are discretionary in the sense that a subject with a certain access permission is capable of passing that permission on to any other subject. Dynamically Generated Processing: Indicates processing initiated during execution of other processes, but dependent upon information and/or parameters to be generated or supplied to these other processes. The information/parameters may be data dependent, or application dependent, or dependent on a manual process/human intervention. It will include controls qualifying the processing involved. Location Transparency: occurs when the physical location of data is transparent to the applications and users of the database system. For example, a view that joins table data from several databases provides location transparency because the user of the view does not need to know where the data originates from. Mandatory Access Control (MAC): Imediates access to an object based on the clearance level of the subject (user) and the sensitivity label of the object. (These controls are always enforced above any discretionary control implemented by users). Mirrored Databases: Replication and maintenance of a database on a transaction basis for the purpose of rapid error or failure recovery as supported by the resident COTS RDBMS own system utilities and operating system. Object: A passive entity that contains or receives information. Access to an object potentially implies access to the information it contains. Examples of objects are records, blocks, pages, segments, files, directories, directory trees, and programs, as well as bits, bytes, words, fields, processors, video displays, keyboards, clocks, printers, and network nodes. Proponent Scheme: Describes the sites at which databases are replicated and also who owns and has update authority with respect to the data at each site. It refers to proponency at the source and record level. Redundant Array of Inexpensive Disks (RAID): A RAID system appears as one very large, reliable disk to the CPU. The main reason for using RAID storage is its reliability. RAID has the same advantages as shadowing and striping at a lower cost. There are several types/levels of RAID implementations, including: RAID 0 (known as disk striping), RAID 1 (known as disk shadowing), RAID 3 (in which data is distributed in small increments across all data disks and adds a parity value to a separate disk for recovery if any disk fails, RAID 4 (in which data is distributed in large chunks across all data disks and also has a single parity disk. RAID 4 intended to overcome performance penalties of RAID 3 for small transfers. RAID 5 (in which parity over RAID 3 or RAID 4 implementations), and RAID 6 ( in which two parity disks in addition to data disks are used in an attempt to further improve performance). In a RAID 5 implementation, the data is stored as are check sums and other information about the contents of each disk in the array. If one disk is lost, the others can use the check sums and other stored information to recreate the lost data. Storage system vendors may provide additional enhancements to RAID level implementations to improve performance and reliability. Remote Data Access (RDA): is an ISO (9579) application layer interoperability standard (protocol and formats) to support access by an application to a (remote) DBMSs over an OSI network. The goal of RDA is to allow interoperability between applications (clients) and databases (servers) of different manufacturers so that an application is able to read and update data in remote databases via well defined standards. RDA defines a set of client and server standards and a mapping of SQL commands to these services. RDA also defines an interface to ISO (transaction processing) two phase commit TP services in the case where updates to multiple remote databases need to be coordinated. RDA does not yet define interoperability between server databases (i.e. it is not yet a standard for distributed database management). Replication Scheme: Information that precisely identifies DBs, or partitions of DBs, to be copied and/or distributed, replication schedules, and master/remote sites that are to receive the copies. Spatial DBMS: Geographic information system that organizes and maintains spatial data (i.e. data with graphical attributes) in terms of type, scale, location(s), extent, topology and geometry. Supports queries of spatial data where the selection criteria are defined by spatial attributes. SRI: A Standing Request For Information (SRI) is a capability in which CASS monitors for the occurrence of conditions established by an application program, and notifies the calling or establishing application program when the conditions are satisfied. An SRI may be one of three types: timer-based, data-based, or message-based. Subject: An active entity, generally in the form of a person, process, or device that causes information to flow among objects or changes the system state. Technically, a process/domain pair. Transaction Journalling: Individual messages or database transactions are stored in a journal file, which may be a linear log file or a circular file.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: If the term is well recognized outside its container term, designate it as an element. Example: CTRY_CD, CTRY_NM, CTRY_ABBRD_NM, CTRY_OFF_NM, CTRY_SCP_NT_TX, and CTRY_PSTL_NM are all characteristics (ER-attributes) for the entity CTRY. In the relational world, they are columns within the table ctry, attributes of the entity ctry in er-modeling, and member attributes in object modeling. It would be expected that a submitter would identify them as attributes rather than elements. But if they were identified as attributes of the element ctry, then the additional baggage (other attributes) must be carried, or submitted as separate elements. We wish to limit the proliferation of tags, so we strongly urge folks to use XML_Attributes sparingly. If the term is well recognized outside its container term, designate it as an element. Example: CTRY_CD, CTRY_NM, CTRY_ABBRD_NM, CTRY_OFF_NM, CTRY_SCP_NT_TX, and CTRY_PSTL_NM are all characteristics (ER-attributes) for the entity CTRY. In the relational world, they are columns within the table ctry, attributes of the entity ctry in er-modeling, and member attributes in object modeling. It would be expected that a submitter would identify them as attributes rather than elements. But if they were identified as attributes of the element ctry, then the additional baggage (other attributes) must be carried, or submitted as separate elements. We wish to limit the proliferation of tags, so we strongly urge folks to use XML_Attributes sparingly.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall complete Normal Startup (from power-on to fully operational Operations State) within 60 seconds.
generated_requirements.rationale: KPI from Section 2.2 mandates ≤60-second normal startup to minimize mission interruption and protect air vehicle safety.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall complete Recovery Startup (from abnormal termination detection to fully operational Operations State) within 45 seconds.
generated_requirements.rationale: KPI from Section 2.2 mandates ≤45-second recovery startup to prevent loss of air vehicle control following abnormal termination.

generated_requirements.text: Following abnormal termination, the TCS shall present the operator with exactly three recovery options: (1) Resume in the Same Modes and Data; (2) Resume in the Same Modes but Review and Modify Command Data; or (3) Perform a Command Shutdown and Startup via Normal Startup Mode.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-18 mandates these three specific recovery paths and prohibits any other recovery path without operator selection.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall automatically record system state data, interface communications, and operator inputs — including data elements, timestamps, and interface identifiers — sufficient to support full event reconstruction following any abnormal termination, and shall retain this journal for a minimum of the most recent 72-hour operational period.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-19 and the domain expert advisory require tamper-evident journaling with sufficient fidelity to support mishap investigation and after-action review.

generated_requirements.text: No single hardware failure shall result in an unsafe command being transmitted to the air vehicle.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-10 establishes a hard safety constraint on the AV command path to prevent loss of air vehicle due to hardware faults.

generated_requirements.text: No single software error shall result in an unsafe command being transmitted to the air vehicle.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-11 establishes a hard safety constraint on the AV command path to prevent loss of air vehicle due to software faults.

generated_requirements.text: Upon detection of loss of link with an air vehicle, the TCS shall automatically initiate reacquisition attempts, display a Loss of Link timer, display the last known AV status values with their timestamp, and compute and display the estimated AV dead-reckoning position based on last known position and current flight mode (flight route plan, emergency flight route plan, or maintain current heading).
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-12 and BR-13 mandate specific LOL autonomous behaviors to prevent ambiguity that could result in loss of the air vehicle.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall integrate an Uninterruptible Power Supply (UPS) that assumes the full system load within a time interval short enough to prevent loss of air vehicle control during any power outage; UPS engagement shall be mandatory during takeoff and landing phases.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-14 and Section 4.2 mandate UPS integration with a hard operational constraint during critical flight phases to protect air vehicle safety.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall support concurrent uplink and downlink communication with up to two simultaneous UAVs and their payloads from a single TCS station.
generated_requirements.rationale: OC-06 establishes the baseline operational requirement for dual simultaneous UAV control from a single station.

generated_requirements.text: When two simultaneous UAVs generate concurrent caution or warning conditions, the TCS HCI shall prioritize and display alerts according to mission criticality, ensuring the higher-severity alert is presented to the operator without suppression of the lower-severity alert, and shall provide the operator with a clear indication of which UAV each alert pertains to.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert advisory identifies multi-UAV concurrent alert arbitration as a safety-critical HCI requirement to prevent operator cognitive overload.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS AV Standard Interface shall be implemented as a vehicle-agnostic abstraction layer such that integrating a new UAV type requires only the addition of a new AV-specific Interface Design Document (IDD) plug-in and shall not require changes to common planning, control, or C4I dissemination software.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-09 and the domain expert advisory require an abstraction layer to protect the common software baseline when future UAV types are added.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall provide a modular software architecture, compliant with the Defense Information Infrastructure Common Operating Environment (DII COE), such that additional UAV types, payloads, and C4I interfaces can be integrated without redesign of common planning and control functions.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-05 and TC-06 mandate DII COE compliance and modular architecture to support lifecycle extensibility across future UAV programs.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall implement its Human-Computer Interface using X-Windows and Motif as the GUI framework, providing a four-window display configuration per control console comprising: (1) map/flight path, (2) flight/payload data with video background, (3) telemetry graphics, and (4) interface menus.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-03 mandates X-Windows/Motif, and Section 6.1 specifies the four-window layout as the standard operator display configuration.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS HCI shall respond to any operator command with a system acknowledgement within 1 second of input.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 2.2 KPI mandates ≤1-second command-to-acknowledgement latency to ensure operator confidence in command execution.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS end-to-end data latency shall not exceed the lower of the Predator GCS or Outrider GCS data latency benchmarks as measured under equivalent operational conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-13 and Section 2.2 establish legacy GCS latency as the contractual performance floor, not an aspirational target.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall sustain continuous operation in functional Operations Mode for a minimum of 72 hours without interruption.
generated_requirements.rationale: OC-05 and Section 2.2 mandate 72-hour continuous operational availability to support extended tactical missions.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS system Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) shall meet or exceed the MTBF values specified in the Predator and Outrider Operational Requirements Documents (ORDs).
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 2.2 establishes Predator and Outrider ORD MTBF values as the minimum reliability benchmark for the TCS.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS system Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) shall be less than or equal to the MTTR values specified in the Predator and Outrider ORDs.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 2.2 establishes Predator and Outrider ORD MTTR values as the maximum maintainability threshold for the TCS.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS system operational availability (Ao) shall meet or exceed the Ao values specified in the Predator and Outrider ORDs.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 2.2 mandates Ao compliance with legacy ORD benchmarks to ensure the TCS does not degrade operational readiness.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall not utilize more than 50% of delivered system throughput capacity on a 10-second rolling average, with an objective of not exceeding 25%, to preserve headroom for peak loads and future growth.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 2.2 KPI mandates throughput headroom to ensure the system can absorb additional UAV types and payload data streams without degradation.

generated_requirements.text: Each TCS processor shall maintain at least 50% unused memory on a 10-second rolling average during Normal Operations, and at least 50% unused throughput on a 10-second rolling average with no less than 20% unused over any 1-second period.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 2.2 KPIs mandate per-processor headroom to support future capability growth without hardware replacement.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall maintain at least 50% spare storage capacity above delivered storage utilization, with an objective of 75%, and at least 50% reserve I/O channel capacity per processor.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 2.2 KPIs mandate storage and I/O headroom to accommodate payload data growth and additional interface connections.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall store a minimum of 24 hours of payload data in onboard storage, with the ability to expand storage capacity via external devices without architectural changes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 2.2 and Section 6.1 mandate 24-hour payload data retention to support mission data review and intelligence exploitation.

generated_requirements.text: All TCS imagery output shall comply with NITF 2.0, CIGSS, USIS, Video Working Group Standards Architecture, and GCCS standards; imagery files that do not contain complete telemetry and support data shall not be exported to any C4I consumer.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-24 and TC-08 mandate full imagery standards compliance and prohibit export of incomplete imagery to prevent downstream exploitation failures.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall provide limited on-station imagery exploitation capabilities including: image enhancement (contrast, brightness, edge enhancement, sharpness), annotation, freeze-frame capture, target location computation, and range and bearing calculation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 defines the required on-station exploitation functions to support the intelligence cycle without relying on downstream systems.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall display near-real-time (NRT) imagery with date/time group, target coordinates, north-seeking arrow, and AV position and heading overlaid on the imagery display.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 mandates NRT imagery display metadata to provide operators and intelligence consumers with full situational context.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall interface with and exchange data with the following C4I systems: ASAS, JSTARS GSM, JMCIS, JSIPS-N, AFATDS, CARS, AFMSS, TAMPS, GCS/ACS IPF, TES, IAS, ATHS, ATWCS, Trojan Spirit II, TBMCS, MIES, ETRAC, COMPASS, and TEG.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 and Section 4.2 enumerate all required C4I interfaces to achieve joint interoperability across the DoD C4I enterprise.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall support creation, transmission, and reception of tactical messages in USMTF, TACFIRE, OTH-Gold, and IEWCOMCAT formats, and shall exchange both annotated and un-annotated digital imagery with connected C4I systems.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 mandates support for all named tactical message formats to enable seamless joint force communications.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall continuously monitor the health and status of each C4I interface and shall detect, report to the operator, and isolate individual interface failures without cascading impact on other C4I interfaces or on AV control functions.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert advisory requires interface health monitoring and fault isolation across 15+ C4I connections to prevent single-interface failures from degrading AV control.

generated_requirements.text: All incoming and outgoing formatted tactical messages shall be automatically logged; this logging shall be mandatory and shall not be disableable during operations.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-23 mandates non-disableable message logging to ensure a complete audit trail for intelligence and operational review.

generated_requirements.text: All digital messages received by the TCS shall be automatically checked for errors and corrected where possible; messages that cannot be corrected shall be flagged to the operator and shall not be silently discarded or processed as valid data.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-22 mandates error checking and operator notification to prevent corrupted data from propagating into AV commands or intelligence products.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall provide a map display supporting zoom, scroll, print, and clear functions, with support for DTED, DFAD, ADRG, CADRG, and vector format map data, and shall display coordinates in Latitude/Longitude, UTM, and MGRS formats with multi-spheroid conversion accuracy not exceeding sub-meter circular error probable (CEP).
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 and the domain expert advisory mandate map display capabilities and coordinate conversion accuracy to prevent targeting errors in C4I message chains.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS map display shall simultaneously render at least 100 threat icons per overlay layer, including fire support coordination zones, airspace control zones, threat systems, and threat engagement envelopes with radar terrain masking.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 2.2 KPI and Section 6.1 mandate 100-icon-per-layer capacity to provide adequate situational awareness in dense threat environments.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall provide a display declutter capability that allows the operator to select information density levels and shall automatically trigger declutter recommendations when the number of simultaneously displayed icons or data elements exceeds operator-configurable thresholds.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert advisory identifies display declutter as a safety-critical HCI requirement to prevent cognitive overload with 100+ icons per overlay layer.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall support flight route planning including waypoint definition, terrain avoidance warning, minimum reception altitude calculation for line-of-sight flights, and line-of-sight versus terrain profile display.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 mandates these mission planning functions to enable safe and effective UAV mission preparation.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall support separate payload planning for EO, IR, and SAR payloads, including payload swath visualization on the map display.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 requires payload planning to be independent of flight route planning to support multi-sensor mission optimization.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall be capable of planning and launching a UAV mission within 1 hour of receiving a tasking order, after emplacement at the operational site.
generated_requirements.rationale: OC-02 and Section 2.2 mandate 1-hour mission planning and launch readiness to meet tactical responsiveness requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall be configurable for ground, air (CH-47/CH-53D, C-130), sea, and rail transport, and de-configurable from operational configuration, within 2 hours for each transport mode.
generated_requirements.rationale: OC-01 and Section 2.2 mandate 2-hour transport configuration/de-configuration to support rapid multi-domain deployment.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS hardware shall be ruggedized and mounted to withstand inter- and intra-theater movement by ground, air, sea, and rail transport per the environmental specifications defined in the System/Subsystem Design Document.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-12 and Section 6.1 mandate ruggedization to ensure operational readiness after transport across all deployment domains.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall support operation from land-based platforms, shipboard environments, submarines, and airborne platforms using a single system configuration baseline, with service-specific variants achieved through modular hardware selection.
generated_requirements.rationale: BO-08 and Section 4.2 mandate multi-domain operational capability from a single baseline to maximize cross-service flexibility.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS power supply shall support 110/220V, 50/60 Hz military and commercial power sources, and all DoD ground mobile, airborne, and shipboard electrical power standards.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-11 mandates broad power compatibility to enable operation across all service-specific deployment environments.

generated_requirements.text: All TCS warning messages shall be color-coded and flashed in accordance with MIL-STD-1472, and auditory alerts shall be adjustable to at least 20 dB above the speech interference level at the operator's ear; deviations from MIL-STD-1472 color coding and flash rates are not permitted.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-07, RC-05, and Section 2.2 mandate MIL-STD-1472 compliance and measurable auditory alert levels to ensure operator perception of safety-critical warnings.

generated_requirements.text: An operator must explicitly acknowledge and confirm before disabling the display of any critical warning flag associated with AV, Payload, ADT, GDT, or TCS faults; silent dismissal of critical warnings is not permitted.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-06 mandates explicit operator acknowledgement to prevent inadvertent suppression of safety-critical fault indications.

generated_requirements.text: All warning messages and HCI operator actions shall be automatically archived; this archiving shall be mandatory and shall not be disableable by the operator.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-08 mandates non-disableable HCI action archiving to support post-event review and mishap investigation.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS HCI shall be ergonomically designed for operators in the 5th-to-95th anthropometric percentile range and shall be fully operable while wearing cold weather clothing and MOPP gear, in compliance with MIL-STD-1472D Section 5.13 and MIL-STD-2036 Section 5.1.3.11.
generated_requirements.rationale: RC-03, RC-04, and Section 6.1 mandate MOPP-gear operability and MIL-STD compliance to ensure operator effectiveness in all tactical environments.

generated_requirements.text: Software upgrades, modification of programmable parameters, and software debug and diagnostic capabilities shall be accessible only to Authorized Operators authenticated by password protection at minimum.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 through BR-03 mandate password-protected access control for privileged functions to prevent unauthorized system modification.

generated_requirements.text: All TCS communication links shall be secured at a classification level appropriate to the sensitivity of transmitted material in accordance with DoD Directive C-5200.5 (COMSEC), and minimum security requirements shall be determined using the risk assessment procedures defined in DoD 5200.28(D).
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-26, BR-27, RC-01, and RC-02 mandate COMSEC compliance and risk-assessment-driven security baselines for all TCS communication links.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall enforce a hard architectural boundary between training operations and live mission data paths, ensuring that training message injection and simulated AV state data cannot contaminate live mission data, AV commands, or C4I message streams; formal training operations shall not execute concurrently with actual mission execution.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-20, BR-21, and the domain expert advisory require an architectural firewall between training and live modes as a safety and data integrity requirement.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall provide embedded or add-on interactive training courseware supporting self-paced instruction in mission planning, mission control and monitoring, imagery processing, tactical communications, AV control, and online diagnostics, with automated recording and measurement of operator and maintainer proficiency.
generated_requirements.rationale: BO-07 and Section 6.1 mandate embedded training capability to reduce operator retraining burden when transitioning between UAV types.

generated_requirements.text: The TCS shall apply EMI control measures throughout Phase 1 design to ensure compatibility with the electromagnetic environment of all service-specific operational environments; specific RADHAZ and HERO values shall be determined during Phase 1 and formally invoked in LRIP Phase 2 specifications without being prematurely fixed in Phase 1 requirements.
generated_requirements.rationale: RC-08, RC-09, and Section 6.2 mandate phased EMI compliance to protect operational effectiveness without prematurely constraining Phase 2 electromagnetic specifications.

domain: defense / military unmanned aerial systems (UAS) ground control
sme_subject: DoD UAV systems engineer and joint C4I interoperability architect
sme_advisory: The TCS program sits at the intersection of multi-service UAV control, joint C4I interoperability, and safety-critical real-time computing — a combination that demands unusually rigorous requirements coverage across safety, security, performance, and interoperability dimensions simultaneously. Requirements engineers should pay particular attention to the safety-critical command path (no single hardware or software fault shall produce an unsafe AV command), the strict startup/recovery timing thresholds, and the layered imagery standards compliance chain (NITF 2.0 → CIGSS → USIS → GCCS) that underpins the entire intelligence dissemination value proposition. Because the TCS must serve Army, Navy, Marine Corps, and Air Force from a single baseline, requirements must explicitly address service-variant configuration management, cross-service operator transition, and the modular AV Standard Interface that must absorb future UAV types without architectural rework.

ground_truth_requirements.text: To the extent possible, the TCS will use standard DoD components in order to achieve commonality. The TCS will use the computing hardware specified by the service specific procurement contracts. The individual armed services will identify TCS computing hardware, the desired level of TCS functionality, the battlefield C4I connectivity, and the particular type of air vehicle and payloads to be operated depending upon the deployment concept and area of operations. TCS hardware must be scaleable or modular to meet varying Service needs. TCS hardware will permit long range communications from one TCS to another, data storage expansion, access to other computers to share in processing capability, and multiple external peripherals. To the extent possible, the TCS will use standard DoD components in order to achieve commonality. The TCS will use the computing hardware specified by the service specific procurement contracts. The individual armed services will identify TCS computing hardware, the desired level of TCS functionality, the battlefield C4I connectivity, and the particular type of air vehicle and payloads to be operated depending upon the deployment concept and area of operations. TCS hardware must be scaleable or modular to meet varying Service needs. TCS hardware will permit long range communications from one TCS to another, data storage expansion, access to other computers to share in processing capability, and multiple external peripherals.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: When executing in the Normal Startup Mode, the TCS shall provide the system functionality necessary to initialize the system to place it in the Operations State within 60 seconds from the time power is supplied and the TCS application is launched. [SSS024] Initialization of the TCS HWCIs shall include startup of HWCIs [SSS542], download of software [SSS026], startup of CSCIs [SSS027], execution of Startup FD/L [SSS025], and establishment of the state of readiness of all interfaces. [SSS028] When executing in the Normal Startup Mode, the TCS shall provide the system functionality necessary to initialize the system to place it in the Operations State within 60 seconds from the time power is supplied and the TCS application is launched. [SSS024] Initialization of the TCS HWCIs shall include startup of HWCIs [SSS542], download of software [SSS026], startup of CSCIs [SSS027], execution of Startup FD/L [SSS025], and establishment of the state of readiness of all interfaces. [SSS028]

ground_truth_requirements.text: Upon the selection of a shutdown command the TCS shall enter the Shutdown State, which will cause the TCS to be placed in a condition where power can be removed without impacting operations as well as causing damage to the system, and from which restart of the system can be accomplished normally. [SSS043] Shutdown of the TCS shall include storage as well as deletion, as specified by the operator, of mission data files [SSS044], shutdown of appropriate functions [SSS045], shutdown of HCIs [SSS046], and proper termination of all active interfaces [SSS047]. There shall be no modes of operation in the shutdown state. ground_truth_requirements.text: The TCS shall display the AV status, to include but not be limited to the AV location and flight and avionics system status. ground_truth_requirements.text: The TCS shall have the functionality to process payload product data from Electro Optical (EO), Infrared (IR), and Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) payloads. [SSS182] This functionality, as a minimum, shall include: formatting, storing, internally routing, and recording the video [SSS190]; creating and storing a freeze frame of the video [SSS191]; retrieving and displaying the video [SSS192]; printing a hard copy of freeze frame video [SSS193]; and processing digital imagery for export and dissemination [SSS194]. Payload data includes the digital and analog imagery and associated telemetry sent to the TCS from each of these payloads. The TCS shall be able to store up to 24 hours of payload data. [SSS184] External storage can be utilized for this purpose. The TCS shall be in compliance with Common Imagery Ground Surface Station (CIGSS), United States Imagery Standards (USIS), Video Working Group Standards Architecture, National Imagery Transmission Format (NITF) Version 2.0, and Global Command Control Systems (GCCS) when processing payload imagery data. [SSS185] The NITF 2.0 imagery files generated by the TCS shall contain the necessary telemetry and support data to permit subsequent imagery exploitation by C4I systems. [SSS186] The TCS shall have a built-in text entry capability including the ability to annotate textual information on imagery. [SSS187] The TCS shall be capable of receiving secondary HAE UAV payload imagery. [SSS188] Payload telemetry data shall be available to support other TCS functions as required. ground_truth_requirements.text: The TCS shall provide sufficient cues to allow the operator to safely take-off, land and navigate under Instrument Flight Rules. [SSS347] The TCS shall provide adequate capability to allow the operator to operate each UAV within its certified operational flight envelope. [SSS348] Appropriate cautions and warnings shall be provided to the operator if the air vehicle deviates into unsafe flight regime. [SSS349] For mission planning, the TCS shall provide terrain avoidance warning and minimum reception altitude calculations for line of sight flights. [SSS350] During mission execution, the TCS shall provide the operator a cautions and warnings when the UAV system has identified a malfunction. [SSS351] The TCS shall provide the required information to allow the operator to maintain safe separation from other aircraft and a safe altitude in civilian airspace per Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) rules. [SSS352] The TCS shall be designed such that no single hardware failure results in an unsafe command to be transmitted to the air vehicle. [SSS353] The TCS shall be designed such that no single software error results in an unsafe command to be transmitted to the air vehicle. [SSS556] The TCS shall be capable of restoring power in sufficient time to avoid loss of air vehicle control during power outages. [SSS354] The TCS shall monitor the uplink and downlink to each UAV under its control. [SSS355] Upon detection of loss of link, the TCS shall attempt to reestablish communications with the air vehicle. ground_truth_requirements.text: Using risk assessment procedures defined in DoD 5200.28(D), a risk index and the minimum security requirements for TCS shall be determined. These requirements pertain to the TCS computer hardware and software.
