ground_truth_requirements.text: The TCS software shall provide a high resolution, computer generated, graphical user interface that enables the UAV operator that is trained on one system to control different types of UAVs as well as UAV payloads with minimal additional training. [SSS404] Each control console shall have, at a minimum, the capability to display the following four display windows: (1) display to provide aircraft position, TCS position, flight path, and a waypoint graphics in the foreground which are positioned in relation to a map displayed in the background, (2) display to provide aircraft flight data or payload data in the foreground, and downlinked video in the background. (3)A display to provide graphic presentations of downlinked telemetry data, and (4) display to present the interface menus for workstation software. ground_truth_requirements.text: The TCS reliability will be considered in every phase of the design and development process and shall achieve a system reliability (Mean Time Between Failures MTBF) equal to or greater than that which is specified in the Predator and Outrider ORDs. [SSS410] The TCS reliability will be considered in every phase of the design and development process and shall achieve a system reliability (Mean Time Between Failures MTBF) equal to or greater than that which is specified in the Predator and Outrider ORDs. [SSS410]
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The TCS maintainability will be considered in every phase of the design and development process and shall achieve a system maintainability (Mean Time To Repair MTTR) equal to or greater than that which is specified in the Predator and Outrider ORDs. [SSS411] The Design features shall be included to: [SSS412] The TCS maintainability will be considered in every phase of the design and development process and shall achieve a system maintainability (Mean Time To Repair MTTR) equal to or greater than that which is specified in the Predator and Outrider ORDs. [SSS411] The Design features shall be included to: [SSS412] minimize the number and frequency of required preventive maintenance actions based on performance requirements and lowest life cycle costs; minimize the contribution to degradation of TCS equipment reliability as a consequence of performing either preventive as well as corrective maintenance; enable the performance of all maintenance actions with safety and comparative ease by providing adequate access to all equipment components and minimizing the requirements for special tools and test equipment; minimize the requirement for specially trained maintenance personnel; improve system availability by the effective selection and incorporation of Built In Test Equipment (BITE); allow removal and replacement of replaceable units without soldering and unsoldering.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The TCS equipment shall achieve an availability (Ao), as defined below, equal to or greater than that which is specified in the Predator and Outrider ORDs. [SSS413] Ao = (OT + ST)/(OT + ST + TPM + TCM + TALDT) where: The TCS equipment shall achieve an availability (Ao), as defined below, equal to or greater than that which is specified in the Predator and Outrider ORDs. [SSS413] Ao = (OT + ST)/(OT + ST + TPM + TCM + TALDT) where: OT denotes Operate Time ST denotes Standby Time TPM denotes Total Preventative Maintenance TCM denotes Total Corrective Maintenance TALDT denotes Total Administrative and Logistic Downtime
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The total, fully useable, addressable, physically present program instruction memory and data storage memory for each processor shall have at least 50% unused memory during the Normal Operations Mode over any 10 second period. [SSS414] The processing speed of each processor shall be such that at least 50% of the throughput of each processor remains unused over all 10 second periods and at least 20% of the throughput of each processor remains unused over one second periods regardless of the system function performed. [SSS415] The I/O channel reserve capability for each processor shall have at least a 50% reserve, addressable and useable, I/O channel capacity over any 10 second period. [SSS416] TCS software flexibility and expandability shall be provided through use of the DII COE and through use of standardized software development practices. [SSS417] The total, fully useable, addressable, physically present program instruction memory and data storage memory for each processor shall have at least 50% unused memory during the Normal Operations Mode over any 10 second period. [SSS414] The processing speed of each processor shall be such that at least 50% of the throughput of each processor remains unused over all 10 second periods and at least 20% of the throughput of each processor remains unused over one second periods regardless of the system function performed. [SSS415] The I/O channel reserve capability for each processor shall have at least a 50% reserve, addressable and useable, I/O channel capacity over any 10 second period. [SSS416] TCS software flexibility and expandability shall be provided through use of the DII COE and through use of standardized software development practices. [SSS417]
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Hardware and software shall be selected for use in the TCS with the goal of providing ease of future changes to the TCS elements. [SSS418] The object of portability for the TCS is to select or develop hardware which will readily host emerging software packages and software which will be as independent of host hardware as possible. The selection of processors, interface cards for communication interfaces, disk drives, video, networking equipment, and all other hardware for use in the TCS shall be made according to standards for production of an open architecture. [SSS419] The selection of operating system and programming language for use in the TCS shall be made according to standards for development of an open architecture. [SSS420] Hardware and software shall be selected for use in the TCS with the goal of providing ease of future changes to the TCS elements. [SSS418] The object of portability for the TCS is to select or develop hardware which will readily host emerging software packages and software which will be as independent of host hardware as possible. The selection of processors, interface cards for communication interfaces, disk drives, video, networking equipment, and all other hardware for use in the TCS shall be made according to standards for production of an open architecture. [SSS419] The selection of operating system and programming language for use in the TCS shall be made according to standards for development of an open architecture. [SSS420]
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Testability shall be considered in the design and development of the TCS. [SSS421] The system shall be functionally and physically partitioned to allow for efficient fault isolation. [SSS422] Control over internal items and devices shall be provided for detecting and isolating internal faults. [SSS423] Test points and data paths shall be defined to support efficient fault isolation. [SSS424] Testability shall be considered in the design and development of the TCS. [SSS421] The system shall be functionally and physically partitioned to allow for efficient fault isolation. [SSS422] Control over internal items and devices shall be provided for detecting and isolating internal faults. [SSS423] Test points and data paths shall be defined to support efficient fault isolation. [SSS424]
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: System documentation shall be developed as part of the TCS program and will follow MIL-STD-498 for format. [SSS427] The documentation developed shall contain sufficient level of detail to identify the functional, operational and design requirements of the TCS. [SSS428] The documentation shall contain sufficient technical detail to define the hardware and software design implemented to satisfy the system requirements. [SSS429] The TCS documentation shall include: [SSS430] System documentation shall be developed as part of the TCS program and will follow MIL-STD-498 for format. [SSS427] The documentation developed shall contain sufficient level of detail to identify the functional, operational and design requirements of the TCS. [SSS428] The documentation shall contain sufficient technical detail to define the hardware and software design implemented to satisfy the system requirements. [SSS429] The TCS documentation shall include: [SSS430] The TCS System/Subsystem Specification (SSS) The TCS System/Subsystem Design Document (SSDD) The TCS Software Requirements Specification (SRS) (1 for each CSCI) The Software Design Document (SDD) (1 for each CSCI) The TCS Hardware Design Document (HDD) Interface Design Document (IDD) for all interfaces TCS Version Description Document(s) (VDD)
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: TCS material factors shall be governed by the NDI, GFE and COTS specifications developed by the equipment manufacturers, where applicable. [SSS431] TCS material factors shall be governed by the NDI, GFE and COTS specifications developed by the equipment manufacturers, where applicable. [SSS431]
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: During Phase 1, control techniques to minimize electromagnetic interference, emanation, and susceptibility shall be used in the design of TCS equipment. [SSS432] This control will be inherent in the design of the TCS and the electrical and electronic equipment components and assemblies thereof. The susceptibility to coupling and the propagation of Electromagnetic Interference (EMI) will be minimized by component location, cable routing, and judicious use of shielding. There shall be neither unacceptable response nor malfunction of any TCS and associated equipment due to EMI produced by any as well as all of the TCS and equipment associated with the TCS. [SSS434] The TCS shall be compatible with the external electromagnetic environment that is typical of the service specific environment in the TCS will be operated. [SSS435] The specific electromagnetic environment values will be determined during Phase I of the TCS development. The TCS design shall ensure that personnel, fuel, and ordinance are not exposed to electromagnetic radiation as a result of operating the TCS. [SSS436] The specific radiation hazard (RADHAZ) and HERO values will be determined during Phase I of the TCS development. As TCS transitions into Phase 2, electromagnetic radiation safety and operation specifications will be invoked in the LRIP specification. ground_truth_requirements.text: Newly designed software shall be developed in accordance with a tailored MIL-STD-498. [SSS437] Software written for other systems shall be used in TCS where it is determined that the existing software is suitable for use within the TCS software. [SSS438] A modular architecture shall be used by the TCS software in order to support future interoperability with multiple types of UAVs and payloads while maintaining consistent displays and user interfaces. [SSS439] Software components satisfying common planning and control functions will allow for vehicle specific components to be integrated in the future. Newly designed software shall be developed in accordance with a tailored MIL-STD-498. [SSS437] Software written for other systems shall be used in TCS where it is determined that the existing software is suitable for use within the TCS software. [SSS438] A modular architecture shall be used by the TCS software in order to support future interoperability with multiple types of UAVs and payloads while maintaining consistent displays and user interfaces. [SSS439] Software components satisfying common planning and control functions will allow for vehicle specific components to be integrated in the future.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: TCS hardware flexibility and expansion shall be provided through use of GFE, NDI and COTS hardware designed to be upgraded and expanded. [SSS440] TCS hardware flexibility and expansion shall be provided through use of GFE, NDI and COTS hardware designed to be upgraded and expanded. [SSS440]
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: After emplacement at the operational site, the TCS shall be capable of planning and launching a mission within 1 hour of tasking. [SSS441] Required activities include 1) mission planning of a minimum 1 waypoint mission, 2) preparing 2 AVs for flight, 3) data terminal set-up, 4) safety equipment emplaced, 5) and a single AV launched. After emplacement at the operational site, the TCS shall be capable of planning and launching a mission within 1 hour of tasking. [SSS441] Required activities include 1) mission planning of a minimum 1 waypoint mission, 2) preparing 2 AVs for flight, 3) data terminal set-up, 4) safety equipment emplaced, 5) and a single AV launched.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The TCS shall be capable of operating continuously in functional Operation Mode for a minimum of 72 hours. [SSS442] The TCS shall be capable of operating continuously in functional Operation Mode for a minimum of 72 hours. [SSS442]
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The TCS shall have ergonomically designed operator controls and displays for the 5th percentile female to 95th percentile male operator. [SSS443] The controls shall allow the air vehicle and payload operators to perform mission control, mission monitoring, and mission updates and modifications while wearing cold weather clothing and in a Mission Oriented Protective Posture. [SSS444] The TCS shall provide the operator a caution and warning diagnostic when the TCS system has identified a malfunction. [SSS445] The TCS shall have monitor(s) that allow reading of displays under direct sunlight and low light level environments. [SSS446] The TCS HCI shall be a Graphical User Interface (GUI) based on X-windows and Motif. [SSS447] When performing a given task during mission execution, the operator shall be given appropriate warning messages from other concurrently-executing subsystem tasks. [SSS448] TCS Warning messages shall be color coded and flashed based on mission criticality. The color codes and flash frequencies will follow MIL-STD 1472 guidelines. [SSS449] The TCS operator shall be required to enter an acknowledgment prior to disabling the display of critical warning flags for any AV, Payload, ADT, GDT, and TCS faults. [SSS450] A combination of visual and auditory outputs will be provided to alert the TCS operator to situations which may require operator response. Visual alerts to the TCS operator shall be in the form of a displayed message box that has a display priority greater than other existing windows to ensure that it is viewable immediately by the operator. [SSS452] The position of the displayed message window shall be easily adjustable by the operator to ensure that important mission data is not obscured. [SSS453] In addition to displayed alert messages to the TCS operator, auditory alerts to include tones shall also be provided. [SSS454] The volume of these auditory tones shall be adjustable by the operator via keyboard and trackball input to at least 20dB above the speech interference level at the operator’s ear. [SSS455] All TCS warning messages and HCI actions shall be archived for later review. [SSS456] All TCS operator inputs shall be error checked such that any erroneous operator entry will not cause current processing to terminate. [SSS457] The HCI shall prompt the operator for a valid input. [SSS458] Display jitter and flicker shall not be perceptible by the operator. [SSS459] The operational tasks to be performed concurrently by the operator during normal operation will be determined by appropriate task analysis and function allocation. The TCS shall facilitate Human-Computer Interfaces (HCIs) that support operation of all system modes, functions, and capabilities. [SSS461] The Human Computer Interface (HCI) shall be designed and implemented in accordance with the TCS HCI Specification, TCS 108.[SSS462] The HCI shall provide redundancy in all operations, so that the loss of any one HCI input device does not prohibit operation of any TCS function. [SSS463] The TCS shall provide the functionality to display all HCI elements on any available monitor on the TCS workstation. [SSS464] The TCS shall be capable of displaying a window within a window format to include, as a minimum, displaying a video window overlaid on a map screen as well as a map screen overlaid on a video screen. [SSS465] The TCS shall provide full complementary control operations from the keyset as well as the X/Y control device (e.g., trackball, mouse, joystick). [SSS466] The TCS shall provide access to the DII Style Manager so that pointing device characteristics can be modified. [SSS467] The shall provide the functionality to have a maximum delay time of 1 second from operator command to system acknowledgement and response.[SSS 559] The TCS shall provide a capability for porting an off-the-shelf, complex control joystick with multiple toggle and multi-position switches as part of the TCS hardware suite. [SSS468] The TCS shall use graphical representations to convey information, such as system status, C4I links, and AV-GDT links. [SSS469] The TCS shall provide for multi-level information display tailoring by the operator. [SSS470] The TCS shall provide automated TCS system information, control options, and logical and simple operator guidance and support for immediate and adaptive responding to crisis situations. [SSS471] The TCS shall provide maximum automated system software support to system status monitoring and alerting of the TCS operator when a preset system parameter goes under as well as over a set threshold. [SSS472] The TCS shall provide the necessary processing, display, and control capabilities to ensure dynamic situational awareness input to the TCS operator. [SSS473] The TCS shall minimize alphanumeric data display in favor of graphic, pictorial information display [SSS474] The TCS HCI shall provide unambiguous AV and payload control and status feedback indicators to ensure safe, efficient operations of two AVs and their payloads by a single TCS station. [SSS475] The TCS shall provide for a specific icon shape on a constant contrast background, as well as other visual information coding mechanisms, to cue the TCS operator regarding which UAVs are under his or her primary control. [SSS476] The TCS HCI shall provide the capability to select and amplify an object and point on a map as well as payload screen. [SSS477] The TCS HCI shall provide coarse and fine payload control capabilities directly on the payload screen. [SSS478] The TCS HCI shall display the SAR imaging swath on the map display. [SSS479] The TCS HCI shall provide the on-screen capability to select and efficiently move as well as reorient a previously defined SAR imaging swath. [SSS480] The TCS HCI shall provide the capability to lock onto and hold a coordinate point on the payload imagery window. [SSS481] The TCS HCI shall provide the capability to display operator definable “Lock Out” zones around waypoints, Launch and Recovery Point (LRP), as well as any selected point on the AV flight path. [SSS482] The TCS HCI shall provide for a rapid means to cancel aural warnings. [SSS484] The TCS HCI shall provide for separation, grouping, and visual coding of multiple categories of alerts, to include Warnings, Cautions, and Advisories. [SSS485] The TCS HCI shall provide for visual Warnings, Cautions, and Advisories to be displayed at or near the center of the field of view, i.e., within a 30o cone, of all monitors in a TCS system. [SSS486] For AV safety as well as mission-critical Warnings, the TCS HCI shall provide a default selection as well as an override option, along with a selection of adaptive responses, and the minimum information necessary to assist the operator in responding quickly and adaptively to the emergency. [SSS487] The TCS HCI shall provide for on-screen information to include, as a minimum, overlays, headers, cursors, alphanumeric annotation, waypoints, crosshairs, designed to be visible against the complete spectrum of map and payload video backgrounds. [SSS488] The TCS HCI shall provide continuously-available, on-screen control functions for time and missioncritical operations, to include as a minimum print, freeze, declassification, mark VCR, declutter, cease RF transmission. [SSS489] The TCS HCI shall provide for the capability to automatically designate target locations from the payload screen onto the map screen. ground_truth_requirements.text: Formal training programs shall not be required for TCS Phase 1, Program definition and Risk Reduction. Trained and proficient personnel from the Original Equipment Manufacturers, Government Engineering Teams, supporting Contractors, and Military Personnel will support the operation and maintenance of the demonstration system equipment throughout Phase 1. TCS training and training support shall include the processes, procedures, techniques, training devices and equipment to train civilian, active duty and reserve military personnel to operate and support the TCS system. [SSS491] This will include: individual and crew training; new equipment training; initial, formal, and on-the-job training. TCS training will strike a balance between institutional, new equipment and unit training. The TCS system shall provide, for the operator and maintainer, an embedded or add-on interactive training courseware with self-paced instruction, duplicating UAV flight performance characteristics, capabilities, and limitations. [SSS492] The OUTRIDER TCS system shall be compatible with the U.S. Army Intelligence and Electronic Warfare Tactical Proficiency Trainer as an objective. [SSS493] The interactive courseware training capability for TCS shall be developed during Phase I and introduced to the user during scheduled demonstrations and tests. [SSS494] The training capability for performance of TCS functions shall include primary mission (flight route/payload) planning, mission control and monitoring, imagery processing, tactical communications, AV control communications and TCS system on line diagnostics. [SSS495] This capability will be qualified and improved during Phase I based upon use and feedback from qualified operators and users demonstrating TCS system equipment throughout Phase I. Instructional support materials and training courseware for classroom discussion and lecture will be developed to support institutional, new equipment training and unit training. The TCS shall provide the functional capability to train in the operation of the TCS system, performance of TCS UAV functions, and on line system troubleshooting. [SSS496] TCS system training shall include system architecture, component familiarization, and system startup, initialization, system recovery and shutdown. [SSS497] The TCS system shall not support formal Training operations concurrent with the execution of an actual mission. [SSS499] The capability for the conduct of actual communications processing concurrently with Training operations shall be provided if and only if messages are identified as training messages. [SSS500] Training shall be adequate to maintain operator and maintainer skills and proficiencies. [SSS501] TCS shall record operator and maintainer actions for self assessment and performance enhancement. [SSS502] Operator and maintainer performance shall be measurable using parameters retrievable from the TCS to determine proficiency levels. [SSS503] Formal training programs shall not be required for TCS Phase 1, Program definition and Risk Reduction. ground_truth_requirements.text: The TCS shall be ground transportable. [SSS521] The TCS shall be ground transportable. [SSS521]
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The TCS shall be air transportable by helicopter (CH-47/CH-53D) and C-130 drive-on/drive-off capable. [SSS522] The TCS shall be air transportable by helicopter (CH-47/CH-53D) and C-130 drive-on/drive-off capable. [SSS522]
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The TCS shall be sea transportable. [SSS523] The TCS shall be sea transportable. [SSS523]
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The TCS shall be rail transportable. [SSS524] The TCS shall be rail transportable. [SSS524]

ground_truth_requirements.text: The TCS shall be configurable for sea, ground, as well as air transport in 2 hours or less. [SSS525] The TCS system shall be capable of being de-configured from sea, ground, as well as air transport and ground-mobile in 2 hours or less. [SSS526] The TCS shall be configurable for sea, ground, as well as air transport in 2 hours or less. [SSS525] The TCS system shall be capable of being de-configured from sea, ground, as well as air transport and ground-mobile in 2 hours or less. [SSS526]

ground_truth_requirements.text: All requirements in this specification are of equal weight and criticality unless otherwise identified in the traceability Table in Appendix A. When the requirements of the Operational Requirement Document (ORD), this System/Subsystem Specification (SSS), other related requirement documents, and referenced documents are in conflict, the following precedence will apply: All requirements in this specification are of equal weight and criticality unless otherwise identified in the traceability Table in Appendix A. When the requirements of the Operational Requirement Document (ORD), this System/Subsystem Specification (SSS), other related requirement documents, and referenced documents are in conflict, the following precedence will apply: In the event of conflicting requirements within a TCS requirement document, the requirements shall be traced to the next higher precedence requirement document for clarification. If further resolution is required, the developer will notify the procuring agency. TCS ORD – The ORD shall have precedence over any other TCS documentation. TCS SSS – This SSS shall have precedence over other requirements documents except item (1.) above. Other TCS requirement documents – Other requirement documents shall have precedence over any referenced documents. Referenced documents – Documents referenced herein and in other TCS requirement documents shall have precedence over all applicable subsidiary documents referenced therein.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Quality Assurance (QA) Program is an integral part of the development process for the TCS , whereby all phases of the development of a system must be inspected and tested, as these phases occur. The objectives and processes of the QA Program applicable to this development effort are contained in the TCS Integration Program Quality Assurance Plan, NSWCDD/TR-96/XXX. The Quality Assurance (QA) Program is an integral part of the development process for the TCS , whereby all phases of the development of a system must be inspected and tested, as these phases occur. The objectives and processes of the QA Program applicable to this development effort are contained in the TCS Integration Program Quality Assurance Plan, NSWCDD/TR-96/XXX.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

generated_requirements.text: The application shall be implemented exclusively using the Qt graphical framework (a mutually agreed-upon Qt LTS release), and a single codebase shall compile and run natively on all desktop operating systems officially supported by that Qt release, including Windows, macOS, and Linux.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-1, TC-3, and BO-1 mandate a single Qt-based codebase targeting all Qt-supported desktop platforms to avoid separate per-platform builds.

generated_requirements.text: The specific Qt LTS version selected for the project shall be explicitly documented as a project constraint prior to development commencement, and the resulting minimum supported OS versions (Windows, macOS, Linux distributions) shall be recorded in the SRS and used to define the test matrix.
generated_requirements.rationale: The Qt LTS version locks the minimum supported OS versions and directly determines the deployment and test environment scope.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall present a main window containing, at minimum, a game board area and a menu bar, and this layout shall be visible immediately upon application launch without requiring any additional user configuration.
generated_requirements.rationale: BO-5 and the Future State description require a clearly structured main window with a game board and menu bar to provide immediate player orientation.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall implement the complete logic of the agreed single-player puzzle game (type and win condition to be confirmed with the project sponsor per A-1 and D-3), including all valid move rules, illegal-move rejection, and the exact board state or logical condition that constitutes a solved puzzle.
generated_requirements.rationale: The win-condition definition is the gating trigger for score recording, timer stop, and undo stack freezing, and must be unambiguous before any scoring or history logic can be implemented.

generated_requirements.text: Upon detection of the win condition, the application shall immediately stop the play timer, freeze the undo/redo stack to prevent further moves, and notify the player that the puzzle has been solved before proceeding to score capture.
generated_requirements.rationale: Win-condition detection must explicitly gate timer stop, undo stack freezing, and score recording to ensure accurate and consistent session metrics.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall display a real-time elapsed-time counter that is visible to the player throughout the entire play session; the counter shall start when the player begins a new game and stop when the puzzle is solved or the session is otherwise terminated.
generated_requirements.rationale: BO-2 and BR-5 require a live timer that starts at game begin and stops at session end to provide continuous performance feedback.

generated_requirements.text: The elapsed-time display shall be accurate to within ±1 second of actual elapsed wall-clock time; the implementation shall use QElapsedTimer with a high-resolution clock source rather than QTimer alone to meet this accuracy requirement across all supported platforms.
generated_requirements.rationale: The KPI table mandates ±1 second timer accuracy, and QTimer's OS-dependent resolution (historically ~15 ms on Windows) makes QElapsedTimer mandatory for reliable cross-platform accuracy.

generated_requirements.text: The elapsed-time display shall show time at a granularity of whole seconds (HH:MM:SS or equivalent), and the display shall refresh at least once per second during an active play session.
generated_requirements.rationale: Specifying display granularity and refresh rate ensures the timer is meaningful to the player and consistent with the ±1 second accuracy KPI.

generated_requirements.text: The play timer shall be suspended whenever the application window is minimised, the application loses operating-system focus, or an OS-level screensaver activates; the timer shall resume automatically when the application window is restored to the foreground.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-5 hints at pause semantics and the domain expert identifies focus-loss timer suspension as a critical underspecified rule that directly affects timer accuracy and fairness.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall display a real-time player-action counter that is visible to the player throughout the entire play session; the counter shall increment by exactly one for each discrete board-level player action (e.g., each tile move or peg jump) and shall start at zero when a new game begins.
generated_requirements.rationale: BO-2, BR-3, and BR-4 require a live move counter that accurately reflects every discrete player action with zero missed increments.

generated_requirements.text: The treatment of undo and redo operations with respect to the move counter (i.e., whether each undo or redo increments the counter) shall be decided, documented, and consistently applied throughout the application; this decision shall be confirmed with the product owner before development of the scoring or history logic begins.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-4 and A-8 explicitly flag this as an open decision that must be resolved to ensure a consistent and unambiguous counting rule.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall provide an undo function that reverses the most recent discrete player action, restoring the game board and all associated state to the condition it was in immediately before that action was taken.
generated_requirements.rationale: BO-4 requires a robust undo capability so players can experiment with strategies without penalty.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall provide a redo function that re-applies the most recently undone action; the redo history shall be invalidated and cleared whenever the player performs a new board-level action after having undone one or more actions.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-1 requires redo history invalidation on new action, which is standard undo/redo stack semantics and must be explicitly specified.

generated_requirements.text: The undo/redo history stack shall support a minimum depth of 1,000 consecutive player actions per session; when the stack reaches its 1,000-action capacity, the oldest entry shall be evicted (FIFO) to accommodate the newest action without error or data loss.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-1 and the KPI table mandate a minimum undo/redo depth of 1,000 actions with FIFO eviction at the boundary.

generated_requirements.text: The undo/redo stack shall be maintained entirely in application memory during a session and shall not be persisted to disk; the stack shall be cleared and reset to empty when a new game session begins.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-5 and BR-1 scope undo/redo history to the current session only, with no cross-session persistence required.

generated_requirements.text: The undo and redo functions shall be accessible via the application menu bar and via standard keyboard shortcuts (Ctrl+Z for undo, Ctrl+Y or Ctrl+Shift+Z for redo); the corresponding menu items and any toolbar buttons shall be visually disabled (greyed out) when the respective stack is empty.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert identifies undo/redo UI affordances and state communication as a required pattern to ensure players know when undo/redo is available or exhausted.

generated_requirements.text: Upon completion of a puzzle session (win condition met), the application shall prompt the player to enter a display name; the prompt shall not accept an empty or whitespace-only name, and shall require the player to provide a valid non-empty name before the score record is saved.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-2 and BR-6 require that a score is only recorded when the puzzle is solved and a non-empty player name is provided.

generated_requirements.text: The player name entry prompt shall specify whether the name is collected at the start of a new game session, at the end upon puzzle completion, or persisted from a previous session; this timing decision shall be confirmed with the product owner during elicitation and documented in the SRS.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert identifies player name entry timing as an underspecified decision that affects the entire session flow and must be explicitly resolved.

generated_requirements.text: Upon saving a score record, the application shall persist, at minimum, the following fields: player display name, total elapsed play time (in whole seconds), and total player action count for the session; a score record missing any of these three fields shall be considered invalid and must not be written to storage.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-3 mandates that every score record contains player name, elapsed time, and action count as mandatory fields.

generated_requirements.text: Score data shall be persisted to a flat file (JSON or XML format using Qt's built-in APIs) that includes a schema version field; the file shall be stored in the platform-appropriate user-writable directory resolved at runtime using Qt's QStandardPaths::AppDataLocation API (e.g., %APPDATA% on Windows, ~/.local/share on Linux, ~/Library/Application Support on macOS).
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-4, OC-2, and the domain expert mandate flat-file storage in a platform-conventional user-writable path resolved via QStandardPaths to avoid privilege failures and hardcoded paths.

generated_requirements.text: The score data file format shall include a schema version field, and the application shall be capable of detecting a version mismatch on file load and handling it gracefully (e.g., by migrating the data or displaying an informative message) to support forward-compatible evolution of the score record structure.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert requires a schema version field in the flat-file format to allow forward-compatible migration if the score record structure changes in future releases.

generated_requirements.text: Score data shall survive application restarts; 100% of completed play sessions in which the player provides a valid name shall result in a persisted score entry, and zero score records shall be lost on normal application exit.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-8 and the KPI table require 100% score persistence on normal exit with zero data loss.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall use a defensive write strategy for score file updates (e.g., write to a temporary file and atomically rename it to replace the target file) to prevent score data corruption in the event of abnormal application termination mid-write.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert identifies flat-file persistence without transactional guarantees as a real corruption risk on abnormal exit that must be mitigated through defensive write strategies.

generated_requirements.text: If the score data file is found to be corrupt or unparseable on application load, the application shall detect this condition, log or display an informative error message, and continue to operate (starting with an empty scoreboard) rather than crashing or refusing to launch.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert and elicitation strategy require graceful handling of corrupt score files as an edge case that must be explicitly addressed.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall provide a scoreboard window that displays all recorded score entries across all players and all sessions stored on the machine; no score entry shall be hidden or filtered from the default scoreboard view without an explicit player action.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-6 and BO-3 require a scoreboard that shows all recorded scores for all players to foster competition and motivation.

generated_requirements.text: The scoreboard window shall be accessible from the main menu within a single user interaction (one click or one menu selection) from the main application window.
generated_requirements.rationale: The KPI table mandates that the scoreboard is reachable within one user interaction from the main menu.

generated_requirements.text: The scoreboard shall display, for each entry, at minimum: the player's display name, the total elapsed play time, and the total action count; the default sort order (e.g., by elapsed time ascending, by action count, or by insertion order) and sort direction shall be confirmed with the product owner during elicitation and documented in the SRS.
generated_requirements.rationale: A-7 defers sort order to elicitation, and the domain expert identifies default sort key and direction as consistently underspecified items that generate rework if left open.

generated_requirements.text: The scoreboard shall display all entries regardless of player name duplication; the system shall not enforce unique player names, and multiple score entries under the same name shall all be visible in the scoreboard.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-7 explicitly states that player name uniqueness is not enforced and all entries must be displayed.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall provide a 'New Game' function accessible from the menu bar; if a game session is currently in progress (timer running or moves made), the application shall display a confirmation dialog warning the player that the current session will be abandoned and its score will not be recorded before proceeding.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert identifies the new-game/restart flow and its effect on in-progress state as a required pattern to prevent accidental session abandonment.

generated_requirements.text: When a new game session begins (whether at first launch or after a confirmed restart), the application shall reset the game board to its initial state, reset the elapsed-time counter to zero, reset the action counter to zero, and clear the undo/redo stack.
generated_requirements.rationale: All session-scoped state (board, timer, move counter, undo stack) must be fully reset at the start of each new game to ensure accurate and independent session metrics.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall provide visual feedback for illegal or invalid player moves (e.g., a visual highlight, colour flash, or shake animation on the affected board element) so that the player is immediately informed that the attempted action was rejected without altering game state.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert identifies illegal-move rejection feedback as a required visual feedback contract that drives significant UI implementation work if left unspecified.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall provide visual feedback for valid player moves (e.g., an animated transition of the moved tile or piece to its new position) to confirm that the action was accepted and to communicate the resulting board state clearly.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert identifies animated move transitions as part of the visual feedback contract that is consistently omitted from BRDs but drives significant UI work.

generated_requirements.text: The game board rendering shall scale correctly with window resizing or shall operate at a defined fixed size; the chosen approach (scalable vs. fixed) shall be documented in the SRS, and the board shall render without distortion or clipping on all supported screen resolutions.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert identifies board rendering and scalability as a requirement that affects maintainability and the experience on high-DPI displays.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall be DPI-aware on all supported platforms; on Windows, the application manifest shall declare DPI awareness, and appropriate Qt high-DPI scaling attributes shall be set at application startup so that the game board and all UI elements render sharply on high-DPI and 4K/Retina displays.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert identifies high-DPI handling as a cross-platform portability concern that causes blurry or incorrectly scaled rendering if not explicitly addressed in both the Windows manifest and Qt attribute flags.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall run without requiring administrator or root privileges on any supported operating system, including installation, execution, and all score file read/write operations.
generated_requirements.rationale: OC-1 mandates that the application operates without elevated privileges to ensure compatibility with managed and multi-user environments.

generated_requirements.text: Score data files shall be stored exclusively in a user-writable, per-user directory resolved at runtime via Qt's QStandardPaths API; the application shall never hardcode an absolute file system path for score data storage.
generated_requirements.rationale: OC-2 and the domain expert require platform-conventional user-writable paths resolved via QStandardPaths to prevent privilege failures in managed multi-user environments.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall not require a database server; all score data persistence shall use a flat-file mechanism (JSON or XML) implemented via Qt's built-in file and serialisation APIs.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-4 prohibits database server dependencies to keep the application self-contained and deployable without infrastructure prerequisites.

generated_requirements.text: A new player shall be able to launch the application, start a game, understand the elapsed-time and move-count displays, and access the scoreboard within 2 minutes of first launch, without consulting any documentation or help file.
generated_requirements.rationale: BO-5 and the KPI table set a 2-minute onboarding target for new players with no documentation, requiring an intuitive, self-explanatory interface.

generated_requirements.text: All primary game functions (new game, undo, redo, view scoreboard, quit) shall be accessible via the menu bar; menu item labels shall be self-explanatory and consistent with platform UI conventions on each supported operating system.
generated_requirements.rationale: BO-5 requires a clean, menu-driven graphical interface that is intuitive without a manual, necessitating clear and conventionally labelled menu items.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall launch and reach a playable state (main window displayed with game board ready) within 5 seconds on a machine meeting the minimum hardware specification defined in the SRS.
generated_requirements.rationale: A lightweight desktop puzzle game must start quickly to meet the expectation of a self-contained, responsive application and support the 2-minute onboarding KPI.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall respond to all player board interactions (move attempts, undo, redo) within 100 milliseconds of the input event, ensuring the UI does not feel sluggish during gameplay.
generated_requirements.rationale: Responsive interaction is essential for a puzzle game experience; perceptible lag on move or undo actions would degrade player satisfaction and contradict the lightweight application objective.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall be distributed in a manner consistent with Qt LGPL licensing obligations; the chosen distribution model (dynamic linking with bundled Qt DLLs/shared libraries, or static linking with source availability) shall be confirmed with the project sponsor before architecture is finalised.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert identifies Qt LGPL licensing as a constraint requiring an explicit distribution model decision, as static linking triggers copyleft obligations.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall be implemented from a single shared codebase with no platform-specific conditional compilation blocks for core game logic; platform-specific code, if any, shall be isolated to clearly identified modules (e.g., file path resolution, DPI manifest) and documented.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-3 and BO-1 require a single codebase to avoid separate per-platform builds and reduce maintenance overhead.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall handle abnormal termination (e.g., OS kill signal, power loss mid-write) without permanently corrupting the score data file; on next launch after an abnormal exit, the application shall load the last successfully committed score data without error.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert and elicitation strategy require graceful handling of abnormal termination as a data integrity edge case for flat-file persistence.

generated_requirements.text: An incomplete or abandoned play session (puzzle not solved, or player dismisses the name entry prompt) shall not generate or persist any score record; only fully completed sessions with a valid player name shall result in a stored score entry.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-2 explicitly prohibits score recording for incomplete or abandoned sessions to maintain scoreboard integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall not collect, transmit, or store any personal data beyond the player-supplied display name; no network communication of any kind shall be initiated by the application.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD's regulatory section and the out-of-scope definition (no online features) require that the application is fully offline and collects no personal data beyond the display name.

generated_requirements.text: The move counter displayed during gameplay shall reflect every discrete player board action with zero missed increments; the counter value recorded in the score entry at session completion shall exactly match the total number of qualifying actions taken during that session.
generated_requirements.rationale: The KPI table mandates zero missed move counter increments, making counter accuracy a reliability requirement for score integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The scoreboard window shall remain accessible and openable at any point during a play session (including while a game is in progress) without interrupting or pausing the active game timer.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-6 states the scoreboard must be accessible at any time; however, the timer pause rule (R009) applies only to window minimisation/focus loss, so viewing the scoreboard as a child window must not inadvertently trigger a timer pause.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall provide a 'Quit' or 'Exit' function accessible from the menu bar; if a game session is in progress, the application shall display a confirmation dialog before exiting, and shall ensure all previously completed score records are fully persisted to disk before the process terminates.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-8 requires score data to survive application restarts, and the domain expert identifies abnormal exit as a data integrity risk requiring a confirmed-exit flow with guaranteed flush of persisted data.

generated_requirements.text: The application's source code shall be structured and documented such that the puzzle game type can be replaced or extended (e.g., swapping game rules or board layout) with changes confined to a defined game-logic module, without requiring modifications to the scoring, timer, undo/redo, or persistence subsystems.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD notes the puzzle type is still being confirmed and the elicitation strategy anticipates potential scope evolution; modular separation of game logic from infrastructure subsystems reduces future rework risk.

sme_advisory: For qheadache, the requirements engineer should pay close attention to three intersecting concerns: (1) Qt-specific cross-platform behavioural divergence — particularly around file system paths, timer resolution, and widget rendering — which can silently violate portability and accuracy requirements if not explicitly called out; (2) the puzzle-domain interaction model, where the precise definition of a "discrete player action," the win-condition detection trigger, and the undo/redo stack eviction policy must be unambiguous before any scoring or history logic can be correctly specified; and (3) score data integrity at the file level, since a flat-file persistence mechanism without transactional guarantees introduces real risk of corruption on abnormal exit, which must be addressed through defensive write strategies. The engineer should also probe stakeholders on scoreboard sort order, timer pause semantics, and the visual feedback contract (e.g., animations, highlights) as these are consistently underspecified in early-stage puzzle game BRDs and generate significant rework if left open.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product uses the Qt graphical library. It must run with all the operating systems that Qt supports. The product uses the Qt graphical library. It must run with all the operating systems that Qt supports.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product must count and display the number of the user' asction. The product must count and display the number of the user' asction.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product must record the score (time and number of counts) of a play associated with the name of a user. The product must record the score (time and number of counts) of a play associated with the name of a user.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The software must be portable to the Windows OS. The software must be portable to the Windows OS.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall assign a unique Event ID to each health event within a jurisdiction at the time of record creation, and shall reject any attempt to create a duplicate Event ID within the same jurisdiction.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.1 and KPI table mandate 100% uniqueness of Event IDs within a jurisdiction as an enforceable constraint.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall assign a unique Specimen ID to each specimen collected within a jurisdiction at the time of record creation, and shall reject any attempt to create a duplicate Specimen ID within the same jurisdiction.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.1 and KPI table mandate 100% uniqueness of Specimen IDs within a jurisdiction as an enforceable constraint.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall assign a unique Subject ID to every entity (person, animal, organization, location, object, conveyance, gathering, or other living thing) involved in an investigation, and shall link that Subject ID to all associated specimens, laboratory results, epidemiological data, and countermeasure records for that entity.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.1 requires a single Subject ID to serve as the universal linkage key across all investigation data for an entity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall capture and store separately, for every case and associated investigation record, both the investigating jurisdiction (where the health event is managed) and the reporting jurisdiction (the case's jurisdiction of residence), preserving both values even when they differ.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.2 explicitly requires dual jurisdiction capture to support cross-jurisdictional investigations and data sovereignty.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect when an entity appears in both a case investigation context and an exposure investigation context, present the investigator with the existing demographic record for confirmation, and prevent creation of a duplicate demographic record, enforcing a single demographic record that serves both contexts.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.4 and the KPI of zero duplicate demographic captures require system-enforced deduplication across case and exposure records.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce a many-to-many relationship between contacts and cases, allowing a single contact to be linked to multiple case records and a single case to be linked to multiple contact records simultaneously.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.5 explicitly mandates many-to-many case-to-contact linkage as a core contact tracing data model requirement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall require that every entity exposed to a case be formally recorded and linked to that case record before the exposure record can be saved.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.5 states that all entities exposed to a case must be recorded and formally linked to that case record.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support updating a case's diagnosis classification either manually by an authorized user or automatically when the event's case definition changes, while preserving all prior classification values and their timestamps in an immutable historical record.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.3 requires both manual and automatic reclassification with full historical preservation for audit purposes.

generated_requirements.text: When a case definition changes mid-investigation, the system shall support bulk automated reclassification of all affected case records as well as individual manual override of any single case's classification, with each prior classification preserved in the historical record and not overwritten.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert advisory identifies configurable case definition and automatic reclassification workflow as a required requirement pattern.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall link laboratory results to the specimen collection record via Specimen ID when that record exists; when no specimen collection record exists, the system shall link laboratory results directly to the subject via Subject ID.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.6 defines the conditional linkage logic for laboratory results to ensure no result is orphaned.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support multi-level parent-child specimen derivation chains (e.g., original swab → aliquot → sub-aliquot), propagating subject linkage and chain-of-custody context through all derived specimens, and shall make laboratory results for any child specimen traceable back to the root parent specimen record.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.6 and the domain expert advisory require full multi-level specimen lineage traceability, not just immediate parent linkage.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall store laboratory results at all levels of granularity, from specimen-level aggregate results down to individual assay-level results, and shall associate each result record with the appropriate specimen or sub-specimen record.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.6 explicitly requires support for all levels of result granularity from specimen to individual assay.

generated_requirements.text: For forensic and select-agent specimens, the system shall maintain a complete, immutable chain-of-custody record documenting: the identity of the collector, the collection location, the identity of every individual who handled the sample during preparation for shipment, and confirmation of acceptance by the shipper.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.7 and 42 CFR Part 73 (Federal Select Agent Program) impose strict chain-of-custody documentation requirements for forensic and select-agent samples.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall require that every countermeasure administration record (vaccination, prophylaxis, or treatment) be linked to the specific entity who received it before the record can be saved, and shall prevent the creation of unlinked, free-standing countermeasure records.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.8 mandates that countermeasure records may not exist without linkage to a recipient entity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restrict creation, revision, and publication of investigation-specific questionnaires to authorized administrators only, and shall maintain version control so that the specific questionnaire version active at any point during an investigation can be identified and retrieved.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.9 requires role-restricted questionnaire governance with version control for audit and retrospective analysis integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall record, for each questionnaire response collected, the version identifier of the questionnaire that was active at the time the response was captured, creating an investigation-point-in-time snapshot that is not altered by subsequent questionnaire revisions.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert advisory requires questionnaire versioning with point-in-time snapshots to prevent retrospective analysis corruption.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support recording a tag-based Subject ID for animals involved in investigations, in addition to any standard demographic identifiers, to enable unambiguous animal identification.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.11 recognizes that standard demographic identifiers are insufficient for animals and requires tag-based Subject ID support.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall include all data exchanges with partner systems with clearly defined mapping interfaces and data dictionaries that describe both standard fields and event-specific custom fields, and shall explicitly identify custom fields as such in all outbound messages.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.10 requires that all data exchanges include mapping interfaces and data dictionaries with explicit custom field identification.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enable authorized administrators to add custom data fields, entity types, entity relationship types, and event-specific data structures without requiring any software code changes or vendor involvement, and this configuration capability must be available within 4 hours of a new health event being declared.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 2.2 KPI and Constraint 7.3 set a hard 4-hour, no-code configuration target as a contractual and operational requirement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support structured data capture for all entity types involved in an investigation, including persons, organizations, locations, animals, objects, conveyances, public gatherings, private gatherings, and other living things, with formal relationship linkage between any two entities of any supported type.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 scope defines comprehensive entity management across all listed entity types with formal relationship linkage as in-scope.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall capture exposure-level detail for each exposure record, including at minimum: exposure type, exposure place, exposure duration, exposure frequency, and proximity of the exposed entity to the source.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 scope explicitly lists exposure type, place, duration, frequency, and proximity as required exposure investigation data elements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall capture travel history for entities involved in an investigation and formally link travel history records to the entity's Subject ID.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 scope includes travel history recording as part of the exposure investigation functional area.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall capture epidemiological case data including at minimum: symptom onset date, symptom types, risk factors, medical history, laboratory data, procedure data, and questionnaire responses, and shall support dynamic event-specific case data fields configured by administrators.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 scope enumerates the required epidemiological data elements for case investigation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support electronic creation and transmission of laboratory test requests to laboratory information systems using PHIN-compliant message formats as defined in the applicable PHIN message implementation guides, and shall achieve 100% electronic transmission of all lab test requests.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 2.2 KPI designates 100% electronic lab test request transmission as a mandatory PHIN Preparedness Key Performance Measure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall electronically transmit case notifications for confirmed, probable, and suspect cases to surveillance and Early Event Detection (EED) systems using PHIN-compliant message formats, achieving 100% electronic exchange of all such notifications.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 2.2 KPI designates 100% electronic case notification exchange as a mandatory PHIN Preparedness Key Performance Measure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall electronically transmit countermeasure administration requests to partner systems using PHIN-compliant message formats, achieving 100% electronic transmission of all countermeasure administration requests.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 2.2 KPI designates 100% electronic countermeasure administration request transmission as a mandatory PHIN Preparedness Key Performance Measure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support bidirectional electronic exchange of investigation data, exposure contact records, and aggregated data with partner OM systems in other jurisdictions using PHIN-compliant message formats, including both inbound receipt and outbound transmission.
generated_requirements.rationale: Constraint 7.2 requires the system to support inbound as well as outbound message exchange with partner OM systems.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall process functional acknowledgments (ACKs and NACKs) received from partner systems in response to all outbound PHIN messages, implement configurable retry logic for messages that receive a NACK or no acknowledgment within a defined timeout period, and generate alerts to administrators for any message delivery failure.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert advisory requires bidirectional HL7/PHIN message acknowledgment handling with retry and alerting to satisfy 100% electronic exchange KPIs.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide built-in compliance reporting that demonstrates, for each PHIN Preparedness Key Performance Measure, the percentage of transactions transmitted electronically, enabling jurisdictions to produce evidence of compliance on demand.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert advisory states that PHIN KPIs are mandatory compliance targets requiring demonstrable measurement, not aspirational goals.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall bind all structured data fields (including symptom types, exposure types, organism codes, and conveyance types) to CDC/PHIN-approved standard code sets (e.g., SNOMED CT, LOINC, HL7 value sets), and shall support updates to these vocabularies without requiring software code changes.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert advisory identifies vocabulary governance as a constraint requiring code-change-free vocabulary updates due to regular SNOMED CT and LOINC release cycles.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support generation and printing of event-specific questionnaires in a format suitable for both in-person field use and telephone interviews, and shall provide a corresponding structured data entry screen to allow field-collected paper data to be entered upon return to network connectivity.
generated_requirements.rationale: Constraint 7.6 requires offline/printable questionnaire support for field investigations, and the domain expert advisory requires a reconciliation workflow for paper-collected data.

generated_requirements.text: When field-collected paper data is entered into the system after return to connectivity, the system shall perform duplicate detection against any records already entered electronically for the same entity and alert the data entry user to potential duplicates before saving.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert advisory requires duplicate detection during offline data reconciliation to maintain the zero-duplicate-demographic-capture KPI.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide real-time aggregate dashboards displaying at minimum: case counts, contact counts, and vaccination/treatment tallies per event, accessible to state epidemiologists and authorized public health officers.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 scope includes data aggregation, and the domain expert advisory identifies real-time aggregate dashboards as a required reporting pattern for state epidemiologists.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support export of line-list data for any event in standard formats including at minimum CSV, HL7, and XML, for use in after-action reporting and CDC submission.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert advisory identifies exportable line-list data in standard formats as a required reporting pattern for PHIN program offices and after-action reporting.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support batch shipment tracking for specimen collections, recording the contents of each shipment batch, the shipper identity, the destination laboratory, and the shipment date and time.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 scope includes batch shipment tracking as part of the specimen and laboratory management functional area.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement role-based access control (RBAC) that restricts each user's ability to read, create, update, and delete records to only those functions authorized for their assigned role, and shall enforce jurisdiction-scoped data visibility so that users in one jurisdiction cannot read or modify records belonging to another jurisdiction unless an explicit data-sharing agreement is in effect.
generated_requirements.rationale: HIPAA Security Rule, PHIN Cross Functional Components Requirements, and jurisdiction data sovereignty laws require RBAC with data-layer jurisdiction scoping, not merely UI-layer controls.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce access controls for select-agent specimen records that satisfy the requirements of 42 CFR Part 73 (Federal Select Agent Program), including restricting access to only personnel with documented authorization and logging all access attempts.
generated_requirements.rationale: 42 CFR Part 73 imposes strict access control requirements for select-agent specimens that go beyond general RBAC and must be explicitly satisfied.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain an immutable audit trail for all create, read, update, and delete operations on sensitive records (including cases, specimens, chain-of-custody records, and countermeasure records), capturing for each operation: the user identity, timestamp, the previous value of any changed field, and the new value.
generated_requirements.rationale: HIPAA Security Rule compliance and forensic chain-of-custody defensibility require a complete, tamper-proof audit trail with field-level change history.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall encrypt all individually identifiable health information (IIHI) at rest using a FIPS 140-2 validated encryption mechanism and in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher, in compliance with the HIPAA Security Rule technical safeguard requirements.
generated_requirements.rationale: HIPAA Security Rule (45 CFR Part 164) requires technical safeguards including encryption at rest and in transit for all IIHI processed by the system.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement authentication mechanisms that meet the strength requirements defined in the PHIN Cross Functional Components Requirements, including multi-factor authentication for remote access, and shall enforce session timeout and re-authentication policies consistent with those requirements.
generated_requirements.rationale: The PHIN Cross Functional Components Requirements define binding authentication and session management standards for all PHIN-connected systems.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve a minimum annual uptime of 99.9%, measured as the percentage of total scheduled operating hours during which the system is available to authenticated users.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 2.2 KPI table sets 99.9% annual uptime as a hard, measurable availability target.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall remain available or be fully recoverable subsequent to a catastrophic event (including natural disaster or cyberattack) through redundant infrastructure, geographic failover capability, and tested disaster recovery procedures consistent with PHIN Cross Functional Components Requirements, with a Recovery Time Objective (RTO) and Recovery Point Objective (RPO) defined and documented prior to production deployment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Constraint 7.5 and the PHIN Cross Functional Components Requirements mandate post-catastrophic-event availability with redundant infrastructure and tested recovery procedures.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enable an authorized administrator to configure a new health event — including creation of event-specific custom fields, entity types, relationship types, and questionnaires — within 4 hours of event declaration, without requiring any software code changes or vendor involvement.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 2.2 KPI and Constraint 7.3 set a hard 4-hour no-code configuration target as a contractual preparedness performance requirement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support updates to standard public health vocabularies and code sets (e.g., SNOMED CT, LOINC, HL7 value sets) through an administrative configuration interface without requiring software code changes, and shall apply updated code sets to new records while preserving the code set version used for existing records.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert advisory identifies vocabulary governance as requiring code-change-free updates due to regular versioned releases of SNOMED CT and LOINC.

generated_requirements.text: All inter-system message exchanges shall conform strictly to the applicable PHIN message implementation guides, including mandatory message structures, vocabulary bindings, and transport protocols, with no use of proprietary message formats for cross-jurisdictional or cross-system exchange.
generated_requirements.rationale: Constraint 7.2 prohibits proprietary message formats and the domain expert advisory states that deviation from PHIN guides constitutes a preparedness compliance failure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide data entry interfaces that allow a field epidemiologist to create a complete case record — including entity demographics, exposure linkage, and specimen collection — within a single, unified workflow without navigating to separate systems or re-entering demographic data already present in the system.
generated_requirements.rationale: The field epidemiologist stakeholder's primary need is rapid, intuitive data entry, and the deduplication business rule requires seamless reuse of existing demographic data within the workflow.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce that 0% of common data elements require re-mapping when a new health event is declared, by maintaining a standard core data model that is reused across all events without modification, supplemented only by event-specific custom fields.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 2.2 KPI sets a target of 0% re-mapping of common data elements per new event as a measurable standardization objective.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce data access boundaries at the database layer — not solely at the application or UI layer — so that a user's jurisdiction-scoped access restrictions cannot be bypassed through direct database queries or API calls.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert advisory explicitly requires jurisdiction-scoped data visibility to be enforced at the data layer to satisfy state public health data sovereignty laws.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support capture of animal-specific entity data including at minimum: species, owner identity and contact information, and tag-based Subject ID, to support zoonotic outbreak investigations involving veterinary and animal control partners.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 3 stakeholders include veterinary and animal control partners with a specific need for animal-specific entity data capture including species, owner, and tagging information.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall link each new case record to a valid Event ID at the time of case creation, establishing the case's membership in the investigation, and shall reject case records that are not associated with an existing Event ID within the jurisdiction.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.1 requires each new case to link its Entity ID to the relevant Event ID to establish investigation membership.

sme_subject: PHIN-certified public health informaticist and outbreak epidemiologist
sme_advisory: This OM system sits at the intersection of outbreak epidemiology, public health informatics, and emergency preparedness infrastructure. Requirements engineers should pay close attention to PHIN Preparedness Key Performance Measures as hard, measurable compliance targets — not aspirational goals — and should treat the PHIN Cross Functional Components Requirements document as a normative dependency that must be fully incorporated into security and availability requirements. The many-to-many entity relationship model, parent-child specimen lineage, and the deduplication business rule are architecturally complex and will generate a disproportionate share of data integrity defects if not specified with precision; each deserves its own detailed requirement cluster with explicit edge-case handling.
