generated_requirements.text: The GSM-R network shall provide a minimum radio coverage probability of 95% per 100-metre location interval at a field strength of 38.5 dBmV/m (–98 dBm) for voice and non-safety-critical data across all covered lines.
generated_requirements.rationale: Directly maps to Objective 1 success criterion and establishes the measurable baseline coverage target for all lines.

generated_requirements.text: On all lines equipped with ETCS Level 2 or Level 3 operating at speeds up to 220 km/h, the GSM-R network shall provide a minimum radio coverage probability of 95% per 100-metre location interval at a field strength of 41.5 dBmV/m (–95 dBm).
generated_requirements.rationale: ETCS Level 2/3 train control depends on continuous radio connectivity; the higher field strength threshold reflects the safety-critical nature of these lines.

generated_requirements.text: The network coverage design shall assume a maximum antenna-to-receiver loss of 3 dB and shall incorporate an additional 3 dB margin to account for equipment ageing and other degradation factors.
generated_requirements.rationale: Ensures that coverage probability targets remain met throughout the operational lifetime of the infrastructure.

generated_requirements.text: All call setup times shall meet the targets defined in the EIRENE Functional Requirements Specification (FRS) with both authentication and ciphering enabled; authentication may be deferred only for Railway emergency calls and Railway operation calls where deferral is necessary to meet the FRS timing target, and deferral shall not exceed the duration of the call setup phase.
generated_requirements.rationale: Objective 2 mandates FRS-compliant call setup times even with security services active, while explicitly permitting bounded deferral to resolve the performance–security trade-off.

generated_requirements.text: The transit time for any call passing through a network external to GSM (e.g., an ISDN connection between an MSC and a controller terminal) shall not exceed 250 milliseconds.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-03 establishes this as a hard architectural constraint on external network segments to ensure end-to-end call setup performance.

generated_requirements.text: If a Railway emergency call is not established within 2 seconds of initiation, the Cab radio shall immediately provide the driver with both an audible alert and a visual status indication on the MMI display.
generated_requirements.rationale: Objective 2 requires the driver to be informed of call establishment failure within a defined time to enable corrective action.

generated_requirements.text: The Cab radio shall automatically retry a failed Railway emergency call without any manual intervention for a period of up to 30 seconds from the initial attempt, or until the driver explicitly abandons the attempt.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-02 and Objective 4 mandate automatic retry to guarantee emergency call delivery without requiring driver action during a safety-critical event.

generated_requirements.text: All mobile equipment shall operate within the R-GSM frequency band (876–915 MHz uplink / 921–960 MHz downlink) without requiring any hardware or software reconfiguration when roaming between participating EIRENE networks.
generated_requirements.rationale: Objective 3 and TC-02 require a single common frequency band to enable seamless cross-border operation without equipment modification.

generated_requirements.text: The GSM-R network shall implement the EIRENE numbering plan as a private numbering plan, including all internationally mandated short codes (four-digit, Call Type = 1) and function codes, identically across all participating networks.
generated_requirements.rationale: Objective 3 and BR-30 require a standardised numbering plan to enable location-independent functional addressing across all EIRENE networks.

generated_requirements.text: On-train functional numbers based on the train number shall be automatically re-registered to the new EIRENE network every time the train crosses a network boundary, without requiring any driver or operator intervention.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-10 and Objective 3 mandate transparent re-registration at borders to maintain continuous functional addressing during cross-border operations.

generated_requirements.text: International calls between EIRENE networks shall be routed using the standardised combination of the EIRENE Breakout Code and the International Code assigned by the UIC to the destination network, in accordance with ITU-T E.164.
generated_requirements.rationale: Objective 3 and BR-32 require a standardised international routing mechanism to ensure interoperability without manual number translation.

generated_requirements.text: All Railway emergency calls shall use GSM Voice Group Call Service (VGCS) with eMLPP priority levels enforced at every network element, ensuring that no lower-priority call can pre-empt an active Railway emergency call.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 and Objective 4 establish the non-negotiable priority hierarchy that underpins the safety model of the EIRENE system.

generated_requirements.text: When a lower-priority call is pre-empted by a higher-priority eMLPP call, the pre-empted party shall receive a defined in-band or out-of-band notification (as specified in the eMLPP supplementary service specification within EN 301 515) before the connection is released.
generated_requirements.rationale: The eMLPP specification mandates pre-emption notification; omitting this creates an undefined user experience and potential safety confusion.

generated_requirements.text: After cleardown of any Railway emergency call, the system shall automatically initiate a confirmation process: a confirmation message shall be sent to the designated confirmation centre using the standardised short code, with a random delay applied to avoid network congestion, and the confirmation message shall use eMLPP priority 4.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-02 and Objective 4 require 100% confirmation of Railway emergency calls to provide an auditable safety record.

generated_requirements.text: The confirmation centre shall support both Format A and Format B Railway emergency call confirmation message structures, with explicitly defined field lengths and encoding rules for each format; if the confirmation centre is unreachable or returns an error, the Cab radio shall log the failed confirmation attempt with a timestamp and retry delivery.
generated_requirements.rationale: Objective 4 requires 100% confirmation; specifying both formats and error handling prevents silent confirmation failures.

generated_requirements.text: Functional number registration shall be performed using USSD messages over the GSM air interface following the GSM Follow-me service protocol, transmitting the functional number together with the subscriber's IMSI to the network-side USSD gateway.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-05 mandates the specific protocol mechanism for functional number registration to ensure interoperability across all EIRENE networks.

generated_requirements.text: The network-side HLR and USSD gateway shall update the functional number routing database upon receipt of a valid registration USSD message, associating the functional number with the registering subscriber's IMSI and MSISDN, and shall return an acknowledgement to the mobile within the call setup timing constraints defined in the EIRENE FRS.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 scopes network-side routing database management; the HLR/USSD gateway behaviour must be specified to ensure end-to-end registration reliability.

generated_requirements.text: Every functional number registration, deregistration, forced de-registration, and re-registration event shall generate a timestamped audit record at the network side, capturing the functional number, the IMSI, the MSISDN, the event type, and the outcome, stored in a manner accessible for operational audit.
generated_requirements.rationale: Functional number lifecycle auditability is required for safety investigation and operational accountability, particularly for forced de-registration events.

generated_requirements.text: Deregistration of a functional number shall only be accepted by the network from the MSISDN currently associated with that functional number; any deregistration request from a different MSISDN shall be rejected unless the full seven-step forced de-registration sequence has been successfully completed.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-06 and BR-07 protect against accidental or malicious deregistration of safety-critical functional identifiers.

generated_requirements.text: The forced de-registration sequence shall comprise exactly seven steps: interrogation of the current registration, receipt of the currently registered MSISDN, forced de-registration of the existing registration, acknowledgement of de-registration, registration of the new functional number, acknowledgement of new registration, and notification to the user; national operators may require additional steps before or instead of this sequence.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-07 defines the mandatory sequence to prevent functional number conflicts and ensure controlled handover of safety-critical identifiers.

generated_requirements.text: The Cab radio shall not initiate deregistration of an existing functional number until registration of the replacement functional number has been confirmed as successful by the network; if registration fails, the existing functional number shall remain registered.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-08 prevents functional numbers from entering an unregistered state due to failed registration attempts, which would create a communication blackout.

generated_requirements.text: If the HLR or USSD gateway is unavailable when a functional number registration or re-registration is attempted, the Cab radio shall continue in service using the last successfully registered functional number and shall display a persistent warning to the driver indicating that registration is pending.
generated_requirements.rationale: Graceful degradation under partial network failure is required to prevent a complete loss of operational capability when network nodes are temporarily unavailable.

generated_requirements.text: The Engine Number to MSISDN association shall require explicit manual confirmation from an authorised operator before it can be removed from the routing database; automated or unconfirmed removal shall be rejected by the network.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-09 mandates manual confirmation to prevent accidental deregistration of safety-critical engine identifiers.

generated_requirements.text: The Cab radio MMI shall provide a dedicated physical emergency call control (e.g., a red button) that initiates a Railway emergency call with a single unambiguous action; the control shall be distinct from all other call initiation controls and shall be operable by the driver without visual attention.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 and Stakeholder 3.3 require the MMI to minimise driver cognitive burden, particularly for emergency calls; a dedicated physical control is essential for safety.

generated_requirements.text: The Cab radio MMI shall support the following call initiation methods: (a) dedicated emergency call control, (b) priority call initiation, (c) stored number selection and dial, and (d) manual digit entry and dial; each method shall be accessible without navigating more than two MMI levels from the idle state.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 explicitly scopes all four call initiation methods; the two-level navigation constraint reflects the driver cognitive burden minimisation requirement.

generated_requirements.text: The Cab radio MMI display shall show, at minimum, the following information in each state: (a) idle — current network, registered functional number, and signal strength; (b) call active — call type, calling/called party functional number or CLI, call duration, and eMLPP priority; (c) emergency call — call status, retry countdown, and audible/visual alert as specified in R006.
generated_requirements.rationale: Stakeholder 3.3 requires the driver to have situational awareness of the radio system state at all times; defined display content per state ensures this.

generated_requirements.text: The Cab radio shall implement the complete call arbitration state machine as defined in the EIRENE call arbitration tables, covering every combination of incoming and outgoing call type (emergency, priority, stored-number, group, data) against every possible current call state (idle, active voice, active data, VGCS listening, VGCS talking, shunting group active), applying the defined arbitration outcome without requiring driver intervention.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-03 mandates automatic arbitration; completeness across all state combinations is required to prevent undefined behaviour in edge cases.

generated_requirements.text: Replacement of the handset during an active call shall produce the outcome defined in the EIRENE call management tables for each call type and state; the system shall not leave any call in an undefined or uncleared state as a result of handset replacement.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 requires defined handset replacement outcomes to prevent safety-critical calls from being silently dropped or left active without the driver's awareness.

generated_requirements.text: Termination of a VGCS or VBS call by DTMF signalling shall use exclusively the three-digit sequence '***'; the network shall reject any other DTMF sequence as a call termination command.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-15 mandates a single, unambiguous termination sequence to prevent accidental or malicious call termination.

generated_requirements.text: When a controller transmits the DTMF sequence '###' to signal intent to talk in a VGCS group call, the network shall send a SET-PARAMETER message with D-ATT=T to the currently talking mobile; if multiple controllers are already talking simultaneously, receipt of '###' from an additional controller shall not cause the network to send a duplicate SET-PARAMETER message with D-ATT=T.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-16 and BR-17 define the precise multi-controller talking arbitration behaviour; the duplicate suppression rule prevents conflicting SET-PARAMETER messages.

generated_requirements.text: The network shall send a SET-PARAMETER message with D-ATT=F only when all controllers currently talking in a VGCS group call have transmitted the DTMF sequence '#**'; receipt of '#**' from a subset of talking controllers shall not trigger D-ATT=F.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-17 requires that talking rights are only revoked when all talking controllers have signalled cessation, preventing premature muting of active group calls.

generated_requirements.text: DTMF tones added to the VGCS voice channel by controller terminals or fixed-line dispatchers shall have a duration of 70 ms ± 5 ms with a minimum inter-tone gap of 65 ms; equipment that cannot meet these timing constraints shall not be used for VGCS/VBS group call management.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-18 and TC-04 define these as hard timing constraints required for reliable DTMF decoding across all network elements.

generated_requirements.text: On entering shunting mode, the terminal shall first check whether a shunting group ID is already activated on the SIM; if so, it shall re-establish the existing group call rather than initiating a new one, and shall initially select the common shunting group.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-20 and BR-21 define the mandatory entry sequence for shunting mode to ensure continuity of group communications.

generated_requirements.text: Members of a dedicated shunting team shall be prevented by the terminal application from initiating new calls while in shunting mode, unless they are the registered shunting team leader who has left shunting mode using the 'maintain group call' function.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-22 restricts call initiation to prevent shunting team members from disrupting safety-critical group communications.

generated_requirements.text: A user shall not be permitted to join a dedicated shunting group unless a valid functional number for that group has been successfully registered to the GSM-R network by their terminal; the terminal shall reject the join attempt and display an error if registration has not been confirmed.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-23 prevents unauthorised or unregistered users from joining dedicated shunting groups, which could compromise shunting safety.

generated_requirements.text: The shunting leader shall be presented with exactly two options when leaving shunting mode: 'Release group call' (which deregisters all members and terminates the group call) or 'Maintain group call' (which allows the leader to leave temporarily while other members remain active in the group call).
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-24 defines the two mandatory exit options to give the shunting leader controlled flexibility without leaving the group in an undefined state.

generated_requirements.text: The Operational radio shall support shunting mode, including SIM-based group ID management, common and dedicated shunting group call establishment, link assurance signal generation and deactivation in accordance with the MORANE SM FFFIS, and the call initiation restrictions defined in BR-22 through BR-24.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 explicitly scopes shunting mode for the Operational radio; this requirement closes the gap identified in the previous critique.

generated_requirements.text: The list of mandatory emergency Group IDs stored on the SIM shall be refreshed automatically when the train crosses into a new EIRENE network; the Group ID refresh shall be completed before or concurrently with functional number re-registration, and all mandatory emergency Group IDs shall maintain active status throughout the transition.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-26 requires mandatory emergency Group IDs to be active at all times; specifying the timing relationship with re-registration prevents a window of vulnerability at network boundaries.

generated_requirements.text: All SIM cards used in EIRENE mobile equipment shall comply with the MORANE FFFIS for GSM-R SIM Cards; SIM cards in Cab radios shall be physically integrated and removable only by maintenance staff using appropriate tools; SIM cards in Operational radios shall be fixed and removable only with a tool.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-25 mandates SIM compliance and physical security to prevent accidental removal and ensure identity integrity in operational service.

generated_requirements.text: The calling party's functional number shall be passed to the receiving mobile using UUS1 during call setup; if the functional number is not available, the CLI (MSISDN) shall be displayed on the receiving MMI instead.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-27 requires functional number presentation to enable controllers and drivers to identify callers by role rather than by MSISDN.

generated_requirements.text: The EIRENE numbering plan shall define the following functional number categories with their respective Call Type values, digit structures, and maximum lengths: Train Function Numbers, Engine Function Numbers, Coach Function Numbers, Shunting Team functional numbers, Maintenance Team functional numbers, and Train Controller functional numbers; all numbers shall not exceed 15 digits and shall consist entirely of digits 0–9.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 scopes all six functional number categories; BR-28 constrains length and character set; explicit structure definitions are required for interoperability.

generated_requirements.text: The EIRENE numbering plan shall define Service Area numbers and group/broadcast call identifiers, including VBS (Voice Broadcast Service) numbering; Service Area allocation in network boundary areas shall be determined by bilateral agreement between adjacent national operators, and the system shall apply a defined default behaviour (maintaining the last active Service Area assignment) in boundary areas pending such agreement.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 scopes Service Area and VBS numbering; OC-03 establishes the bilateral agreement constraint; a default behaviour is required to prevent undefined operation at boundaries.

generated_requirements.text: Each Train Number shall be unique within each GSM-R network for the duration of the journey; the network shall reject any attempt to register a Train Number that is already active within the same network.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-29 mandates Train Number uniqueness to prevent routing ambiguity in location-dependent addressing.

generated_requirements.text: Access to public networks from the GSM-R network shall require the use of a defined Access Code followed by the international or national number of the called subscriber; the network shall apply call barring restrictions to public network access as configured by the national operator.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-31 controls public network access to prevent misuse of the safety-critical GSM-R network for non-railway communications.

generated_requirements.text: The GSM-R network shall support cell broadcast (SMS-CB) with the message identifier range defined in the EIRENE specification; the network shall reject cell broadcast messages using identifiers outside the defined EIRENE range to prevent conflicts with non-railway cell broadcast services.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 scopes SMS-CB support; constraining the identifier range prevents interference with other broadcast services and ensures predictable message routing.

generated_requirements.text: Mobile equipment shall give preference to the R-GSM band during automatic network selection; manual network selection shall not be available to the driver during an active voice call; if directed network selection is triggered by an external device during an active call, the network change shall be deferred until the call is terminated or coverage is lost, and the driver shall be notified of the pending change.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-12 and BR-13 define network selection priority and the prohibition on mid-call manual selection to prevent disruption of active safety-critical calls.

generated_requirements.text: The mobile shall not enter direct mode if GSM-R service is available; the transition from GSM-R mode to direct mode shall only occur when GSM-R coverage is confirmed lost, with a hysteresis threshold applied to prevent oscillation in marginal coverage areas; the transition back to GSM-R mode shall occur automatically when GSM-R coverage is re-established above the hysteresis threshold.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-14 prohibits direct mode when GSM-R is available; hysteresis is required to prevent rapid mode switching in marginal coverage areas, which could disrupt communications.

generated_requirements.text: Where direct mode is implemented, it shall use frequency-modulated equipment conforming to ETS 300 086, operating at a maximum transmit power of 1 Watt, with a minimum receiver sensitivity of –107 dBm, CTCSS squelch at exactly 203.5 Hz, simplex PTT operation, and a common access channel at 876.0125 MHz.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-07, TC-08, and RC-12 define the mandatory direct mode parameters required for interoperability between equipment from different manufacturers.

generated_requirements.text: The Cab radio shall implement the EURORADIO interface to the ETCS on-board unit as defined in the MORANE EURO FFFIS, without modification to message formats, timing parameters, or connection management procedures defined therein.
generated_requirements.rationale: RC-15 mandates EURORADIO FFFIS compliance; the prohibition on modification ensures interoperability between Cab radios and ETCS units from different manufacturers.

generated_requirements.text: The Cab radio shall provide a standard data interface for external computer connection, with the physical connector type, communication protocol, and data format specified in the EIRENE SRS; this interface shall support reception of Railway emergency call confirmation data for transmission to a connected train-borne recorder.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 scopes the standard data interface; specifying the physical and logical interface parameters is required for interoperability with recorders from different manufacturers.

generated_requirements.text: Inter-network call routing between EIRENE networks shall use SS7 with MAP over SCCP with ITU-T E.164 Global Titles; no alternative signalling protocol shall be used for inter-EIRENE-network communication.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-06 mandates SS7/MAP as the exclusive inter-network signalling protocol to ensure consistent routing across all participating networks.

generated_requirements.text: All EIRENE mobile equipment shall operate correctly across the temperature range –20°C to +55°C (Cab radio: –20°C to +70°C) and shall survive storage at –40°C without permanent degradation of performance.
generated_requirements.rationale: Objective 6 defines the mandatory temperature operating and storage ranges for all equipment categories to ensure reliability in the railway environment.

generated_requirements.text: All EIRENE mobile equipment shall pass the full IEC 68 environmental test suite, comprising: Cold, Dry heat, Damp heat, Impact, Vibration, Acceleration, Corrosive atmospheres, Air pressure, Temperature change, and Fire hazard tests, conducted at facilities accredited in accordance with EN 45001.
generated_requirements.rationale: Objective 6 and RC-09 mandate the complete IEC 68 test suite to verify equipment durability across all railway environmental conditions.

generated_requirements.text: All EIRENE mobile equipment shall comply with the applicable parts of ENV 50121 for both EMC emissions and immunity in the railway environment; where GSM transmission band emissions exceed ENV 50121 limits, EN 301 515 shall take precedence and this precedence shall be explicitly documented in the equipment compliance declaration.
generated_requirements.rationale: RC-05 establishes the ENV 50121 compliance requirement and the EN 301 515 precedence rule; explicit documentation prevents test failures due to ambiguous precedence.

generated_requirements.text: Cab radio equipment shall comply with insulation co-ordination requirements for clearances and creepages as defined in prEN 50124 Part 1, for the pollution degree and overvoltage category applicable to the cab environment, and shall pass electrical tests as defined in IEC 571 Parts 1, 2, and 3, including withstand of power supply transients as defined in prEN 50155.
generated_requirements.rationale: RC-10 and RC-11 mandate these electrical safety standards for Cab radio equipment operating in the high-voltage railway rolling stock environment.

generated_requirements.text: When the Cab radio supply voltage drops below the minimum operating threshold during an active Railway emergency call, the radio shall maintain the call for a minimum of 30 seconds using any available backup supply before gracefully terminating the call with a defined release cause; if no backup supply is available, the radio shall transmit a final status message before power loss.
generated_requirements.rationale: Power supply failure during an emergency call is a critical edge case; defined behaviour prevents silent call loss at the most safety-critical moment.

generated_requirements.text: Cab radio and Operational radio equipment shall conform to EN 50153 to protect users against electrical hazards from the railway rolling stock power supply environment.
generated_requirements.rationale: RC-04 mandates EN 50153 compliance to protect train crew from electrical hazards in the rolling stock environment.

generated_requirements.text: All EIRENE mobile equipment shall conform to EN 60950 (including amendments A1 and A2) for safety of information technology equipment.
generated_requirements.rationale: RC-03 mandates EN 60950 compliance as a baseline electrical safety requirement for all mobile equipment.

generated_requirements.text: Operational radios shall conform to a minimum ingress protection rating of IP 54 as defined in IEC 529 / EN 60529, and shall withstand shock loads of 5g semi-sinusoidal (operational) and 10g exceptional, and a free-fall drop of 1.0 metre onto a hard surface without loss of function.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 and RC-13 define the enhanced environmental requirements for Operational radios used in trackside maintenance environments.

generated_requirements.text: Operational radios shall comply with the vibration and shock profiles defined in prEN 50125 and prEN 50155 for railway equipment, in addition to the IEC 68 test suite.
generated_requirements.rationale: RC-06 mandates prEN 50125/50155 compliance for all railway equipment; this closes the gap identified in the previous critique for Operational radio vibration requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The General Purpose radio shall operate correctly across the temperature range –20°C to +55°C, shall pass the applicable IEC 68 environmental tests, and shall comply with the applicable parts of ENV 50121 for EMC emissions and immunity.
generated_requirements.rationale: Objective 6 applies to all mobile equipment categories; this closes the gap identified in the previous critique for General Purpose radio environmental requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The General Purpose radio rechargeable battery shall provide a minimum of 8 hours of operation at an ambient temperature of +18°C to +25°C under the defined EIRENE usage cycle, measured without the use of discontinuous reception or transmission (DTX/DRX).
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 and OC-05 define the battery life requirement and prohibit DTX/DRX measurement to ensure realistic operational endurance.

generated_requirements.text: The Operational radio rechargeable battery shall provide a minimum of 8 hours of operation across the temperature range –10°C to +55°C under the defined EIRENE usage cycle (including 60% group calls), measured without the use of DTX/DRX.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 defines the enhanced battery requirement for Operational radios operating in cold trackside environments with high group call usage.

generated_requirements.text: The GSM-R network shall enforce GSM ciphering for all voice and data communications using standardised ciphering algorithms as defined in EN 301 515; no call shall be established in unciphered mode unless explicitly permitted by the network operator for a defined call type.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 scopes encryption; mandatory ciphering protects safety-critical railway communications from interception and manipulation.

generated_requirements.text: Closed User Group (CUG) shall be implemented on the GSM-R network in accordance with EN 301 515, with interoperability considerations applied to ensure that CUG restrictions do not prevent mandatory inter-network calls (e.g., Railway emergency calls) between participating EIRENE networks.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 scopes CUG with interoperability considerations; emergency calls must not be blocked by CUG restrictions at network boundaries.

generated_requirements.text: All design, manufacturing, testing, and installation of EIRENE mobile radio equipment shall be performed under a quality management system certified to ISO 9001; procurement processes shall verify ISO 9001 certification of all equipment manufacturers before contract award.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-33 and RC-07 mandate ISO 9001 compliance across the entire equipment lifecycle to ensure consistent quality and traceability.

generated_requirements.text: National operators may apply stricter environmental or physical standards than those defined in this specification, provided that such stricter standards do not prevent the use of EIRENE-compliant mobile equipment from other participating countries on their network.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-34 and OC-02 preserve national sovereignty while protecting the pan-European interoperability principle.

generated_requirements.text: The multi-party call service shall be used to establish and maintain calls between all active cabs on the same train in multi-driver operations; the call shall be maintained automatically without requiring driver intervention when additional cabs are activated or deactivated.
generated_requirements.rationale: Objective 5 and Section 6.1 scope multi-party call support for multi-driver operations as a mandatory capability.

generated_requirements.text: Link assurance signals for shunting mode shall be generated and deactivated in strict accordance with the timing and procedural requirements defined in the MORANE SM FFFIS; any deviation from the specified link assurance signal parameters shall be treated as a link failure.
generated_requirements.rationale: Objective 5 and the MORANE SM FFFIS dependency define link assurance as a safety-critical mechanism for confirming shunting radio connectivity.

generated_requirements.text: Where enhanced Location Dependent Addressing (eLDA) is implemented using train-based position information, the implementation shall comply with the eLDA FRS and IRS; cell-based location-dependent addressing shall be implemented as the minimum mandatory capability on all EIRENE networks.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 scopes LDA with cell-based as the mandatory minimum and eLDA as an optional enhancement, consistent with RC-16.

sme_subject: GSM-R network architect and railway radio interoperability engineer
sme_advisory: The EIRENE GSM-R specification sits at the intersection of safety-critical railway operations and cellular telecommunications, meaning requirements engineers must treat every performance figure (coverage probability, call setup time, retry duration, DTMF timing) as a hard safety constraint rather than a quality-of-service aspiration. Pay particular attention to the boundary conditions: cross-border re-registration, forced de-registration sequencing, and the transition between GSM-R and direct mode are the areas most likely to produce latent failure modes that only manifest in operational edge cases. Requirements must be traceable to specific clauses of EN 301 515, the EIRENE FRS, and the MORANE FFFIS documents, and every mandatory/optional distinction must be explicitly stated, because ambiguity here directly translates to interoperability failures at national borders.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The EIRENE System Requirements Specification defines the set of requirements which a railway radio system shall comply with in order to ensure interoperability between national railways. (I) The EIRENE System Requirements Specification defines the set of requirements which a railway radio system shall comply with in order to ensure interoperability between national railways. (I)
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Section 8 covers controller equipment. Section 9 is concerned with a numbering plan for the variety of functional numbers which are required. Section 10 details the handling of other information, such as priority, closed user groups and encryption algorithms, which must be applied consistently in all networks. (I) Section 8 covers controller equipment. Section 9 is concerned with a numbering plan for the variety of functional numbers which are required. Section 10 details the handling of other information, such as priority, closed user groups and encryption algorithms, which must be applied consistently in all networks. (I)
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The following minimum values shall apply: (M) The following minimum values shall apply: (M) coverage probability of 95% based on a coverage level of 38.5 dBmV/m (-98 dBm) for voice and non-safety critical data; coverage probability of 95% based on a coverage level of 41.5 dBmV/m (-95 dBm) on lines with ETCS levels 2/3 for speeds lower than or equal to 220km/h.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The EIRENE mobile installation shall be designed to operate in a network meeting the criteria in 3.2.2 and 3.2.3. (M) The EIRENE mobile installation shall be designed to operate in a network meeting the criteria in 3.2.2 and 3.2.3. (M) Note 1: The specified coverage probability means that with a probability value of at least 95% in each location interval (length: 100m) the measured coverage level shall be greater than or equal to the figures stated above. The coverage levels specified above consider a maximum loss of 3 dB between antenna and receiver and an additional margin of 3 dB for other factors such as ageing. (I). The values for ETCS levels 2/3 concerning coverage and speed-limitations are to be validated and, if necessary, reviewed after the first operational implementation of ETCS. (I)
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Call setup times as defined in the EIRENE FRS shall be achieved with authentication and ciphering procedures enabled. (M) Call setup times as defined in the EIRENE FRS shall be achieved with authentication and ciphering procedures enabled. (M) Note: Authentication may be delayed if necessary to achieve the required call setup times.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: In order to achieve these times the passage of the call through any networks external to GSM (eg from the GSM MSC to a controller linked by an ISDN connection) must take less than 250ms. (I) In order to achieve these times the passage of the call through any networks external to GSM (eg from the GSM MSC to a controller linked by an ISDN connection) must take less than 250ms. (I)
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The network shall operate in a sub-band, or combination of sub-bands, of the R-GSM band as defined in [EN 301 515, Index [35]]. (M) The network shall operate in a sub-band, or combination of sub-bands, of the R-GSM band as defined in [EN 301 515, Index [35]]. (M)

ground_truth_requirements.text: The carrier frequency is designated by the absolute radio frequency channel number (ARFCN), and is defined in [EN 301 515, Index [35]]. ground_truth_requirements.text: 1) Call Type (CT) This prefix defines how to interpret the User Number that follows. It shall consist of one or two digits as defined in table 9-1. 2) User Number (UN) The UN is variable in length and depends on the information on which it is based. The following formats are defined: Train Function Numbers (TFN) shall take one of the forms as defined in table 9-2. Leading zeros shall be used in those situations where the train number is less than five digits. Engine Function Numbers (EFN) shall take the format as defined in table 9-3. Coach Function Numbers (CFN) shall take the format as defined in table 9-4. Maintenance and Shunting Team Members. The UIN field format for calls to shunting teams and maintenance teams shall consist of a Location Number (LN), which identifies the location where the called party is registered, followed by a Function Code (FC). The Location Number shall consist of 5 digits and shall be assigned on a national basis. The Function Code shall consist of 4 digits. The first digit of the FC is related to the Team Type (TT) and is specified by table 9-5. The composition of the Function Code field for calls to shunting teams and maintenance teams shall be as defined in table 9-6. Train Controllers. The UIN field for calls to train controllers shall be a Location Number (LN) which identifies the location where the called party is registered. The Location Number shall consist of 5 digits and shall be assigned on a national basis. The FC field for calls to train controllers shall consist of two digits and be as defined in table 9-7. Mobile Subscriber Number (MSN). The UN of the MSN shall consist of the Subscriber Number. Group and broadcast calls. The UIN field format for group calls shall be a Service Area (SA) indicator, which identifies the area in which the group call is to be active. Each Service Area shall be allocated on a national basis. In network boundary areas, the Service Area shall be allocated on a bilateral basis. (See also subsection 9.9.) The FC field format for group calls shall consist of three digits and be as defined in table 9-8.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

generated_requirements.text: The system shall establish a railway emergency call such that an audible indication commences at the receiving end within 2 seconds in at least 95% of cases and within 3 seconds in at least 99% of cases, measured end-to-end including any functional number translation time.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §2 Objective 1 defines these as hard, safety-critical, contractually binding call set-up time thresholds for railway emergency calls.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall establish group calls between drivers in the same area within 5 seconds in at least 95% of cases, all operational mobile-to-fixed calls within 5 seconds (95th percentile), all operational fixed-to-mobile calls within 7 seconds (95th percentile), and all operational mobile-to-mobile calls within 10 seconds (95th percentile), measured end-to-end.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §2 Objective 1 specifies these quantitative call set-up time SLAs as measurable success criteria for operational voice services.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically retry a failed railway emergency call connection for up to 30 seconds before reporting failure to the originating user.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §2 Objective 1 explicitly defines the 30-second automatic retry window as a success criterion for emergency call resilience.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall transmit automatic confirmation messages for both the originating and receiving mobiles of a railway emergency call to a central ground location within 5 minutes of call termination, with a probability of successful receipt of at least 99%.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §2 Objective 6 and §5.11 define the 99% / 5-minute confirmation delivery target as a hard reliability criterion for post-incident auditability.

generated_requirements.text: If network contact is lost while an emergency call confirmation message is pending, the system shall buffer the confirmation message on-board and retransmit it as soon as network contact is regained, for a maximum retry window of 5 minutes from the point of call termination; if the retry window expires or the buffer is full, the system shall log the failure and cease retransmission attempts.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §5.11 and §2 Objective 6 define the confirmation retry and buffering behaviour, and the domain expert advisory identifies buffer-full and window-expiry edge cases as required.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate emergency call confirmation messages automatically, without any user input, commencing at the end of the call or when the mobile moves out of the call area.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §5.11 mandates that confirmation is fully automatic and requires no driver or operator action.

generated_requirements.text: The cab radio shall provide a standardised real-time interface to the train-borne recorder, reporting the following event types with their associated data fields and timestamps: emergency call activation, emergency call termination, emergency call connection failure, driver safety device alarm, radio fault, network change, and functional number registration and deregistration.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §6.1 and §3.11 require a standardised train-borne recorder interface; the domain expert advisory mandates enumeration of all event types and their data fields.

generated_requirements.text: A railway emergency call shall be initiable by a single MMI action only (e.g. pressing the dedicated red emergency button); the type of emergency call (train emergency or shunting emergency) shall be determined automatically by the system based on the current operating mode of the radio.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §5.4 mandates single-action initiation and automatic call-type determination to minimise driver workload in safety-critical situations.

generated_requirements.text: A railway emergency call shall only be terminable by the originator, a participating controller, or the network after a nationally determined period of no speech; drivers who did not initiate the call shall be prevented from terminating it.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §5.4 defines the strict termination authority rules to prevent premature clearing of safety-critical emergency calls.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce a strict five-level call priority hierarchy in descending order: (1) Railway Emergency, (2) Control-Command (Safety), (3) Public Emergency and Group Calls Between Drivers in the Same Area, (4) Railway Operation, (5) Railway Information; a higher-priority call shall always pre-empt a lower-priority call, and when pre-emption is required, the lowest-priority ongoing call shall be terminated first.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §5.1 defines the mandatory five-level priority hierarchy and the lowest-first pre-emption rule as a core business rule.

generated_requirements.text: When a higher-priority call pre-empts multiple lower-priority calls simultaneously, the system shall: (a) terminate the lowest-priority call first, (b) provide an audible and visual pre-emption indication to each affected party, (c) re-order any queued calls by priority after pre-emption is complete, and (d) connect the pre-empting call within the applicable call set-up time SLA.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert advisory identifies the pre-emption cascade sequence, per-party indications, and queue re-ordering as a required state-machine completeness pattern.

generated_requirements.text: Railway emergency calls and control-command calls shall be answered automatically on voice channels; railway operation calls shall be answered automatically on cab radios (mandatory) and optionally on other equipment types; railway information calls shall not be answered automatically.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §5.1 specifies the auto-answer rules per call priority level and per equipment type.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restrict access to all network functions and services to authorised EIRENE users only, enforcing the closed user group (CUG) access matrix that defines which subscriber types may call which other subscriber types; national railways may apply additional restrictions to the access matrix but shall not grant permissions beyond those defined in the EIRENE CUG specification.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §5.2 mandates CUG enforcement as a core business rule governing all network access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate every device and user attempting to attach to the EIRENE network using SIM-based or equivalent credential mechanisms; any device or user not presenting valid EIRENE credentials shall be denied attachment and all network services.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §5.2 and §8.1 require that only authorised users access the network; the previous critique identifies the absence of an explicit authentication NFR as a gap.

generated_requirements.text: Access to all network management and maintenance functions — including call barring configuration, group area definition, subscription profile management, and shunting mode switching — shall be restricted to authenticated and authorised personnel only, and shall not be accessible to general users.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §8.1 explicitly states that maintenance and configuration access is restricted to authorised personnel; the previous critique flags this as a distinct security gap.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support functional addressing, allowing controllers to initiate calls to any train using its operational train number; the network shall automatically translate the functional number to the current telephone number of the cab radio registered to that train at the time of call initiation.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §4.2 and §1 identify functional addressing as the core solution to the operational inefficiency of manual cross-reference tables.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow up to three functional numbers to be assigned to a single user simultaneously; if a user attempts to register a fourth functional number, the system shall reject the attempt, provide an explicit error indication to the registering user identifying which existing registration is in conflict, and shall not silently discard the request.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §5.3 defines the three-number limit; the domain expert advisory requires explicit error notification rather than silent rejection for the overflow case.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect duplicate functional number registrations; upon detection, the system shall warn the newly registering user, provide a controlled override mechanism to the newly registering user, and notify the previously registered user that their registration has been displaced if the override is exercised.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §5.3 mandates duplicate detection, user warning, controlled override, and notification to the displaced user as a complete workflow.

generated_requirements.text: Upon a network change, the system shall automatically re-register up to ten functional numbers for the user without any manual driver intervention, completing all re-registrations within 30 seconds of network selection.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §2 Objective 2 defines zero-intervention automatic re-registration and the 30-second completion target as measurable success criteria.

generated_requirements.text: If automatic functional number re-registration fails during a network change mid-journey, the system shall: (a) provide an audible and visual failure indication to the driver, (b) continue attempting re-registration for the duration of network coverage, and (c) report the re-registration failure event to the train-borne recorder.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert advisory identifies re-registration failure mid-journey as a required lifecycle edge case that must be explicitly specified.

generated_requirements.text: Functional numbers shall consist of numeric characters only and shall remain valid as the user roams between EIRENE networks; a fixed relationship shall be maintained between the telephone numbers of radios installed in a locomotive or coach and the stock number, even if the radio unit is replaced.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §5.3 and §5.9 define the numeric-only constraint and the stock-number-to-telephone-number fixed relationship as mandatory business rules.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support three network selection modes: manual, directed (triggered by an external device such as a balise), and automatic; automatic network selection shall be categorically prohibited for international border crossings, and only manual or directed network selection shall be permitted at borders; this constraint shall not be overridable by any national railway operator.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §5.6 and §7.2 define the three modes and the absolute prohibition on automatic selection at borders as a hard regulatory constraint.

generated_requirements.text: The network selection priority order shall always be: (1) home EIRENE network, (2) foreign EIRENE networks, (3) public networks; the system shall never attach to a public network in preference to an available EIRENE network.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §5.6 mandates this priority order to prevent inadvertent attachment to public networks with degraded railway service capability.

generated_requirements.text: If a directed network selection command (e.g. from a balise) is received while a voice call is in progress, the system shall defer the network change until the call is terminated or coverage of the current network is lost; if coverage is lost before the call ends, the system shall immediately execute the network change and provide an audible and visual indication to the driver.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §5.6 and the domain expert advisory require explicit specification of the deferral logic and the coverage-loss override condition.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an audible and visual indication to the driver whenever a network change occurs without user intervention.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §4.6 mandates that drivers are always informed of automatic network changes to maintain situational awareness.

generated_requirements.text: The shunting mode shall provide a regulated group call with a link assurance signal of 800–850 Hz, with a 1-second tone followed by a 2-second silent interval; this signal specification is fixed and not configurable.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §7.1 and §2 Objective 5 define the link assurance tone frequency and cadence as a fixed, non-configurable specification.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support two configurable link assurance operating modes to meet the requirements of different national railways; the selection of operating mode shall be configurable by authorised network management personnel and shall not be changeable by end users.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §4.4 explicitly states two configurable operating modes for link assurance; the previous critique identifies this as a missing requirement.

generated_requirements.text: When a shunting emergency call is received while the link assurance tone is active, the system shall immediately and automatically deactivate the link assurance signal and connect the shunting emergency call to all shunting group members; the shunting emergency call shall be receivable by all group members at any time.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §5.5 and §2 Objective 5 mandate automatic link assurance deactivation on emergency call receipt; the domain expert advisory requires this interaction to be explicitly specified.

generated_requirements.text: If a shunting group member who is transmitting the link assurance signal leaves the group or loses radio coverage, the system shall immediately cease the link assurance signal from that member and provide an indication to the shunting leader that the signal source has been lost.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert advisory identifies the member-departure-during-tone-transmission scenario as a required element of the link assurance interaction matrix.

generated_requirements.text: Only one member of the shunting group may transmit the link assurance signal at any time; the driver shall be prevented from transmitting the link assurance signal; only the shunting leader may communicate with parties external to the shunting group; non-leader members who attempt external calls shall have those attempts ignored by the system.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §5.5 defines these shunting group membership and transmission control rules as mandatory business rules.

generated_requirements.text: Shunting group membership shall be controlled by the shunting leader and protected against unintentional and unauthorised access; the functionality to enter or leave shunting mode shall not be available while there are ongoing calls involving the radio.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §5.5 and §7.3 mandate shunting group access control and the prohibition on mode changes during active calls.

generated_requirements.text: All cab radios shall be members of the following standard emergency groups as a minimum: Railway Emergency Call, All Train Drivers, and Shunting Team; mobiles with a railway emergency group call subscription shall be prevented from deactivating the emergency group subscription while operational.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §5.7 defines the mandatory minimum group memberships and the hard constraint against emergency group deactivation.

generated_requirements.text: Operational radios and general purpose radios used by trackside maintenance personnel shall be capable of receiving railway emergency calls at all times while operational, regardless of any other active service or call state.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §3.4 identifies emergency call reception as a primary need for maintenance personnel; the previous critique flags the absence of an explicit FR for this equipment type.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a standardised data bearer supporting a minimum data rate of 2.4 kbit/s for all data applications, including ERTMS/ETCS Level 2 and Level 3 train control communications.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §2 Objective 3 and §7.1 define 2.4 kbit/s as a non-negotiable minimum data rate floor for ERTMS/ETCS bearer support.

generated_requirements.text: The cab radio shall provide a standardised interface to the ERTMS/ETCS on-board unit; where both ERTMS/ETCS and radio systems are available, the train number shall be entered only once on either system and automatically transmitted to the other.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §5.9 and §2 Objective 3 mandate the single-entry train number rule and the standardised ETCS interface.

generated_requirements.text: When the cab radio enters emergency mode or executes a network change, the system shall maintain the ERTMS/ETCS RBC data session where technically possible; if the data session is interrupted, the system shall notify the ETCS on-board unit immediately, attempt re-establishment of the data session, and report the interruption duration and outcome to the train-borne recorder.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert advisory identifies ERTMS/ETCS data session continuity across mode transitions as a critical state-machine completeness requirement.

generated_requirements.text: The emergency call button on all cab radios and operational radios that support railway emergency calls shall be red in colour and physically protected against accidental activation.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §2 Objective 4, §5.4, and §7.2 mandate the red colour and accidental-activation protection as mandatory MMI requirements.

generated_requirements.text: All cab radios shall implement the standardised audible and visual indications specified in the CENELEC EN 50075 / EN 301 515 Standard for the Driver-Machine Interface for ERTMS, including all mandatory indications; national MMI variants are only permitted where the CENELEC standard is silent on a specific requirement.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §2 Objective 4 and §7.2 mandate full CENELEC DMI compliance as a non-negotiable constraint for all cab radios.

generated_requirements.text: Displayed characters on all cab radio and operational radio displays shall have a minimum height of 5 mm and shall be readable from a normal driving or operating position; display brightness and contrast shall be user-adjustable.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §2 Objective 4 and §7.1 define the 5 mm minimum character height and adjustable brightness/contrast as mandatory display requirements.

generated_requirements.text: All cab radios shall support a minimum of ten display languages; the specific languages supported by each national deployment shall be determined during national procurement.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §2 Objective 4 mandates ten-language minimum support to enable international driver operation.

generated_requirements.text: Operational radio displays shall support at least 6 lines of 20 characters.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §7.1 specifies the minimum display capacity for operational radios as a physical constraint.

generated_requirements.text: Cab radio physical installation shall be compatible with UIC Fiche 651 (cab equipment layout), ensuring no obstruction of the driver's forward vision and no impediment to safe driving.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §7.2 mandates UIC Fiche 651 compliance as a regulatory constraint on cab radio physical installation.

generated_requirements.text: Where a driver safety device (DSD) interface is implemented on a cab radio, it shall be electrically and functionally compatible with UIC Fiche 641 (automatic vigilance devices for international traffic).
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §7.2 and §8.1 mandate UIC Fiche 641 compatibility for the DSD interface as a regulatory dependency.

generated_requirements.text: All EIRENE mobile equipment shall operate exclusively in the 876–880 MHz (uplink) and 921–925 MHz (downlink) GSM-R frequency bands allocated for railway use under ECC Decision (02)05, and in the public GSM 900 MHz band where roaming is permitted; equipment shall not be designed for or operate in any other frequency bands.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §7.1 and the domain expert advisory define the GSM-R frequency bands as a hard technical constraint with no permissible deviation.

generated_requirements.text: EIRENE equipment shall not cause harmful interference to any of the following railway-critical systems: signalling relays, track circuits, axle counters, train describers, radar speed measurement equipment, switched-mode power supplies, telecommunications circuits, or electronic locking systems; cab radios shall meet a more stringent EMC class than general purpose or operational radios.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §7.1 and the domain expert advisory mandate EMC compliance against specific railway-critical systems, with a stricter class for cab radios.

generated_requirements.text: Equipment installed in rolling stock shall be demonstrated to be unaffected by pressure pulses generated by trains entering, leaving, or passing through tunnels, in accordance with the relevant European Norm for rolling stock environmental conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §7.1 and the domain expert advisory mandate pressure pulse resilience as a hard environmental constraint for rolling stock equipment.

generated_requirements.text: Cab radios shall operate for a minimum of 1 hour on battery backup following train power failure, with a usage profile of 20% point-to-point calls, 5% group calls, and 75% standby; as battery charge falls, services shall be degraded in the following priority order: (1) data applications suspended first, (2) group call capability suspended second, (3) point-to-point voice maintained until battery exhaustion; railway emergency call capability shall be the last service suspended and shall remain available at the point of battery exhaustion.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §7.1 defines the 1-hour battery minimum and usage profile; the domain expert advisory requires an explicit service degradation priority order.

generated_requirements.text: General purpose radios shall provide a minimum of 8 hours of operation from a single charge with a usage profile of 20% point-to-point calls, 5% group calls, and the remainder standby; operational radios shall provide a minimum of 8 hours from a single charge with a usage profile of 20% point-to-point calls, 60% group calls, and the remainder standby.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §7.1 specifies the 8-hour battery minimum and distinct usage profiles for general purpose and operational radios.

generated_requirements.text: General purpose radios including battery shall not exceed 250 g in total weight; operational radios including battery shall not exceed 800 g in total weight.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §7.1 defines the weight limits as physical constraints directly affecting portability and usability for field personnel.

generated_requirements.text: Operational radios shall be operable by a user wearing standard railway work gloves, with all safety-critical controls — including the emergency call button and PTT — accessible and actuable without removing gloves.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §3.4 identifies glove operability as a primary need for trackside maintenance personnel; the previous critique flags the absence of an explicit usability requirement for this.

generated_requirements.text: The network shall provide radio coverage for vehicle-mounted equipment with an external antenna at a minimum of 95% of the time over 95% of the designated coverage area.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §2 Objective 2 defines the 95%/95% coverage standard as a measurable success criterion for network availability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be capable of supporting simultaneous ERTMS/ETCS data communications for a high density of trains in a given area without degrading voice call set-up time SLAs or emergency call performance.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §2 Objective 3 requires high-density simultaneous data support; the domain expert advisory identifies concurrent service interaction under congestion as a critical concern.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an overall network availability such that safety-critical communication services — including railway emergency calls, control-command calls, and shunting link assurance — are available at all times during operational hours; planned maintenance windows shall not reduce availability of emergency call services below the 95%/95% coverage standard.
generated_requirements.rationale: The previous critique identifies the absence of an overall system availability NFR as a notable gap given the safety-critical nature of the system.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support point-to-point voice calls (full duplex), broadcast voice calls (one-way from a single user to a pre-defined group), group voice calls (multi-user PTT within a pre-defined area), multi-party voice calls supporting up to six simultaneous parties, and public emergency calls including 112.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §6.1 defines these as the mandatory in-scope voice service types that every EIRENE network deployment must support.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support text messaging (point-to-point and point-to-multipoint, ground-to-mobile and mobile-to-ground), general data applications (including timetable information, maintenance diagnostics, e-mail, and remote database access), and optionally automatic fax between ground and mobile users; the text message facility shall not interfere with the ability of users to make or receive high-priority voice or data calls, and where fax is provided it shall be interruptible for high-priority calls.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §6.1 and §5.10 define the mandatory data and messaging services and the non-interference rule with high-priority calls.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display the calling and called party identity (telephone number and functional description) to the user; optional restriction of identity display shall be supported; optional display of charging information shall be supported where configured by the network operator.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §6.1 lists caller identity display, identity restriction, and optional charging display as in-scope call-related services; the previous critique flags the charging display as a missing requirement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support call forwarding (unconditional and on-busy as mandatory; on-no-reply and not-reachable as optional), call hold and re-join, and call waiting notification; during call hold, the held call shall remain subject to the priority pre-emption rules, and if a railway emergency call is received while a call is held, the held call shall be terminated and the emergency call connected automatically.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §6.1 defines these call-related services; the previous critique identifies the need to specify call hold behaviour under priority pre-emption.

generated_requirements.text: Network management shall provide the capability to manage individual subscriber profiles, including adding and removing service subscriptions, modifying service entitlements per user, configuring call barring rules (outgoing and incoming, by destination/originating network type, number type, or specific number), and managing functional number registrations and deregistrations; any call barring implementation shall not affect international interoperability.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §6.1 and §5.8 define subscription profile management and call barring configuration as in-scope network management functions; the previous critique flags subscription profile management as a missing FR.

generated_requirements.text: All design, testing, and installation of EIRENE mobile radio equipment shall be conducted under a quality management system certified to ISO 9001; procurement contracts shall verify ISO 9001 certification as a mandatory condition of supply.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §5.12 and §7.2 mandate ISO 9001 compliance for all equipment design, testing, and installation as a regulatory quality constraint.

generated_requirements.text: Equipment design shall comply with relevant European Norms for protection against physical and environmental hazards including pollution, chemical contaminants, flammable substances, biologically active substances, dust, sand, mould growth, and other environmental hazards; national environmental standards that are stricter than EIRENE requirements shall not prevent the use of EIRENE-compliant mobiles from other countries in that national network.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §5.12 and §7.2 mandate European Norm environmental compliance and prohibit national standards from blocking cross-border equipment use.

generated_requirements.text: The cab radio shall define and implement a complete state machine covering all valid operating mode transitions — normal, shunting, emergency, and roaming/network-change — explicitly specifying for each transition: which active services are maintained, suspended, or terminated; the behaviour of any active ERTMS/ETCS data session; the behaviour of any active link assurance signal; and the indications provided to the driver.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert advisory identifies state-machine completeness across all mode transitions as a critical requirement pattern to prevent integration testing failures.

generated_requirements.text: The mandatory minimum service set defined in the EIRENE specification shall be implemented in every UIC standard network deployment; no national railway may implement a subset of the mandatory services, though additional national services beyond the mandatory minimum are permitted.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §7.3 defines the minimum service set as a non-negotiable deployment constraint to ensure pan-European interoperability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support location-dependent addressing, automatically routing calls from controllers to the appropriate cab radio based on the current geographical location of the mobile within the network.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §6.1 lists location-dependent addressing as an in-scope railway-specific service required for correct controller-to-train call routing.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support an optional direct mode (off-network, short-range communications) for use when network coverage is unavailable; direct mode shall not be activatable while the radio is registered on a network and has active safety-critical calls.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §6.1 lists optional direct mode as an in-scope railway-specific service; the operational constraint on activation is implied by the safety-critical call priority rules.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a driver safety device (DSD) interface on cab radios; DSD alarm events shall be transmitted to the train-borne recorder in real time and shall be reported to the controlling authority via the network.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §6.1 lists the DSD interface as an in-scope railway-specific service; BRD §3.11 requires DSD alarm events to be captured by the train-borne recorder.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a primary controller MMI providing: call queuing with priority display, group call management, text messaging, and the ability to initiate and receive calls to and from any train within the control area using the train's functional number; secondary and power supply controller MMIs shall be supported with their respective addressing and communication capabilities.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §6.1 and §3.2 define the controller MMI types and their core capabilities as in-scope requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a standardised international numbering plan for key functions including railway emergency calls, controller routing, ERTMS/ETCS RBC routing, and public emergency (112); functional addressing shall cover train numbers, engine/coach numbers, shunting team numbers, maintenance team numbers, and controller numbers.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §6.1 defines the standardised numbering plan and functional addressing categories as in-scope requirements for interoperability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support group call service area configuration, allowing network management to define pre-defined local areas for group and broadcast calls; group call area definitions at network boundaries shall be coordinated through bilateral agreements between adjacent network operators.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §6.1 and §8.2 define group call area configuration as an in-scope network management function with a bilateral agreement dependency.

generated_requirements.text: The requirement for cab radios entering a group call area to automatically join an ongoing call to all drivers in the same area (BRD requirement 3.5.7) is noted as pending further technical specification and shall not be implemented in the initial deployment; this requirement shall be flagged as deferred and tracked for inclusion in a future release.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §8.2 explicitly identifies requirement 3.5.7 as incomplete and dependent on future technical work, requiring it to be flagged as deferred rather than omitted.

generated_requirements.text: Mobile equipment shall function correctly across the full operational speed range of the railway, including when stationary and when travelling at the maximum allowable line speed for the network on which it is operating.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD §7.1 mandates correct operation across the full speed range as a hard technical constraint for all mobile equipment.

domain: railway telecommunications and train control systems
sme_subject: GSM-R railway radio communications engineer and ERTMS/ETCS systems integrator
sme_advisory: The EIRENE FRS sits at the intersection of GSM-R bearer technology, ERTMS/ETCS train control, and safety-critical railway operations, meaning requirements must be traceable to both telecommunications standards (ETSI GSM-R, 3GPP) and railway safety norms (EN 50126/50128/50129, CENELEC). The requirements engineer must be especially careful to capture the precise quantitative performance envelopes (call set-up times, coverage percentages, battery durations, data rate floors) as hard, testable acceptance criteria rather than aspirational targets, since these values are contractually and safety-critically binding. Particular attention is needed on the interaction effects between concurrent services — for example, how simultaneous ERTMS/ETCS data sessions, active group calls, and link assurance tones interact under radio congestion — and on the state-machine behaviour of the cab radio across mode transitions (normal, shunting, emergency, roaming), which are a common source of specification gaps that only surface during integration testing.

ground_truth_requirements.text: 2.1.1 The network services necessary to meet the range of UIC requirements are detailed below. These services are to be considered as a minimum set for implementation within each UIC standard network. Railways may implement additional network services as desired. ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 2.2.6 Such emergency calls include 112 calls and may not be used for railway emergencies. (I) Broadcast voice calls 2.2.6 Such emergency calls include 112 calls and may not be used for railway emergencies. (I) Broadcast voice calls
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 4.3.1 This sub-section defines the core environmental and physical requirements for all EIRENE mobile equipment. The requirements provided in this section are augmented by those provided in later sections for each individual radio type, with each radio type being specified by the superset of the core plus specific requirements. (I) 4.3.1 This sub-section defines the core environmental and physical requirements for all EIRENE mobile equipment. The requirements provided in this section are augmented by those provided in later sections for each individual radio type, with each radio type being specified by the superset of the core plus specific requirements. (I)
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 4.3.2 The categories of requirements defined for each type of mobile equipment are as follows: (I) 4.3.2 The categories of requirements defined for each type of mobile equipment are as follows: (I) climatic conditions (temperature, humidity, solar radiation, altitude, etc); physical conditions (flammability, contamination, physical protection, etc); mechanical conditions (shock and vibration); electrical conditions (power supply variation, battery life, overloading, etc); EMC (both emissions and immunity).
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 4.3.3 In normal operation of a mobile radio unit, it is expected that a combination of the above environmental conditions will be experienced. (I) 4.3.3 In normal operation of a mobile radio unit, it is expected that a combination of the above environmental conditions will be experienced. (I)
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 4.3.4 Any environmental and physical requirements stated may be superseded by national requirements if the national standards provide a higher level of environmental and physical protection. However, stricter national standards shall not prevent the use of other EIRENE mobiles in that country. (M) 4.3.4 Any environmental and physical requirements stated may be superseded by national requirements if the national standards provide a higher level of environmental and physical protection. However, stricter national standards shall not prevent the use of other EIRENE mobiles in that country. (M)
