ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

Mechanical conditions

ground_truth_requirements.text: 4.3.8 All EIRENE mobile equipment shall be capable of being operated on board rolling stock. (M) Electrical 4.3.8 All EIRENE mobile equipment shall be capable of being operated on board rolling stock. (M) Electrical

ground_truth_requirements.text: 4.3.10 EIRENE mobiles will generate electromagnetic emissions. It is the responsibility of national railways operating EIRENE networks to ensure that EIRENE equipment does not interfere with the normal operation of any on-train or ground-based systems. It is the responsibility of national railways operating EIRENE networks to ensure that EIRENE equipment does not interfere with the normal operation of any on-train or ground-based systems. (I)

ground_truth_requirements.text: 5.2.1.1 In this subsection, the functions to be provided by the Cab radio are described. (I) 5.2.1.1 In this subsection, the functions to be provided by the Cab radio are described. (I)

ground_truth_requirements.text: 5.2.1.2 The following functions will be provided: Driver call-related functions: 5.2.1.2 The following functions will be provided: Driver call-related functions: call controller: (M) primary controller; secondary controller; power supply controller; call other drivers in the area; (M) send Railway emergency call; (M) confirm receipt of Railway emergency call; (M) communicate with other drivers on same train; (M) call train staff; (M) call other authorised users; (M) receive incoming voice calls; (M) terminate calls; (M) receive text messages; (M) enter/leave shunting mode; (M) enter/leave direct mode; (O) monitor calls to other on-train users/devices; (M) forward calls/cancel call forwarding to/from driver handheld; (O) Other driver-related functions: powering up radio; (M) switch radio MMI on and off; (M) select language; (M) adjust loudspeaker volume; (M) select mobile radio network; (M) register and deregister train number; (M) register and deregister on-train users; (O) register and deregister stock number; (M) store/retrieve numbers and their details; (M) invoke supplementary services; (M) invoke tests; (M) Other Cab radio functions: automatic connection of incoming calls to appropriate on-train users or devices (conductor, public address system, data systems, etc); (M) automatic establishment of outgoing calls initiated by on-train users or devices; (M) automatic handling of calls of varying priorities; (M) send to the controller(s) a signal on activation of driver safety device; (M) transmit Railway emergency call event indication to train-borne recorder; (M) run-time diagnostics. (O)
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 7.2.1.2 The following functions will be provided: Call related functions 7.2.1.2 The following functions will be provided: Call related functions call authorised users; (M) call controller; (M) send Railway emergency calls; (M) receive Railway emergency calls; (M) receive incoming calls; (M) group and broadcast calls; (M) terminate calls; (M) shunting mode communications; (O) enter/leave direct mode; (O) Other functions switch radio on/off; (M) select language; (M) select mobile radio network; (M) adjust loudspeaker volume; (M) register and deregister functional number; (M) store/retrieve numbers and their details; (M) computer interface. (O)

ground_truth_requirements.text: 7.2.2.2 It shall be possible for the user of an Operational radio to establish a call with each of the following types of controller (who are not permanently included in a shunting group) with a minimum number of actions being required: 7.2.2.2 It shall be possible for the user of an Operational radio to establish a call with each of the following types of controller (who are not permanently included in a shunting group) with a minimum number of actions being required: primary controller; (M) secondary controller; (M) power supply controller. (O)
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 7.2.2.3 For some users, particularly for shunting team leaders, up to twenty valid numbers may be stored for one controller type. In these cases, the identities of the controllers corresponding to these valid numbers shall be displayed with an audible and visual prompt for one to be selected. The number of the controller selected shall then be used to establish the call. If no selection is made by the user within 10 seconds the radio shall abandon the call attempt. (O) 7.2.2.3 For some users, particularly for shunting team leaders, up to twenty valid numbers may be stored for one controller type. ground_truth_requirements.text: 15.1.1 The operational requirement for direct mode is to: (I) 15.1.1 The operational requirement for direct mode is to: (I) provide short range fall-back communications between train drivers and trackside personnel in the event of failure of mobile telephony services normally available; provide short range communications for railway personnel operating in remote areas where no mobile telephony facilities are available.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 15.2.1 Implementation of direct mode is optional. Where the facility is provided, the following requirements are mandatory. (O) 15.2.1 Implementation of direct mode is optional. Where the facility is provided, the following requirements are mandatory. (O)
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 15.2.2 Principal requirements are: (M see above) 15.2.2 Principal requirements are: (M see above) a range of up to at least 2000m in open terrain between a direct mode transmitter and receiver; a voice only capability that supports the use of the link assurance signal; open channel mode of operation such that all users employing direct mode receive transmissions when in range of the transmitting user; it is sufficient that the user may talk or listen only, but not both. The ability to talk shall be achieved through use of the Push-To-Talk function; a minimum of one channel is to be available for use of direct mode facilities; if more than one channel is provided, there must be a function allowing the user to manually select the channel; where equipment provides both normal and direct mode capability, the user shall be able to switch between the two modes in a straightforward manner (but not by accident); the user may only select direct mode when the normal mobile telephony services are not available; controls shall be simple to use, eg: direct mode on/off switch; Push-To-Talk function; channel selection; volume control; battery warning for handportables; battery life for handportables operating in direct mode shall be a minimum of 8 hours based on the following cycle: 20% transmit; 20% receive; 60% stand-by; when direct mode is used as a fall-back, an audible and visual indication shall be provided of normal mobile telephony services returning.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

generated_requirements.text: The ETCS onboard equipment shall support operation at all four application levels: Level 0 (unfitted), Level 1 (balise-based), Level 2 (radio-based with balise position reference), and Level 3 (radio-based with onboard train integrity). A train equipped for Level N shall be capable of operating on Level N and all levels below N.
generated_requirements.rationale: Directly derived from BR-L01 and C-INT01; establishes the fundamental interoperability hierarchy across all ETCS application levels.

generated_requirements.text: When a train enters a section of line fitted with multiple ETCS application levels, the onboard shall automatically select the highest application level for which it is equipped, in accordance with the priority order specified by the trackside, without requiring driver intervention.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from BR-L02 and C-INT02; ensures automatic optimisation of supervision level without adding driver workload.

generated_requirements.text: If the onboard receives a command to transition to an ETCS application level for which it is not equipped, it shall immediately apply the emergency brake and display an appropriate indication on the DMI.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from BR-L03; prevents unsafe operation on a level the onboard cannot supervise.

generated_requirements.text: The current ETCS application level shall be continuously displayed to the driver on the DMI in a dedicated display zone, consistent with the layout and colour coding defined in Subset-034.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from BR-L04 and C-TECH05; ensures the driver is always aware of the active supervision level.

generated_requirements.text: When a level transition requires driver acknowledgement, the onboard shall apply the brakes automatically if the driver does not acknowledge within the defined acknowledgement window. The brake shall be releasable upon subsequent driver acknowledgement.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from BR-L05 and BR-M04; enforces timely driver awareness of supervision level changes.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard shall implement the complete ETCS operational mode state machine as defined in Subset-026 §4, supporting all valid modes including: Full Supervision (FS), Partial Supervision (PS), Staff Responsible (SR), On Sight (OS), Unfitted (UN), Train Trip (TR), Post Trip (PT), National (NL/STM), Tandem (TAN), Multiple (MUL), Shunting (SH), Stand By (SB), and Reversing (RV). All forbidden mode transitions shall be prevented by the onboard.
generated_requirements.rationale: Establishes completeness of the mode state machine; forbidden transitions are a direct safety requirement per Subset-026 §4.

generated_requirements.text: Any mode transition that increases driver responsibility (except transitions to/from National Operation via STM) shall require explicit driver acknowledgement, regardless of whether the train is moving or stationary. If acknowledgement is not given, the onboard shall apply the brakes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from BR-M02 and BR-M04; ensures the driver is always aware when their supervisory burden increases.

generated_requirements.text: Transitions to and from National Operation mode (STM) shall always require explicit driver acknowledgement. If acknowledgement is not given within the defined window, the onboard shall apply the brakes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from BR-M03; STM transitions involve a change in the supervising system and must be consciously confirmed by the driver.

generated_requirements.text: During any transition period between two operational modes, the supervision applied by the onboard shall be at least as restrictive as the least restrictive of the two modes involved, until the transition is complete.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from BR-M05; prevents any gap in safety supervision during mode transitions.

generated_requirements.text: When transitioning from Full Supervision to Partial Supervision and driver acknowledgement is required, the onboard shall apply the brakes automatically if the driver does not acknowledge within 5 seconds of the acknowledgement request being displayed on the DMI.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from BR-M10 and the KPI table; the 5-second window is a safety-critical timing constraint.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard shall enter Full Supervision mode automatically when a complete movement authority and all necessary train and infrastructure data are received from the trackside, without requiring driver action.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from BR-M11; automatic entry to FS removes a potential source of human error.

generated_requirements.text: Transfer to Shunting mode by driver selection shall only be permitted when the train is stationary. If operating under RBC control, the onboard shall require and display explicit RBC permission before allowing the transfer. Exit from Shunting mode shall only be possible when the train is stationary.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from BR-M06, BR-M07, and BR-M09; prevents unsafe mode changes during movement.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard shall complete a fully automatic self-test at start-up (Start of Mission) requiring no driver action. The self-test shall verify the integrity of all safety-critical onboard functions before permitting any train movement. The result shall be displayed on the DMI.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from C-OPS01 and the SoM procedure in Subset-026 §3.3; ensures equipment integrity before service.

generated_requirements.text: During the Start of Mission procedure, the onboard shall present previously stored train data to the driver for confirmation before permitting movement. Manual entry or overwriting of train data shall only be permitted when the train is stationary. Train data shall be retained until the traction unit is taken out of service, except on transition to Shunting mode.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from BR-D01 through BR-D04; ensures train data integrity and prevents unsafe operation with incorrect data.

generated_requirements.text: The Train Running Number entered or received by the onboard shall be a maximum of 8 numeric digits. The Driver ID and Train Running Number shall be modifiable during a journey without requiring the train to be stationary.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from BR-D05, BR-D06, and C-TECH08; standardises train identification across all networks.

generated_requirements.text: The speed value displayed to the driver on the DMI shall be identical to the speed value used by the onboard for supervision purposes. No discrepancy between displayed speed and supervised speed shall be permitted under any operating condition.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from BR-S01 and the KPI table; a discrepancy between displayed and supervised speed is a direct safety hazard.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard shall provide the driver with a warning at least 5 seconds before any ETCS brake intervention within a braking curve area (Target Speed Monitoring). The warning shall be displayed on the DMI and accompanied by an audible alert.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from BR-S02 and the KPI table; the 5-second lead time is a safety-critical timing constraint allowing driver reaction.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard shall implement four distinct speed supervision monitoring functions: Pre-Indication Monitor (PIM), Target Speed Monitor (TSM), Ceiling Speed Monitor (CSM), and Release Speed Monitor (RSM). Each function shall have independently specified intervention thresholds, warning behaviours, and brake command outputs consistent with Subset-026 §3.13.
generated_requirements.rationale: Addresses the domain expert advisory on CSM/TSM distinction; these are separate safety functions with different trigger conditions.

generated_requirements.text: If the train speed exceeds the permitted ceiling speed by the harmonised permitted overspeed margin, the onboard shall apply a brake intervention. The driver shall be permitted to release the brake only once actual train speed no longer exceeds the permitted ceiling speed.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from BR-S03; enforces the ceiling speed supervision function.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard shall calculate both an emergency braking curve and a service braking curve using train characteristic data and infrastructure data (gradients, speed restrictions, target locations). Where the service brake is detected as insufficient to stop the train before the danger point, the onboard shall automatically apply the emergency brake.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from BR-S04, BR-S05, and BR-S06; ensures the most appropriate brake is always applied to prevent overrun.

generated_requirements.text: All position and speed values used in supervision and braking curve calculations shall be worst-case values that account for the full odometry error envelope (both over-reading and under-reading), as specified in Subset-026 §3.6 and Subset-041. The odometry error model shall be agreed with the vehicle manufacturer and validated before system acceptance.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from C-TECH07 and the domain expert advisory; failure to account for odometry error leads to unsafe braking curve calculations.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard shall validate all received balise telegrams, including: consistency checks between linked and unlinked balises, reaction to unexpected balises, reaction to missing linked balises (linking reaction), and rejection of balise groups received out of sequence. Validation failures shall result in a defined safe response and DMI indication.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from the domain expert advisory on balise message validation; these checks prevent spoofing and data corruption from affecting supervision.

generated_requirements.text: If track data covering at least the full extent of the movement authority is not available onboard, the movement authority shall be rejected by the onboard. Movement authority time-outs for defined sections shall be enforced onboard; expiry of a time-out shall shorten the movement authority to the expired section boundary.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from BR-A01 and BR-A02; prevents the train from operating beyond the limits of known track data.

generated_requirements.text: Movement authority revocation shall be cooperative: if the onboard determines that the train cannot stop at the proposed new end of authority, it shall reject the revocation and retain the original movement authority, reporting the rejection to the RBC.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from BR-A03; prevents unsafe revocation that would require the train to stop in an impossible distance.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard shall continuously monitor the direction of train movement against the permitted direction. After the train travels a distance in an unauthorised direction exceeding the value defined by the applicable national value, the onboard shall automatically apply the emergency brake and display a roll-away/reverse movement indication on the DMI. The emergency brake shall only be releasable after the train is stationary. Roll-away protection shall be active only on the leading traction unit.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from BR-R01 through BR-R05; roll-away protection is a primary safety function preventing uncontrolled movement.

generated_requirements.text: When a traction unit passes a stop signal (train trip condition), the onboard shall immediately apply the emergency brake. The emergency brake shall remain applied until the traction unit is stationary. The driver shall acknowledge the train trip condition before the emergency brake is released. After acknowledgement, the driver may move the train backwards for a distance defined by the applicable national value.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from BR-T01 through BR-T04; the train trip function is a fundamental automatic train protection mechanism.

generated_requirements.text: The train trip function shall be automatically suppressed on all non-leading traction units in multiple-unit and tandem traction configurations. The train trip function shall be applicable to shunting movements passing a signal displaying 'danger for shunting'.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from BR-T05 and BR-T06; prevents spurious emergency brake applications on non-leading units while retaining shunting safety.

generated_requirements.text: The override control for passing a stop signal under authorisation shall be physically or logically protected against inadvertent activation. When the override is active and the train passes the stop signal, the train trip function shall be suppressed, actual speed shall remain displayed, a special override indication shall be shown on the DMI, and the supervised speed shall not be displayed.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from BR-O01 and BR-O02; the override is a safety-critical control requiring deliberate activation.

generated_requirements.text: When the ETCS onboard is isolated, it shall be electrically and logically disconnected from the vehicle braking system. In the isolated state, the DMI shall display only the isolation status; no other ETCS information shall be displayed.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from BR-O04; isolation must be complete to prevent spurious brake commands and to clearly indicate the system is not supervising.

generated_requirements.text: National values received from the trackside shall be applied by the onboard within the geographic area for which they are valid. National values from a previous area shall not persist into a new area once new values are received. If no valid national values exist for the current location, harmonised default values permanently stored in the onboard equipment shall be used. Default values shall not be modifiable by operators or drivers. National values shall be retained through onboard equipment power-off cycles.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from BR-N01 through BR-N03, C-TECH03, C-TECH04, and the domain expert advisory on national values precedence.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard shall record all data entered, received, or indicated to the driver, referenced to UTC time (resolution ≤ 1 second) and a geographic reference point. The minimum recorded data set shall include: level and mode transitions, driver confirmations, supervision data, national system information, actual speed, service brake interventions, emergency brake interventions, train trip activations, override selections, route suitability overrides, isolation events, and all data entered, received, or displayed.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from BR-REC01 and BR-REC05; defines the mandatory content of the onboard data record.

generated_requirements.text: Accident investigation data shall be retained in onboard non-volatile storage for a minimum of 24 hours at high granularity. Operational and driver performance data shall be retained for a minimum of 7 days. Recorded data shall be exportable via standardised output interfaces consistent with Subset-027.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from BR-REC02 through BR-REC04; meets regulatory obligations for post-incident analysis.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard data recording system shall implement a tamper-evident mechanism such that any modification or deletion of recorded data after the fact can be detected. The tamper-evidence mechanism shall be verifiable by an accident investigation body using the standardised output interface.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from BO-07 and the domain expert advisory on data integrity; tamper-evidence is a regulatory requirement for accident investigation data.

generated_requirements.text: In Levels 2 and 3, the onboard shall send position reports to the RBC under all of the following trigger conditions: periodically at the interval specified by the RBC, on occurrence of defined events (mode change, level change, train trip, emergency brake application), on explicit request from the RBC, and on establishment of a new radio session. Each position report shall include: train position with confidence interval, direction of movement, train speed, and train integrity status.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from the domain expert advisory on position report completeness; omitting trigger conditions causes RBC integration failures.

generated_requirements.text: In Level 3, the onboard shall determine and report the safe rear-end position of the train. Train integrity shall be reported to the RBC based on onboard sensor data or driver confirmation. If train integrity cannot be confirmed, the onboard shall assume integrity is lost, report this to the RBC, and apply the supervision response defined for loss of integrity.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from the domain expert advisory on safe consist length and train integrity; critical for Level 3 where track circuits are absent.

generated_requirements.text: In Levels 2 and 3, the onboard shall establish and maintain a radio session with the RBC using the Euroradio protocol (Subset-037). The onboard shall reject out-of-sequence and duplicate messages. On loss of radio session, the onboard shall respond according to the applicable national value: (a) apply emergency brake with DMI indication, (b) apply full service brake with DMI indication, or (c) proceed unrestricted to end of movement authority with DMI indication and driver notification.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from BR-F01 and the domain expert advisory on RBC communication; safe message handling and session loss response are primary safety scenarios.

generated_requirements.text: RBC handover between adjacent RBC areas shall occur automatically without driver intervention. With two operational radio units, RBC handover shall impose no performance penalty on train spacing or speed. With one operational radio unit, RBC handover shall remain possible but may result in a performance penalty, which shall be indicated on the DMI.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from BR-RBC01 through BR-RBC03; seamless RBC handover is essential for uninterrupted high-speed operation.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard shall interface with one or more Specific Transmission Modules (STMs) via the interface defined in Subset-035. The onboard shall pass national system data received from the STM to the driver via the DMI, including the ability to replicate the national system's own display format where required.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from C-INT03 and the STM stakeholder need; STM integration is the primary mechanism for backward compatibility with national systems.

generated_requirements.text: The DMI shall conform to the layout, display zones, colour coding, and audio/visual alert sequences defined in Subset-034. Fixed text messages shall be rendered in the driver's selected language. Plain text messages received from the trackside shall be displayed as received. The DMI shall support all European languages required by the CCS TSI.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from C-TECH05 and the domain expert advisory on DMI conformance; standardised DMI is essential for cross-border driver interoperability.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard shall issue service brake and emergency brake commands to the vehicle braking system via the defined brake interface. The onboard shall monitor brake application feedback signals. If confirmed brake application is not received within the maximum permissible latency defined in the brake interface specification, the onboard shall escalate to emergency brake application and display a brake failure indication on the DMI.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from the domain expert advisory on brake interface and feedback; brake command confirmation is a SIL-4 interface requirement.

generated_requirements.text: The Reversing function shall only be operable from a single active cab that remains open throughout the reversing procedure. Reversing shall be supervised to speed and distance limits defined by the applicable national values, extendable by trackside instruction. Once reversing commences, the existing movement authority shall be cancelled. The driver shall not be required to re-confirm train data to use the Reversing function.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from BR-REV01 through BR-REV04; defines the complete set of constraints on the Reversing mode.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard shall receive, process, and display to the driver infrastructure-commanded information including: pantograph lowering/raising commands, power supply change commands, air tightness commands, and regenerative, eddy current, and magnetic shoe brake inhibition commands.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from the in-scope items for pantograph, power supply, air tightness, and brake inhibition information.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard shall support the Start of Mission procedure as defined in Subset-026 §3.3, including: defined behaviour when train position cannot be determined (no balise received), defined behaviour when stored position conflicts with a received balise position, and defined conditions under which the onboard may permit movement before position is confirmed.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from the domain expert advisory on SoM completeness; incomplete SoM handling is a common source of certification findings.

generated_requirements.text: Where Level 1 with infill (via Euroloop or radio infill) is implemented, the onboard shall apply defined priority and override rules between balise-received data and infill-received data. When infill data conflicts with the last received balise data, the onboard shall apply the more restrictive value and display an appropriate indication on the DMI.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from the domain expert advisory on Euroloop and infill handling; conflict resolution between data sources is a safety-critical function.

generated_requirements.text: All safety-critical functions in the onboard equipment — from odometry input through supervision calculation to brake command output — shall be designed and verified to achieve Safety Integrity Level 4 (SIL 4) as defined in EN 50129, with a Tolerable Hazard Rate (THR) not exceeding 10⁻⁹ per hour for catastrophic hazard categories. The SIL allocation shall be formally derived and agreed with the National Safety Authority before detailed design commences.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from C-REG04 and the domain expert advisory on SIL allocation; the brake command chain is explicitly required to achieve SIL 4.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard software shall be developed in accordance with EN 50128 (Railway Applications — Communication, Signalling and Processing Systems — Software for Railway Control and Protection Systems) at the software safety integrity level consistent with the SIL 4 system requirement. The software development lifecycle, including verification and validation evidence, shall be documented in a safety case conforming to EN 50129.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from C-REG04; EN 50128 compliance is a mandatory regulatory constraint for railway safety software.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard equipment shall be functional across the full operational speed range of 0 to 500 km/h. All supervision calculations, brake curve computations, and DMI updates shall complete within their defined real-time deadlines at the maximum operational speed of 500 km/h.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from C-TECH01 and BO-06; real-time determinism at maximum speed is a safety-critical performance requirement.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard equipment shall conform to the Control-Command and Signalling Technical Specification for Interoperability (CCS TSI) as published by the European Union Agency for Railways (ERA), and shall be certifiable by a recognised Notified Body under the EU railway interoperability framework. All functional requirements shall be traceable to a specific clause in Subset-026 and to the applicable Essential Requirement of CCS TSI Annex III.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from C-REG01 and C-REG03; traceability to TSI and Subset-026 is a mandatory condition for Notified Body certification.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard equipment shall be designed for maintainability and testability: it shall perform a fully automatic self-test at power-on, provide clear failure indications on the DMI and via a maintenance interface, support isolation without tools, and provide a defined isolation procedure that disconnects the equipment from the braking system. All failure modes shall be detectable and shall result in a defined safe state.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from the Fleet Maintenance Organisation stakeholder need and C-OPS01; testability and clear failure indication reduce maintenance burden and downtime.

generated_requirements.text: The onboard data recording system shall ensure data integrity such that recorded data cannot be corrupted by power loss, equipment failure, or normal operational vibration. Non-volatile storage used for national values and recorded data shall retain data through a minimum of the number of power-off cycles expected over the equipment's operational lifetime, as defined in the equipment specification.
generated_requirements.rationale: Derived from C-TECH03 and BR-REC02; data integrity through power cycles is essential for both operational continuity and accident investigation.

sme_subject: ERTMS/ETCS certified railway signalling engineer with CCS TSI and Subset-026 expertise
sme_advisory: This BRD is well-structured and closely mirrors the Subset-026 (SRS v3.x) functional decomposition, but requirements engineers must be careful not to simply paraphrase the BRD — they must derive testable, atomic, unambiguous requirements traceable to specific Subset-026 clauses and CCS TSI Essential Requirements. Key areas of risk are: (1) the boundary between onboard and trackside responsibilities, particularly for braking curve authority and MA management; (2) the precise SIL allocation across the onboard functional chain (odometry → supervision → brake command), which must be consistent with EN 50129 safety case arguments; and (3) the handling of degraded and failure modes, which are underspecified in most BRDs but are the primary source of safety incidents and certification findings. Requirements engineers should structure elicitation around the Subset-026 functional block decomposition (Chapters 3–12) and ensure every mode transition in the ETCS mode state machine (Subset-026 §4) is covered by at least one requirement, including all 'forbidden' transitions.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The following definitions shall apply for the ETCS application levels: The following definitions shall apply for the ETCS application levels: Level 0: ETCS active for limited train control function; trackside not fitted with any train control system or fitted with a train control system for which no STM is available onboard. Level 1: Basic track to train information via intermittent transmission media, e.g. balises. This information can be supported by infill, transmitted via balise, loop or radio. Level 2: Basic track to train and train to track information via continuous transmission media, i.e. radio. The train detection is provided by trackside. Level 3: Same as level 2 except that train integrity is provided by onboard and therefore trackside. train detection is optional. Level STM (Specific Transmission Module): Track to train information provided by national system. Onboard functions provided by national system (STM) in co-operation with onboard ETCS.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: It shall be possible to implement one or more of the ETCS application levels on a line. It shall be possible to implement one or more of the ETCS application levels on a line.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Trains equipped for ERTMS/ETCS application level 3 shall be able to run on lines equipped with ERTMS/ETCS application level 3, 2, 1 and 0, trains equipped for ERTMS/ETCS application level 2 shall be able to run on lines equipped with ERTMS/ETCS application level 2, 1 and 0 and trains equipped for ERTMS/ETCS application level 1 shall be able to run on lines equipped with ERTMS/ETCS application level 1 and 0. Trains equipped for ERTMS/ETCS application level 3 shall be able to run on lines equipped with ERTMS/ETCS application level 3, 2, 1 and 0, trains equipped for ERTMS/ETCS application level 2 shall be able to run on lines equipped with ERTMS/ETCS application level 2, 1 and 0 and trains equipped for ERTMS/ETCS application level 1 shall be able to run on lines equipped with ERTMS/ETCS application level 1 and 0.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The current application level shall be indicated on the DMI. The current application level shall be indicated on the DMI.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The driver shall acknowledge the level transitions, if requested from trackside. If the driver does not acknowledge after the transition the brake shall be applied. If the driver does not acknowledge after the transition the brake shall be applied. If the driver acknowledges afterwards, the brake can be released

generated_requirements.rationale: BR-06 and TC-01 mandate .kdb exclusivity as both a technical and compliance boundary that prevents substitution attacks via malformed or mistyped files.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall accept master password input of unlimited length, including passphrases exceeding 100 characters, without truncation, character-count restriction, or alteration of the input value.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-18 and the success criteria table require that the master password field support passphrases of 100+ characters to enable strong passphrase adoption.

generated_requirements.text: When creating a new credential entry, the system shall require that if the password field is populated, the repeat-password field must also be populated and must contain a value identical to the password field; if the values do not match, the system shall display an explicit validation error and prevent the entry from being saved.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-15 and BR-16 mandate password confirmation on creation to prevent silent credential corruption from typographic errors.

generated_requirements.text: When modifying an existing credential entry and changing the password field, the system shall require that the repeat-password field also be updated to contain a value identical to the new password; if the values do not match, the system shall display an explicit validation error and prevent the modification from being saved.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-17 extends password confirmation validation to the modification path, ensuring data integrity is enforced consistently across both create and edit operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall organize all credential entries within a hierarchical structure of groups and subgroups; it shall be architecturally impossible to create or persist a credential entry that is not assigned to an existing group.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-09 mandates that every entry belongs to a group, making orphaned entries architecturally impossible and enforcing structural data integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall permit creation of a subgroup only when a parent group is actively selected in the user interface; attempting to create a subgroup without a selected parent shall result in the operation being blocked.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-10 requires that subgroup creation is gated on parent selection to maintain a valid and navigable group hierarchy.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall require a non-empty name when creating a new group or subgroup; the creation operation shall not complete and no group shall be persisted if the name field is empty or contains only whitespace.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-11 enforces non-empty name validation as a pre-condition for group creation to prevent unnamed, unnavigable groups from entering the hierarchy.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall require a non-empty name when renaming an existing group or subgroup; the rename operation shall not complete and the existing name shall be preserved if the new name field is empty or contains only whitespace.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-12 enforces non-empty name validation as a pre-condition for group rename to prevent groups from becoming unnamed after modification.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall require that a credential entry be explicitly selected before allowing the user to view or modify that entry; view and edit operations on unselected entries shall not be permitted.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-13 gates view and edit operations on explicit selection to prevent accidental modification of unintended entries.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall require that a credential entry be explicitly selected before allowing the user to duplicate that entry; the duplicate operation on an unselected entry shall not be permitted.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-14 gates the duplicate operation on explicit selection, consistent with the selection-first pattern applied to all entry-level operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall perform a full-text search across all credential fields — including title, username, password, URL, and notes — within the selected group or database, returning all entries where the query term appears as a full or partial match in any of those fields.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-20 requires comprehensive field coverage in search results, including partial matches, to ensure no relevant entry is omitted from results.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall reject a search query that contains no characters (empty search field) and shall not initiate a search operation or return any results in response to an empty query.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-19 mandates that at least one character must be present in the search field before a search is triggered, preventing meaningless full-database result dumps.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall return search results for any database size within five seconds of query submission.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 3 and the success criteria table specify a five-second upper bound on search response time regardless of database size.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recognize an auto-type sequence in a notes field only if the sequence definition line begins with the exact prefix string 'Auto-Type:'; any sequence definition line that does not begin with this exact prefix shall be ignored.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-21 mandates the exact 'Auto-Type:' prefix as the sole recognition token for auto-type sequences, making prefix validation a discrete, testable acceptance criterion.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce a maximum length of 59 characters for each auto-type sequence definition line; any sequence definition line containing 60 or more characters shall be treated as invalid and shall not be executed.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-22 and TC-03 establish 59 characters as a hard, non-configurable platform limit; sequences of exactly 59 characters are valid and sequences of 60+ are invalid.

generated_requirements.text: When a notes field contains more than one auto-type sequence definition line, the system shall execute only the first valid sequence and shall ignore all subsequent sequence definitions in that notes field.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-23 specifies first-sequence-only precedence to produce deterministic auto-type behavior when multiple definitions are present.

generated_requirements.text: Upon the first use of a TAN entry, the system shall immediately and irrevocably transition the TAN entry from the 'active' state to the 'expired' state; no subsequent operation, user action, or administrative function shall be capable of transitioning an expired TAN entry back to the 'active' state.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-25 defines the TAN lifecycle as a one-way active→expired state machine, aligning with PCI-DSS requirements for one-time authentication token invalidation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent any use of a TAN entry that is in the 'expired' state; an expired TAN entry shall not be presentable, copyable for authentication purposes, or otherwise usable as a credential.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-25 prohibits reuse of TAN entries under any circumstances; this requirement enforces the post-expiry access block as a distinct testable condition.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall make the title, username, and URL fields of a TAN entry immutable after the TAN entry is created; any attempt to edit these fields shall be blocked by the system.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-24 mandates immutability of TAN metadata fields to preserve the integrity and traceability of one-time authentication codes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall accept exactly one database file path as a command-line argument per invocation; if more than one database file path is supplied as a command-line argument, the system shall reject the invocation and display an explicit error message.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-26 and TC-04 enforce the single-file-per-invocation CLI contract, making multi-file invocation a testable error condition.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall correctly parse and open a database file whose path contains spaces when that path is enclosed in quotation marks in the command-line argument; paths containing spaces that are not enclosed in quotation marks shall result in a parsing error.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-27 defines the quoting rule for space-containing paths as a mandatory CLI syntax contract with a testable failure condition for unquoted paths.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent a print operation from being initiated if the database contains no credential entries; the print action shall be disabled or blocked, and the user shall be informed that at least one entry must exist before printing.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-28 and TC-10 prohibit printing of empty databases, making zero-entry state a testable pre-condition gate for the print operation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect when a user attempts to create or load a database using a name identical to an existing loaded or stored database name, and shall warn the user that proceeding will overwrite the existing database before allowing the operation to continue.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-07 identifies overwrite-on-duplicate-name as a data integrity risk; a pre-operation warning prevents silent data loss while preserving the defined behavior.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a mechanism within the application to direct users to download additional language translation packs via an external web browser; the application itself shall not perform the download, and the mechanism shall require an active internet connection and a compatible browser to be available.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-29 and TC-06 define language pack acquisition as an out-of-application, browser-based workflow that the application must surface but not execute internally.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall assign a fixed global hot key for application access that cannot be reconfigured, remapped, or disabled by the user through any application setting or preference.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-30 and TC-05 mandate a fixed, non-customizable hot key assignment; this must be documented so usability testing does not flag the absence of customization as a defect.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall store all credential data exclusively in the .kdb file format; the system shall not offer, support, or silently fall back to any alternative file format for database storage.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-01 and BR-06 mandate .kdb exclusivity to ensure portability and avoid vendor lock-in at the storage layer while maintaining a consistent compliance boundary.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall use general-purpose, low-level data layout specifications as the underlying file format structure for .kdb files, rather than a proprietary password-database-specific format, to ensure long-term portability and avoid vendor lock-in.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-08 and TC-02 require that the storage schema be based on general-purpose formats to preserve data accessibility independent of the KeePass application.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall not implement cloud synchronization, remote hosting, mobile interfaces, web-based interfaces, centralized multi-user database management, directory service integration (e.g., Active Directory, LDAP), automated backup, versioning, password recovery mechanisms, or browser plugin integration beyond directing users to download language packs.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD's Out of Scope section explicitly excludes these features; documenting them as exclusion requirements prevents scope creep during development and testing.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall ensure that the security of a composite key configuration is not weakened by the application itself; the application shall document that the overall security of the composite key model is contingent on the security of the external key file distribution channel, which is outside the application's scope.
generated_requirements.rationale: D-05 identifies key file distribution as an external dependency; the application must not create a false sense of security by implying it controls the distribution channel's integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display an explicit, user-readable error message for each of the following failure conditions: incorrect master password, missing or incorrect key file, incomplete composite key, mismatched repeat-password fields, empty group name on create or rename, empty search query, oversized auto-type sequence, invalid database file format, duplicate database name, and invalid CLI invocation syntax.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD defines strict rejection behaviors for numerous boundary conditions; explicit error messages are required to make each failure condition actionable and testable from a usability perspective.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain referential integrity of the group hierarchy at all times, ensuring that deletion of a group also removes or reassigns all entries and subgroups contained within it, and that no entry reference to a deleted group can persist in the database.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-09 makes group membership mandatory for all entries; cascading integrity on group deletion is required to prevent orphaned entry references that would violate this invariant.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall correctly process and execute auto-type sequences of exactly 59 characters in length, and shall reject and not execute any auto-type sequence of 60 or more characters, treating the 59-character boundary as a hard, non-configurable platform limit.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-22 and TC-03 define 59 characters as the exact boundary; both the valid upper bound (59) and the invalid lower bound of rejection (60) must be explicitly testable.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the creation, naming, viewing, editing, duplication, and deletion of credential entries within groups, and shall support the creation, naming, renaming, and deletion of groups and subgroups within the hierarchical database structure.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD's In Scope section enumerates the full set of CRUD operations required for both entries and groups as core functional capabilities.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support loading and opening an existing .kdb database file from the local file system, in addition to creating new .kdb database files, as distinct operations available to the user.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD's In Scope section includes both creation and loading of .kdb files as required database management operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow a database to be protected by a master password only, a key file only, or a composite of both master password and key file, and shall enforce the selected authentication mode consistently on every subsequent open attempt for that database.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 through BR-03 define three distinct authentication modes that must each be independently configurable and consistently enforced.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a multilingual user interface by supporting the installation of downloaded language translation packs, and shall apply the selected language pack to all user-facing interface elements upon activation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 6 and BR-29 require multilingual interface support to serve non-English-speaking users and reduce input errors from language barriers.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be installable on a compatible desktop operating system from the official KeePass distribution source, and shall be fully operational for all in-scope functions following a standard installation procedure without requiring additional configuration beyond initial database setup.
generated_requirements.rationale: D-01 and A-01 establish that successful installation on a validated desktop OS is the foundational dependency for all other system functionality.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support command-line invocation with a database file path argument, opening the specified .kdb file as part of the invocation, and shall enforce the single-file and path-quoting syntax rules as mandatory interface contracts with defined error responses for violations.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 5, BR-26, and BR-27 define CLI invocation as a required capability for power users and administrators integrating KeePass into automated workflows.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall comply with the PCI-DSS requirement for one-time authentication token invalidation by ensuring that TAN entries are irrevocably expired upon first use and that no mechanism within the system permits reuse of an expired TAN entry.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD's regulatory section explicitly aligns the TAN single-use expiry rule with PCI-DSS and similar standards prohibiting reuse of one-time authentication codes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce database naming uniqueness as a pre-condition for safe database management by detecting duplicate names at the point of creation or load, and shall require that a database naming convention policy be established and documented as an operational pre-condition before the system is deployed.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-07 and A-06 identify duplicate database names as a data integrity risk; naming convention governance is required to mitigate the overwrite risk at an operational level.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall not truncate, hash-limit, or otherwise alter a master password of any length during input, storage of the derived key, or authentication comparison, ensuring that the full entropy of long passphrases is preserved throughout the authentication process.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-18 and the success criteria require unlimited master password length; truncation at any stage would silently reduce security strength and violate the stated acceptance criterion.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall produce a print output containing all credential entries in the database when a print operation is initiated on a non-empty database, and shall support output to any printer or print-to-PDF driver configured and accessible on the user's machine.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-28 and D-06 define printing as an in-scope operation contingent on a non-empty database and an available print device, both of which must be reflected in the requirement.

sme_subject: credential management and enterprise security architect
sme_advisory: KeePass is a locally-hosted, zero-recovery credential vault whose security model is fundamentally different from enterprise IAM platforms — there is no centralized authority, no key escrow, and no administrative override. Requirements engineers must be careful to capture not only the functional "happy path" behaviors but also the precise boundary conditions that enforce security invariants: authentication gate strictness, TAN single-use lifecycle, auto-type length enforcement, composite key atomicity, and CLI syntax rules. Because the BRD explicitly calls out quality attributes of security, data integrity, usability/accessibility, interoperability, reliability, and compliance, the engineer should ensure each of those dimensions is represented in the requirements set — particularly around encryption-at-rest guarantees, password confirmation validation, group hierarchy integrity, and the zero-recovery policy as a deliberate design constraint rather than a defect.
