ground_truth_requirements.text: A governing model has to be defined in order to deploy the WP2 systems and tools and to maintain their states and sustainability over time. In this respect the composition, structure and scope of the governing model has to be formally defined to ensure fulfilment of functions like liability, sustainability and maintainability (e.g. especially to maintain and sustain a pre-VCD mapping tool in long term and therewith to ensure (re-)liable functions) The dedicated governing model should have the necessary status and power to govern its assigned functions. Requirement It MUST be clarified how the governance model of WP2 fits into the overall governance model of WP8. Measurement Governing model of WP2. A governing model has to be defined in order to deploy the WP2 systems and tools and to maintain their states and sustainability over time. In this respect the composition, structure and scope of the governing model has to be formally defined to ensure fulfilment of functions like liability, sustainability and maintainability (e.g. especially to maintain and sustain a pre-VCD mapping tool in long term and therewith to ensure (re-)liable functions) The dedicated governing model should have the necessary status and power to govern its assigned functions. Requirement It MUST be clarified how the governance model of WP2 fits into the overall governance model of WP8. Measurement Governing model of WP2.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Description The identity and access management of systems for company evidential documents is regulated by local procedures and could become part of the WP2 proposed systems (cf. stage 2 description). Economic operators have to apply for a VCD account in order to become authenticated. An officer has to verify whether the data that is necessary to apply for an account belongs to the economic operator and has to assure its correctness. An economic operator can authenticate to the VCD system only when the economic operator has sufficient user rights provided by identity and access management. Thereby WP2 has to take into account which natural person has the power to act as authorized representative of the economic operator. Requirement WP2 stakeholders MUST be authenticated to the VCD system and for the PEPPOL infrastructure. Measurement Formal authentication mechanism. Requirement The identity and access management procedures for creating a VCD account SHOULD be harmonized with the registration procedure of the overall infrastructure provided by WP 8. Measurement Single-Sign-On functionality. Requirement A single-sign-on SHOULD be implemented in order to allow identity and access management across different systems. Measurement Single-Sign-On functionality. ground_truth_requirements.text: Description Work package 2 will use the PEPPOL infrastructure for discovering addressing information which is typically stored in a registry. Therefore we need to find a method how addresses can be allocated and being provided to the WP 2 stakeholders so that they can use the PEPPOL infrastructure for exchanging VCDs and access related services. Requirement A method MUST be found how to allocate identifiers to the participants that use the infrastructure for transportation of VCDs and related services. Measurement Joined strategy and method with WP8 how to allocate identifiers among stakeholders and WP2 related services. ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Description “Member States are building electronic identity management systems as a key element in their national strategy to deploy electronic services. Those national based systems are evolving without taking into account the requirement for interoperability that is needed in a cross-border scenario, resulting in a fragmented service that prevents the deployment of pan-European electronic services. In the public procurement business process, as in other electronic business processes, electronic identity is a key element for two main purposes: Avoiding unauthorized use of individuals identity Assuring to administrations that individuals are the persons they claim to be and have the mandate they claim to have” (Bausa O. and Boujraf A. 2009) Requirement Every party participating MUST be uniquely identifiable Description “Member States are building electronic identity management systems as a key element in their national strategy to deploy electronic services. Those national based systems are evolving without taking into account the requirement for interoperability that is needed in a cross-border scenario, resulting in a fragmented service that prevents the deployment of pan-European electronic services. In the public procurement business process, as in other electronic business processes, electronic identity is a key element for two main purposes: Avoiding unauthorized use of individuals identity Assuring to administrations that individuals are the persons they claim to be and have the mandate they claim to have” (Bausa O. and Boujraf A. 2009) Requirement Every party participating MUST be uniquely identifiable
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Description “To exchange electronic documents between parties in a cross-border environment, a major challenge is to find out the other party’s address and to understand what are his systems’ capabilities and requirements. Different levels of maturity and implementation of electronic business interfaces on public administrations and economic operators across Europe obliges to create mechanisms for discovering the supported standards, documents and endpoints for trader partners.” (Bausa O. and Boujraf A. 2009) Requirement In stage 4 it MUST be possible to discover the verified electronic address for an identified party. Description “To exchange electronic documents between parties in a cross-border environment, a major challenge is to find out the other party’s address and to understand what are his systems’ capabilities and requirements. Different levels of maturity and implementation of electronic business interfaces on public administrations and economic operators across Europe obliges to create mechanisms for discovering the supported standards, documents and endpoints for trader partners.” (Bausa O. and Boujraf A. 2009) Requirement In stage 4 it MUST be possible to discover the verified electronic address for an identified party.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The PEPPOL infrastructure will make it possible to exchange electronic business documents in a secure and reliable manner. It connects existing frameworks and networks, but also allows small businesses to participate in the electronic business processes if they have Internet access and can be securely identified. The PEPPOL infrastructure is being built on existing technologies and standards that have been tested and which have proven to be reliable and secure to use. For WP2 it will important to access the infrastructure from different perspectives. An economic operator for example has to submit a VCD via the PEPPOL infrastructure. A contracting authority may require access to a database hosted by a national service provider to receive an attestation by using a direct reference to it. Some web services may be connected to the infrastructure to add additional functionalities to a VCD or for example to receive mapping information from the pre-VCD mapping tool. Therefore different access points to the PEPPOL infrastructure may be necessary in particular: The PEPPOL infrastructure will make it possible to exchange electronic business documents in a secure and reliable manner. It connects existing frameworks and networks, but also allows small businesses to participate in the electronic business processes if they have Internet access and can be securely identified. The PEPPOL infrastructure is being built on existing technologies and standards that have been tested and which have proven to be reliable and secure to use. For WP2 it will important to access the infrastructure from different perspectives. An economic operator for example has to submit a VCD via the PEPPOL infrastructure. A contracting authority may require access to a database hosted by a national service provider to receive an attestation by using a direct reference to it. Some web services may be connected to the infrastructure to add additional functionalities to a VCD or for example to receive mapping information from the pre-VCD mapping tool. Therefore different access points to the PEPPOL infrastructure may be necessary in particular: Requirement It MUST be clarified which are the best access points for economic operators to use the infrastructure for transportation of a VCD package. Measurement Definition of access point within PEPPOL infrastructure and definition of VCD transportation system. Requirement It MUST be clarified whether the possibility exist to have access to web clients via the infrastructure in order to have access to specific web services which depend upon functionalities provided by the infrastructure. Measurement Discussion with WP8. National connection point Direct access points for SMEs Web services and client-side code generation in SOAP frameworks
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Description The VCD is just one part of bid that an economic operator may submit to a contracting authority. Therefore the systems in each Member State have to be analysed with regard to the prospects of separating both parts from each other. This may include for example to submit a VCD via the PEPPOL infrastructure while submitting other parts or the bid itself via secondary channels or whether tendering platforms enable such separation. Requirement It MUST be clarified whether tendering platforms enable a separated submission of a VCD and the bid itself. Measurement Result of national system analysis. Requirement The PEPPOL infrastructure MUST have the capability to submit a VCD to a contracting authority or tendering platform. Measurement Functionality for submitting VCD to a contracting authority or tendering platform. Requirement The PEPPOL infrastructure SHOULD have the capability to submit a bid which contains a VCD. Measurement Discussion with WP8 about the prospects to have a functionality for submitting a bid to a contracting authority or tendering platform that includes a VCD. ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Description “Ensuring that information is accessible only to those authorized to have access and in a predefined date is mandatory by the European Directive in some public procurement processes. Specifically, contracting authorities must provide a way to receive electronic tenders and ensure those electronic tenders are not accessed until the prescribed time for opening them. Annex X of the Directive 2004/18/EC lists the requirements relating to devices for the electronic receipt of tenders, requests for participation and plans and projects in contests.” (Bausa O. and Boujraf A. 2009) Requirement It MUST be possible to guarantee confidentiality for specific documents. Description “Ensuring that information is accessible only to those authorized to have access and in a predefined date is mandatory by the European Directive in some public procurement processes. Specifically, contracting authorities must provide a way to receive electronic tenders and ensure those electronic tenders are not accessed until the prescribed time for opening them. Annex X of the Directive 2004/18/EC lists the requirements relating to devices for the electronic receipt of tenders, requests for participation and plans and projects in contests.” (Bausa O. and Boujraf A. 2009) Requirement It MUST be possible to guarantee confidentiality for specific documents.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Description WP1 assumes that all actors can sign inside their corporate infrastructure. The e-tendering platform must offer download functionality for forms and other material to be filled in, signed, and returned by an economic operator. A validation service may be a technical service or a Validation Authority (VA) that takes on liability and acts as a trust anchor of its own. Validation services may be chained in order to serve requests that cannot be handled by the “local” validation service. The VCD essentially relies on such validation service in order to guarantee data integrity and confidentially of a VCD (cf. PEPPOL WP1-D1.1). Requirement It MUST be possible to apply the validation service provided by WP1 to a VCD in order to enable economic operators to guarantee data integrity and confidentiality of a VCD. Measurement Testing of validation service with VCD within the PEPPOL infrastructure. Requirement It MUST be clarified whether the VCD can use the validation service of WP1 if the transportation infrastructure is not used by an economic operator. Measurement Discussion with WP1. ground_truth_requirements.text: Description “The purpose of an e-business transport infrastructure is to transport business documents between business partners. The transactions must be performed with high level of security and reliability. E.g. it must be ensured that business documents are actually delivered to the recipient party.” (Bausa O. and Boujraf A. 2009) Requirement Transport mechanisms between party’s addresses MUST be reliable. Description “The purpose of an e-business transport infrastructure is to transport business documents between business partners. The transactions must be performed with high level of security and reliability. E.g. it must be ensured that business documents are actually delivered to the recipient party.” (Bausa O. and Boujraf A. 2009) Requirement Transport mechanisms between party’s addresses MUST be reliable.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Description “The e-business transport infrastructure is to transport business documents between business partners. The transactions must be performed with high level of security. E.g. it must be ensured that third parties cannot intercept and read or modify the business documents.” (Bausa O. and Boujraf A. 2009). Requirement Transport mechanisms between party’s addresses MUST be secure. Description “The e-business transport infrastructure is to transport business documents between business partners. The transactions must be performed with high level of security. E.g. it must be ensured that third parties cannot intercept and read or modify the business documents.” (Bausa O. and Boujraf A. 2009). Requirement Transport mechanisms between party’s addresses MUST be secure.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Description “Data integrity ensures data has not been altered or modified during any operation such as transport or storage. Integrity can be compromised both by malicious intervention from a third party or by errors in the transport or storage mechanisms. Despite legal issues about the integrity of certain documents such as the electronic invoices, it should be a general rule to assure the integrity of all documents exchanged between partners.” (Bausa O. and Boujraf A. 2009). Requirement It MUST be assured that business document instances are consistent and correct during transportation, validation or transformation operations. Description “Data integrity ensures data has not been altered or modified during any operation such as transport or storage. Integrity can be compromised both by malicious intervention from a third party or by errors in the transport or storage mechanisms. Despite legal issues about the integrity of certain documents such as the electronic invoices, it should be a general rule to assure the integrity of all documents exchanged between partners.” (Bausa O. and Boujraf A. 2009). Requirement It MUST be assured that business document instances are consistent and correct during transportation, validation or transformation operations.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Description “In electronic procurement, some processes require to ensure that the sender of an electronic document cannot repudiate its validity“ (Bausa O. and Boujraf A. 2009). Requirement Authentication of origin for business document instances MUST be supported. Description “In electronic procurement, some processes require to ensure that the sender of an electronic document cannot repudiate its validity“ (Bausa O. and Boujraf A. 2009). Requirement Authentication of origin for business document instances MUST be supported.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Description “In electronic procurement it is fundamental to create a trust “environment” for the economic operator. A trust “environment” is needed in several phases of the eProcurement process (e.g. awarding a contract notice). It could be also a good deterrent against insider.” (Bausa O. and Boujraf A. 2009). Requirement The electronic procurement process MUST have an audit trailing to ensure transparency for both parties. Description “In electronic procurement it is fundamental to create a trust “environment” for the economic operator. A trust “environment” is needed in several phases of the eProcurement process (e.g. awarding a contract notice). It could be also a good deterrent against insider.” (Bausa O. and Boujraf A. 2009). Requirement The electronic procurement process MUST have an audit trailing to ensure transparency for both parties.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The first interim report – national country profiles – of the preliminary study of DG Markt comprises a list of qualification documents typically used in tendering procedures in the Member States. The report documents various attestations of the Member States which are arranged according to the Articles 45 to 50 of Directive 2004/18/EC (Siemens_and_Time.lex 2007): The first interim report – national country profiles – of the preliminary study of DG Markt comprises a list of qualification documents typically used in tendering procedures in the Member States. The report documents various attestations of the Member States which are arranged according to the Articles 45 to 50 of Directive 2004/18/EC (Siemens_and_Time.lex 2007): The study may follow up with a “proposal for a tool for a mutual information mechanism on the electronic provision of certificates and attestations usually required in public procurement procedures” which shall be aligned with our approach of a pre-VCD mapping tool. The above mentioned evidences thereby will be further described by templates that define the structural data of evidence for example the: Evidence of the Economic Operator’s identity (e.g. Extracts of commercial records or equivalent documents) Evidence of absence of conviction for natural persons (e.g. Extracts of judicial records or equivalent documents) Evidence of absence of conviction for legal persons (e.g. Extracts of judicial records or equivalent documents) Evidence of non-bankruptcy and financial status (e.g. Declarations from commercial courts or equivalent documents) Evidence of compliance with fiscal obligations (e.g. Declarations from fiscal services or equivalent documents) Evidence of compliance with social security obligations (e.g. Declarations from social security services or equivalent documents) Evidence of economic and financial standing (e.g. Bank statements, turnover sheets or equivalent documents) Evidence of professional qualification (e.g. Extracts from professional registers or equivalent documents) Evidence of technical and/or professional ability (e.g. Diplomas, descriptions of goods and services or equivalent documents) Evidence of adequate insurance (e.g. Insurance certificates or equivalent documents) Evidence of quality assurance (e.g. certification by auditing bodies of goods and services or equivalent documents) Evidence of environmental compliance (e.g. certification by auditing bodies of environmental practices or equivalent documents) Type of document Name of document Issuing entity Source reference Validity period Time of issuance Mandatory or optional Frequency of use Flexibility of submission Semantic characteristics Semantic characteristics Validation requirements
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The major objective of this deliverable is to develop a concept for a VCD and to define major requirements that shape this VCD concept. Therefore we have defined different scenarios and a vision that contains several different levels of maturity. With the staged maturity model we intend to reach a wide flexibility and openness of such a solution encompassing all Member States to implement their individual preferences. Nevertheless the concept provides common grounds such as the pre-VCD mapping and structural data of evidences which ensures interoperability of evidences and their perception across borders. Beside we defined a set of requirements on organisational, semantic, technical and legal level that clarify general aspect of VCD development and deployment. To support the mutual recognition of evidences across border the VCD requires an underlying semantic model that enables a standardized classification and mapping between them (pre-VCD mapping tool). If the contracting authority specifies the relevant evidences which are related to criteria, the data can be processed automatically in order to look-up the evidences required within another Member State. The standard structure of the VCD, with the semantic enrichment of its structure then provides a European-wide understanding and format of company formal evidence. The different levels of maturity can be applied in dependence on the willingness and readiness of every Member State. The aim is to support basic interoperability and exchange of documents on the lower maturity levels and leading towards direct electronic exchange of evidence in the higher maturity levels. Thereby each maturity level is built upon the lower ones thus allowing integration and stepwise advancement. The VCD shall ensure that a significant part of the public procurement processes can be made through electronic cross-border transactions. Therefore the national focus of a VCD needs to be enhanced through a cross border perspective. The VCD will provide the possibility to utilize evidences used in one Member State and to submit them across borders to any European contracting authority. A VCD provides a single point of contact for an economic operator that enables him to invoke and manage different services to create a VCD. A service (re-)compiles the VCD package for the economic operator or even a group of economic operators. The VCD services merge data from several sources into a single container and make this data available and understandable to the actors involved. Economic operators will be enabled to perform checks with regard to the existence and validity of the requested evidences in the VCD package. Structural data of evidence will provide guidance for foreign contracting authorities to understand the evidences contained within a VCD and they can trust that general information since it is provided by national VCD service providers that take the role of trusted parties. Context specific data will be provided on the higher levels to increase decision support. ground_truth_requirements.text: There are also some weaknesses that can be identified for the overall concept. These weaknesses are mostly derived from the complexity of the domain and how the VCD solution reacts on this domain. One of the biggest weaknesses is probably the development of context specific data for the evidences within a VCD. The development of context specific data shall ensure the automatic processing of electronic attestations without regard to linguistic barriers. The development of standards and context specific vocabularies is time consuming and requires long term involvement of user groups that will make use of these standards. The VCD contains several different kinds of evidences and to develop common standards for each one cannot be a major task and focus of this work package and should be done by initiative such as CEN BII, BRITE, XBRL or ECRIS. Since CEN BII workshop is close to an end it will probably not work out to do this development within its scope. Nevertheless we will start to develop comm of contents and marginal variances in their contents. Furthermore we rely on structural data of evidences that describe and characterize evidence from a more generic point of view. The possibility to replace the full document translation with a “selective translation” through context specific data (keywords and major contents) bares certain risks. The “selective translation” could be arranged in multiple languages in particular when context specific data belongs to a common agreed dictionary of expressions. Nevertheless the acceptance of those selective translations instead of certified translations may be questionable for contracting authorities. Thus the resolution of language barriers and linguistic issues cannot be completely managed by a VCD in short term and may require adding official translations beside selective translations. Provision of context specific data is a precondition to establish direct information exchange between attestation providers and contracting authorities. This direct information exchange would provide many opportunities with regard to increase the actuality of evidence, to lower the transaction costs of the parties involved and to reduce the time to create a VCD. Nevertheless such direct exchange is very difficult to establish because a lot of entities and attestation services have to be federated, access policies have to be developed, legal uncertainties have to be resolved and political guidelines have to be considered. To circumvent these problems the VCD provides in particular in stage 4 a guideline on how to move forward in order to reach this ambitious aim but it will not be a major focus of a VCD as such. The VCD will be dependent on a number of services that rely on manual and semi-automated input. Thus the VCD system will be primary a tool for the economic operator to arrange a VCD package instance which is conform according to the requirements stated in particular call for tender which he can fill through additional inputs. A direct access to attestation via the national VCD service provider can only be provided for those public attestations that are already available in electronic format. The VCD will not enforce any development of electronic attestation on a national level as this is a clear national duty and is depended upon the development of context specific data. Nevertheless existing attestations may be extended through additional annotations that describe these documents generically (structural data of evidences). Unclear yet is the role of Member States that have already implemented Article 5224. The scope of mutual recognition needs to be further clarified with regard to acceptance of these procedures across borders and the liability of those trusted third parties across borders. It needs to be further defined how a VCD can support countries that have implemented Article 52 but since there are currently no pilots in our work package that have such procedures already implemented we have left this out of scope. Neverton representations (context specific data) for evidences that have an, easy structure, low quantityheless we see a great opportunity to combine both ideas as shown in stage 4. generated_requirements.text: The System shall restrict access to the user creation workflow exclusively to users holding administrator privileges; general users must receive no access to any user creation function.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-UM-01 and HIPAA 45 CFR §164.308(a)(3) require that only authorized administrators can create user accounts, and this enforcement must be technically implemented.

generated_requirements.text: Before saving a new user account, the System shall query the corporate Active Directory to verify that the prospective user is currently active; if the user is inactive or not found in Active Directory, the System must block the save operation and display a descriptive error message to the administrator.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-UM-03 mandates real-time Active Directory status validation at account creation time to prevent provisioning accounts for terminated or inactive employees.

generated_requirements.text: The Active Directory integration shall retrieve only the minimum directory attributes necessary to confirm user active/inactive status (e.g., account enabled flag and unique identifier); no credential material, password hashes, or extraneous directory attributes may be retrieved or stored by the System.
generated_requirements.rationale: HIPAA minimum-necessary principle and NIST SP 800-63B require that AD queries be scoped to the least data needed, preventing inadvertent exposure of sensitive directory information.

generated_requirements.text: Before saving a new user account, the System shall search both the display name and username columns of the user table for an existing match; if a duplicate is found, the System must block the save operation and display a system-generated error message identifying the conflict.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-UM-02 and HIPAA 45 CFR §164.312(a)(1) require unique user identification; duplicate detection directly enforces this requirement.

generated_requirements.text: The System shall enforce that a new user account cannot be saved unless at least one role, one division, one designator code, and one lab location have been associated with the account; any attempt to save without all four associations must trigger a standard error or warning message identifying each missing association.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-UM-04 mandates enforcement of all required associations at save time to prevent misconfigured accounts from being persisted.

generated_requirements.text: The System shall enforce that the User Name and Display Name fields in the User Information section are populated before a new user account can be saved; both fields must be visually marked with a red asterisk to indicate they are mandatory.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-UM-05 and BR-UI-03 require mandatory field enforcement and visual marking for User Name and Display Name.

generated_requirements.text: The System shall provide a template-based user creation path whereby an administrator selects a pre-configured user template; upon selection, the new user record must automatically inherit all role settings defined in that template, and the administrator may add additional roles but may not remove template-inherited roles during the initial creation session.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-UM-06 defines the template role-inheritance rule and the restriction on removing inherited roles during creation.

generated_requirements.text: The System shall provide a post-creation role management capability allowing an authorized administrator to reduce or modify role assignments on an existing user account after initial creation, subject to audit logging, to prevent privilege lock-in from template inheritance.
generated_requirements.rationale: HIPAA 45 CFR §164.308(a)(4) minimum-necessary access principle requires that template-inherited roles can be recertified and reduced post-creation to avoid privilege accumulation.

generated_requirements.text: The System shall detect and warn the administrator when a proposed role assignment combination for a new or existing user constitutes a separation-of-duties conflict (e.g., result entry combined with result approval in a lab context); the System must display a warning message and require explicit administrator acknowledgment before proceeding.
generated_requirements.rationale: HIPAA 45 CFR §164.308(a)(4) and CLIA 42 CFR Part 493 require that role assignments in clinical lab workflows respect separation-of-duties controls to prevent unauthorized access combinations.

generated_requirements.text: When an administrator clicks Cancel during an active user creation session, the System must clear all data entered during that session, display a standard warning or caution message, and not persist any partial user record to the database.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-UM-07 and BR-UI-04 require that Cancel clears all session data and triggers a warning message, preventing partial records from being saved.

generated_requirements.text: When an administrator clicks Close without having entered any data in the user creation workflow, the System must redirect the user to the designated default page (to be formally named in the UIS — currently referenced as the 'xxx Page') without displaying a data-loss warning. [OPEN: 'xxx Page' identity must be resolved in the UIS before development begins.]
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-UM-08 specifies the Close-with-no-data behavior; the placeholder page name is flagged as an open item pending UIS finalization.

generated_requirements.text: The System shall display the designated default tab (to be formally named in the UIS — currently referenced as the 'xxx Tab') upon entering the user creation workflow; the administrator must be able to navigate to any other tab at any point during the creation process without losing data already entered on other tabs. [OPEN: 'xxx Tab' identity must be resolved in the UIS before development begins.]
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-UI-02 defines the default tab and free tab navigation; the placeholder tab name is flagged as an open item pending UIS finalization.

generated_requirements.text: The System shall defer all mandatory-field and required-association validation to the point of save action; navigating between tabs during user creation must not trigger per-tab validation errors, ensuring the administrator can complete data entry across multiple tabs before the save gate is enforced.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-UI-02 permits free tab navigation during creation, implying validation fires at save time rather than per-tab to avoid blocking multi-tab workflows.

generated_requirements.text: Upon successful save of a new user account, the System must display a standard confirmation message and persist all user account data — including User Name, Display Name, all associated roles, divisions, designator codes, and lab locations — to the SQL Server 2008 database.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-UM-09 and BR-UI-04 require successful-save confirmation and full persistence of all account associations to the database.

generated_requirements.text: The System shall provide an administrator capability to edit an existing user account's attributes, including display name, division assignments, designator codes, lab locations, and role assignments; all edits must be subject to the same mandatory-association enforcement and audit logging as account creation.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD future state describes a full user management workflow; the absence of an edit capability would leave administrators unable to correct or update account configurations post-creation.

generated_requirements.text: The System shall provide an administrator capability to deactivate an existing user account; deactivation must revoke the user's access to the System without deleting the account record, and the deactivation event must be written to the IAM audit log.
generated_requirements.rationale: HIPAA 45 CFR §164.308(a)(3) requires workforce access controls including the ability to revoke access for terminated or role-changed personnel.

generated_requirements.text: The System shall provide an administrator capability to reactivate a previously deactivated user account; reactivation must re-trigger Active Directory status validation before the account is restored to active status, and the reactivation event must be written to the IAM audit log.
generated_requirements.rationale: Standard IAM lifecycle management requires an account unlock/reactivation path; re-validating AD status at reactivation prevents restoring access for still-inactive employees.

generated_requirements.text: The System shall implement a mechanism to detect user accounts that have become inactive in Active Directory after initial creation (e.g., via a scheduled synchronization or on-login check) and must automatically suspend or flag such accounts to prevent orphaned active sessions for terminated employees.
generated_requirements.rationale: HIPAA 45 CFR §164.308(a)(3) requires ongoing workforce access supervision; AD validation only at creation time leaves a gap for accounts belonging to subsequently terminated employees.

generated_requirements.text: The System shall write an immutable IAM audit log entry for every user account creation, role assignment, template application, account edit, deactivation, and reactivation event; each entry must capture the acting administrator's identity, the target user's identity, the action type, a UTC timestamp, and the before-and-after state of any changed attributes.
generated_requirements.rationale: HIPAA 45 CFR §164.312(b) requires audit controls recording activity in systems containing ePHI; the IAM audit log must be separate from the operational error log and capture full event context.

generated_requirements.text: The IAM audit log must be stored in a tamper-evident format such that individual log entries cannot be modified or deleted without detection; the log must be stored separately from the operational error log and must not contain any PHI or PII beyond the minimum necessary to identify the actor and target (e.g., user IDs rather than patient identifiers).
generated_requirements.rationale: HIPAA 45 CFR §164.312(b) and the PHI non-exposure principle require tamper-evidence and minimum-necessary data in audit records.

generated_requirements.text: All system functions must be accessible via clearly labeled hyperlinks accompanied by descriptive text, structured in accordance with the User Interface Specification (UIS); no system function may be reachable only through unlabeled controls or institutional knowledge.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-UI-01 requires a UIS-aligned hyperlink-and-description navigation model across all system functions.

generated_requirements.text: All required fields across all data entry forms in the System must be visually marked with a red asterisk; no required field may be left unmarked, and this standard must be applied consistently across all pages including any new pages introduced by this project.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-UI-03 mandates universal red-asterisk marking of required fields with no exceptions.

generated_requirements.text: The System must generate a standard confirmation message upon every successful data save operation, a standard warning or caution message when the user clicks Cancel or Close during an active data entry session with unsaved data, and a standard error message for all validation failures; these message patterns must be applied consistently across all interactions system-wide.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-UI-04 requires standardized confirmation, warning, and error message patterns applied uniformly across the System.

generated_requirements.text: The System shall provide an online help pop-up window accessible from every page; the help window must open as a separate pop-up and must not replace, navigate away from, or obscure the operational page the user is currently working on.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-HLP-01 requires the help system to open in a non-disruptive pop-up window that preserves the user's current working context.

generated_requirements.text: The help pop-up window must open by default to the '[System Name] Overview' topic located in the Getting Started section, without requiring any additional user action beyond opening the help window.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-HLP-02 specifies the default help landing topic to ensure users immediately see a useful orientation page.

generated_requirements.text: The help system must implement context-sensitive behavior whereby the help topic displayed upon opening the help window corresponds to the page or workflow step the user is currently on; the System must maintain a mapping between each application page and its corresponding help topic to drive this context resolution.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD success criteria explicitly require context-sensitive help; a page-to-topic mapping mechanism is necessary to make this behavior testable and implementable.

generated_requirements.text: Help topic navigation within the help pop-up window must be provided via hyperlinks displayed in the left frame of the window; all help topics must be reachable through this left-frame navigation structure.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-HLP-03 specifies left-frame hyperlink navigation as the required navigation model within the help window.

generated_requirements.text: All help pages must display the company logo in the top-right corner and must use the organization's approved color scheme and background; no help page may deviate from these visual standards.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-HLP-04 mandates consistent visual branding across all help pages.

generated_requirements.text: The help system must include full-text search across all help topics, a topic index, and a glossary of industry-related and company-specific terms; all three capabilities must be accessible from within the help pop-up window.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-HLP-05 requires full-text search, index, and glossary as core help system capabilities.

generated_requirements.text: All errors, warnings, and informational events generated by the System must be written to a centralized external log file residing on the application server; no error, warning, or informational event may result in a silent failure (i.e., an event that is neither logged nor notified).
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-ERR-01 mandates comprehensive centralized logging with zero silent failures.

generated_requirements.text: When a user is directed to the System's error page, the System must simultaneously write an error entry to the external log file and send an automated notification email to the Client Services email distribution list; both actions must occur as part of the same error-handling event and neither may be omitted.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-ERR-02 requires that user-facing error page events trigger both log entry creation and automated Client Services notification.

generated_requirements.text: The automated error notification email sent to the Client Services distribution list must include: a unique error reference ID, the UTC timestamp of the error event, the affected user's system identifier (not name or PHI), the URL or page identifier where the error occurred, and a brief non-PHI technical description of the error; the email must not include any patient identifiers, PHI, or sensitive PII.
generated_requirements.rationale: Operational completeness requires defined email content; PHI non-exposure in notifications is mandated by HIPAA minimum-necessary principles.

generated_requirements.text: The System must define and implement a fallback behavior for SMTP delivery failure of the Client Services error notification email; at minimum, the failed delivery attempt must itself be logged to the external log file so that no error notification failure is silent.
generated_requirements.rationale: Operational reliability requires that email delivery failures are detected and logged to prevent gaps in incident notification.

generated_requirements.text: Any source code modified to implement a requirement under this project must be updated to write errors, warnings, and informational events to the external log file; existing source code that is not otherwise modified under this project is not required to be retrofitted.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-ERR-03 scopes the log file adoption requirement to modified code only, balancing completeness with regression risk.

generated_requirements.text: The Technical Lead must conduct and document a code review of every system change before that change may be committed to the source control system; no code change, regardless of size or perceived risk, may bypass this review gate.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-DEV-02 and OC-05 establish the code review gate as a hard process control applied to all changes.

generated_requirements.text: Every scheduled build must be labeled in the source control system before deployment; all builds deployed to any environment (development, staging, or production) must be produced exclusively from labeled checkouts, and no unlabeled build may be deployed.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-DEV-03 and TC-05 require build labeling in source control to ensure traceability between deployed artifacts and source code.

generated_requirements.text: The Development Team must perform weekly integrations and deploy updated builds to the Staging environment on a weekly cadence; release notes must be prepared and distributed via email to the full Project Team following every Staging deployment.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-DEV-04 and BR-DEV-05 establish the weekly Staging deployment cadence and mandatory release note distribution.

generated_requirements.text: No build may be deployed to the production environment without documented written signoff from the Technical Owner/Lead; this signoff requirement is a hard gate and may not be bypassed under any circumstances.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-DEV-06 and OC-02 establish Technical Owner/Lead signoff as a mandatory production deployment gate.

generated_requirements.text: All production deployments must be scheduled and executed within the approved maintenance window of Tuesdays between 7:00 PM and 7:00 AM; no production deployment may occur outside this window without explicit approval from the Technical Owner/Lead and relevant business stakeholders.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-DEV-07 and OC-01 restrict production deployments to the defined maintenance window to minimize operational disruption.

generated_requirements.text: The QA/QC Team must perform and document regression testing against every scheduled build; no build may proceed to production without completed regression testing, and the project schedule must allocate sufficient time for User Acceptance Testing (UAT) prior to each production release.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-DEV-08 and BR-DEV-09 mandate regression testing and UAT as hard gates before production deployment.

generated_requirements.text: Any source code modified under this project must be brought into conformance with the formally defined coding standards; existing source code that is not being modified under this project is not required to be refactored to meet current coding standards.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-DEV-10 scopes coding standard conformance to modified code only, consistent with TC-06.

generated_requirements.text: All existing HIPAA compliance capabilities of the System must be preserved without degradation; no change introduced by this project may remove, weaken, or bypass any existing compliance control, and all new functionality introduced by this project must be designed and implemented in conformance with the organization's HIPAA compliance requirements.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-HIPAA-01 and BR-HIPAA-02 require both preservation of existing compliance controls and extension of compliance to all new functionality.

generated_requirements.text: UI mockups for all new functionality must be demonstrated to project stakeholders early in the development cycle; reasonable UI adjustments requested by stakeholders must be accommodated within the project schedule before development of the affected screens is finalized.
generated_requirements.rationale: OC-04 requires early stakeholder UI review and accommodation of reasonable adjustments as a formal project process step.

generated_requirements.text: Administrator sessions performing user provisioning or account management operations must be subject to an elevated session timeout threshold and must prompt for re-authentication after a defined period of inactivity, distinct from the session policy applied to general users.
generated_requirements.rationale: HIPAA 45 CFR §164.308(a)(3) and NIST SP 800-63B require elevated session controls for privileged access operations to reduce the risk of unauthorized account changes.

generated_requirements.text: The Active Directory integration must not weaken existing authentication controls; specifically, the integration must not bypass Active Directory account lockout policies, multi-factor authentication configurations, or any other authentication enforcement mechanisms configured in the corporate AD.
generated_requirements.rationale: HIPAA 45 CFR §164.308(a)(5) and NIST SP 800-63B require that AD integration preserve existing authentication assurance levels.

generated_requirements.text: The external log file and the IAM audit log on the application server must be protected with access controls restricting read and write access to authorized system service accounts and administrators only; both log files must be encrypted at rest using an approved encryption standard, and access to the log file path must be auditable.
generated_requirements.rationale: HIPAA 45 CFR §164.312(b) and data-at-rest protection requirements mandate that log files containing access-control events be encrypted and access-controlled to prevent tampering or unauthorized disclosure.

generated_requirements.text: IAM audit log entries must be retained for a minimum of six (6) years from the date of creation, in accordance with HIPAA documentation retention requirements (45 CFR §164.316(b)(2)); the retention mechanism must ensure entries remain accessible and integrity-verifiable for the full retention period.
generated_requirements.rationale: HIPAA 45 CFR §164.316(b)(2) mandates a minimum six-year retention period for documentation of security activities, including audit logs.

generated_requirements.text: The external log file must be subject to a defined log rotation and archival policy that prevents disk exhaustion on the application server; the policy must specify maximum log file size, rotation frequency, archival destination, and retention period for rotated archives.
generated_requirements.rationale: Without a log rotation policy, unbounded log growth risks disk exhaustion on the application server, which would cause silent logging failures and violate BR-ERR-01.

generated_requirements.text: The Active Directory user status query performed during account creation must complete within three (3) seconds under normal network conditions; if the AD query does not return a result within this threshold, the System must display a timeout error message to the administrator and must not proceed with account creation.
generated_requirements.rationale: A defined AD query latency tolerance is necessary to ensure the validation step does not degrade administrator workflow and to define a testable timeout behavior.

generated_requirements.text: All System pages, including the help pop-up window, must load and render completely within five (5) seconds under the expected concurrent user load; the System must be load-tested against a defined concurrency baseline (to be specified by the Technical Lead in the performance test plan) prior to production release.
generated_requirements.rationale: BRD Objective 2 requires responsive navigation; explicit page load and help window performance thresholds with a defined concurrency baseline are necessary for testability.

sme_subject: HIPAA compliance officer with enterprise identity and access management (IAM) expertise
sme_advisory: This system sits squarely in HIPAA-regulated territory: it controls access to clinical laboratory workflows, manages user identities tied to PHI-handling roles, and integrates with Active Directory — all of which are high-sensitivity IAM operations under the HIPAA Security Rule (45 CFR §164.308–312). The requirements engineer must ensure that every user provisioning action, role assignment, and access control decision is fully auditable, that the AD integration does not inadvertently expose credential material or directory attributes beyond what is minimally necessary, and that the error logging and notification subsystem never surfaces PHI in log entries or email notifications. Special attention should be paid to the template-based provisioning model, which carries a well-known IAM risk of privilege accumulation (role bloat) if inherited roles are not periodically reviewed and recertified against the principle of least privilege.
